"<div>In <b>Benedict's test</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;fructose&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> gives a false positive since it can <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>tautomerize </i>to give an aldehyde</span> in its <i>linear</i> form - making it <i>appear</i> as a reducing sugar.</div>"	"<div>In <b>Benedict's test</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">fructose</u></span> gives a false positive since it can <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>tautomerize </i>to give an aldehyde</span> in its <i>linear</i> form - making it <i>appear</i> as a reducing sugar.</div>

<img src=""1200px-L-Histidine_physiological.svg.png""></div>"
"Amino acids are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;amphoteric&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, meaning they're both <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>basic</i></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>acidic</i></span>."	"Amino acids are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>amphoteric</b></span>, meaning they're both <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>basic</i></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>acidic</i></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Amphoteric</b> (a.k.a. <b>amphiprotic</b>): both basic &amp; acidic<div>- <b>Amphipathic</b>: both hydrophobic &amp; hydrophilic</div></div>"
"Amino acids are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>amphoteric</b></span>, meaning they're both <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;basic&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;acidic&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"Amino acids are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>amphoteric</b></span>, meaning they're both <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>basic</i></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>acidic</i></span>.

"<div>On an amino acid, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>pK<sub>a1</sub></b></span> refers to the pK<sub>a</sub> of its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;COOH&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> group.<br></div>"	"<div>On an amino acid, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>pK<sub>a1</sub></b></span> refers to the pK<sub>a</sub> of its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>COOH</u></span> group.<br></div>

<div>Vitamin D helps absorb Ca<sup>2+</sup> from the diet and deposits it in bone. This is why Rickets is caused by a lack of vitamin D.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ea08893b36a5d565b6bbf7a185e28a48.png""></div>"

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

    loaded();

    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'

var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

<div><i>Releases</i> energy</div><div><br></div><div>This is an <i>exception </i>to the rule that bond breakage is normally<i> endergonic</i>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""69e1b7b0eed2dd27681c66c62532d8af.png""></div>"

"<b style="""">DNA </b>sequences must <i>always </i>be written in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">5' → 3'</span> direction.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""b2bb4de4a83c8892be9e4e6bb88997ee.png""></div>"
"<u style="""">Purines</u> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> ring(s), whereas <u style="""">pyrimidines</u> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;1&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> ring(s)."	"<u style="""">Purines</u> have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">2</b></span> ring(s), whereas <u style="""">pyrimidines</u> have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">1</b></span> ring(s).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""a2423a1fd8b9b4fb9cd23c174a71507f.png""></div>"
"<div>Equation for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;H&#xFC;ckel&#x27;s&#x20;rule&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[rule]</span>:<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">4n + 2 = number of π-electrons</span></div>"	"<div>Equation for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hückel's rule</b></span>:<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">4n + 2 = number of π-electrons</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 'n' doesn't represent anything specific. If you plug the number of π electrons in and calculate n, it must come out to some whole number for the compound to be aromatic.</div><div>- Every π bond has 2 π-electrons.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-e3bb91d54833c60f2c1a803b1f47673b2dfdc1e0.jpg""><br></div><div></div></div>"
"<i>Why </i>are <b><i>nitrogenous bases</i></b> exceptionally stable?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""They&#x27;re&#x20;heterocycles&#x20;containing&#x20;nitrogen&#x2C;&#x20;which&#x20;&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;delocalizes&#x20;the&#x20;π&#x20;electron&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;similar&#x20;to&#x20;an&#x20;aromatic&#x20;ring&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<i>Why </i>are <b><i>nitrogenous bases</i></b> exceptionally stable?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">They're heterocycles containing nitrogen, which <u><i>delocalizes the π electron</i></u> similar to an aromatic ring.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""9e84bca3284a886991b5628d8eada532.png""></div>"
"<b style="""">DNA's </b>double helix is <i>stabilized</i> by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;hydrogen&#x20;bonding&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[interaction]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;planar&#x20;base&#x20;&#x28;π&#x29;&#x20;stacking&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[interaction]</span>."	"<b style="""">DNA's </b>double helix is <i>stabilized</i> by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>hydrogen bonding</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>planar base (π) stacking</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The hydrogen bonds between the DNA strands are between the functional groups of the nitrogenous bases.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ce8df75b8ab72c49e6589cd4040f136d.png""></div>"
"<span style=""font-weight: bold;"">DNA's</span>&nbsp;double helical strands run <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;anti&#x2D;parallel&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[direction]</span> to each other."	"<span style=""font-weight: bold;"">DNA's</span>&nbsp;double helical strands run <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">anti-parallel</u></span> to each other.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""67ccb08bac08e48abe28d4f794370538.png""></div>"
"<div>The <b>major </b>&amp; <b>minor grooves</b> in DNA allow <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;transcription&#x20;factors&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> to bind to regulate gene transcription.<br></div>"	"<div>The <b>major </b>&amp; <b>minor grooves</b> in DNA allow <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>transcription factors</u></span> to bind to regulate gene transcription.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>They bind to the <u>promoter regions</u> in the major &amp; minor grooves.</div><div><br></div><img src=""b8777d62d342bc3c93c0b1cbf93238ea.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;B&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b style="""">DNA </b>makes a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>right</i></span>-handed turn every <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">3.4</span> nm, which spans <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">10</span> bases." "<div><b>DNA polymerase</b> <i>travels along</i> the <u>parent strand</u> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""3&#x27;&#x20;→&#x20;5&#x27;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> direction but synthesizes its transcript in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""5&#x27;&#x20;→&#x20;3&#x27;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> direction.</div>"	"<div><b>DNA polymerase</b> <i>travels along</i> the <u>parent strand</u> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">3' → 5'</span> direction but synthesizes its transcript in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">5' → 3'</span> direction.</div>

</div>"
"In DNA replication, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;leading&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[leading/lagging]</span> <b>strand</b> in DNA replication <u style="""">synthesizes</u> in the <i>direction</i> of DNA helicase." "The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;leading&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[leading/lagging]</span> <b>strand's</b> <i>template</i> is in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">3' → 5'</span> direction &amp; its <i>complement</i> is in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">5' → 3'</span> direction."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>leading</b></span> <b>strand's</b> <i>template</i> is in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">3' → 5'</span> direction &amp; its <i>complement</i> is in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">5' → 3'</span> direction.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Its template being in the 3' → 5' direction allows for continual synthesis in the 5' → 3'.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-a56e962b9d866637d80897f421df9e2ab83405dd.jpg""><br></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>leading</b></span> <b>strand's</b> <i>template</i> is in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""3&#x27;&#x20;→&#x20;5&#x27;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> direction &amp; its <i>complement</i> is in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""5&#x27;&#x20;→&#x20;3&#x27;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> direction."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>leading</b></span> <b>strand's</b> <i>template</i> is in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">3' → 5'</span> direction &amp; its <i>complement</i> is in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">5' → 3'</span> direction.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"In DNA replication, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;lagging&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[leading/lagging]</span> <b>strand's</b> <u style="""">template</u> is in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">5' → 3'</span> direction."	"In DNA replication, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">lagging</b></span> <b>strand's</b> <u style="""">template</u> is in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">5' → 3'</span> direction.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;Primase&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> provides&nbsp;<u style="""">DNA polymerase</u> with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>free</i> 3' OH</span> by synthesizing primers in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">5'</span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">3'</span> direction." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">Primase</span></span> provides&nbsp;<u style="""">DNA polymerase</u> with a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>free</i> 3' OH</span> by synthesizing primers in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">5'</span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">3'</span> direction.

"After <b style="""">DNA polymerase</b> adds a base to the growing strand, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;pyrophosphate&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is <i>released</i>."	"After <b style="""">DNA polymerase</b> adds a base to the growing strand, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">pyrophosphate</u></span> is <i>released</i>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The release of pyrophosphate provides DNA polymerase with the energy needed for the formation of a new phosphodiester bond.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-01261d3d602af7b3bf03907f524caf9859c6373b.jpg""><br></div>"
"In <u>prokaryotes</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;DNA&#x20;polymerase&#x20;1&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>removes</i> RNA primers</span> from DNA."	"In <u>prokaryotes</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">DNA polymerase 1</span></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>removes</i> RNA primers</span> from DNA.
<hr>

"In <u>prokaryotes</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">DNA polymerase 1</span></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>removes</i> RNA primers</span> from DNA.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">DNA polymerase</span></span> <u>proofreads</u> the DNA&nbsp;on the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">newly synthesized</i></span> strand.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">DNA</b></span> libraries are generated by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">randomly fragmenting DNA</u></span> and cloning the fragments into a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">vector</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""d182898ac435aac4c743120212eb5613.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;cDNA&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[cDNA/DNA]</span> libraries are made by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">reverse-transcribing mRNA</u></span> back into its original DNA." <div>α's run away</div><div>β's stick around</div><div><br></div><img src=""76f1edf66133c7c073982e0a8d4d1781.png""></div>"
"<div><b>α</b> vs. <b>β</b> carbohydrate anomers&nbsp;(in terms of OH locations)...</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;α&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>-anomer: anomeric OH on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>opposite side</i></u></span> as CH<sub>2</sub>OH<br><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;β&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>-anomer:&nbsp;anomeric OH on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>same side</i></u></span> as CH<sub>2</sub>OH</div>"	"<div><b>α</b> vs. <b>β</b> carbohydrate anomers&nbsp;(in terms of OH locations)...</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b><i>α</i></b></span>-anomer: anomeric OH on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>opposite side</i></u></span> as CH<sub>2</sub>OH<br><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b><i>β</i></b></span>-anomer:&nbsp;anomeric OH on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>same side</i></u></span> as CH<sub>2</sub>OH</div>

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Mutarotation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> is when carbohydrates <i>spontaneously</i> change between <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">α<b style="""">&nbsp;</b>&amp;&nbsp;β anomers</span>." "<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;β&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b>-anomer</b> <i>predominates</i> in solution due to the fact that its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>OH is on an <i>equitorial</i> position</u></span> - giving it the greatest stability.<br></div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>β</b></span><b>-anomer</b> <i>predominates</i> in solution due to the fact that its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>OH is on an <i>equitorial</i> position</u></span> - giving it the greatest stability.<br></div>

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Glycosides</b></span> are carbohydrates with an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">acetal</span></span> group on its anomeric carbon.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Glycosides</b></span> are carbohydrates with an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;text&#x2D;decoration&#x2D;line&#x3A;&#x20;underline&#x3B;&quot;&gt;acetal&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[hemiacetal/acetal]</span> group on its anomeric carbon."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Glycosides</b></span> are carbohydrates with an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">acetal</span></span> group on its anomeric carbon.

"<div>An&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">α,α-1,1</b></span> linkage signifies a glycosidic bond between the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;α&#x2D;1&#x20;anomer&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of one sugar &amp; the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;α&#x2D;1&#x20;anomer&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of another.</div>"	"<div>An&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">α,α-1,1</b></span> linkage signifies a glycosidic bond between the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">α-1 anomer</u></span> of one sugar &amp; the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">α-1 anomer</u></span> of another.</div>

"Humans <u style=""""><i>cannot </i>digest</u> any sugars with&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;β&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b style="""">-linkages</b>, apart from lactose."	"Humans <u style=""""><i>cannot </i>digest</u> any sugars with&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">β</b></span><b style="""">-linkages</b>, apart from lactose.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>We can only digest lactose because we have lactase - the enzyme that digests the β-1,4 linkages specifically only in lactose.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""clip_image001 (2).png""><b><i><br></i></b></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;Cellulase&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> breaks down&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">β-1,4</span> linkages of <u style="""">glucose</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">Cellulase</span></span> breaks down&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">β-1,4</span> linkages of <u style="""">glucose</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Reducing end is shown <u>all the way to the right</u> (since it's not bonded to any more sugars).</div><div>- Produces maltose because it cleaves off the two carbohydrtaes on the end - producing a disaccharide of glucose residues (i.e. maltose).</div><div>- Used by <i>plants</i> and <i>bacteria</i></div><div><i><br></i></div><div><img src=""alpha-amylase.jpg""><i><br></i></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>β</b></span><b>-amylase</b>&nbsp;cleaves amylose at <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;text&#x2D;decoration&#x2D;line&#x3A;&#x20;underline&#x3B;&quot;&gt;the&#x20;reducing&#x20;end&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[location]</span> - yielding <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""maltose"" data-ordinal=""2"">[disaccharide]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>β</b></span><b>-amylase</b>&nbsp;cleaves amylose at <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">the reducing end</span></span> - yielding <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">maltose</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<b style="""">Steroids </b><i>always</i> end in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x2D;ol"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x2D;one"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<b style="""">Steroids </b><i>always</i> end in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">-ol</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">-one</span>.

<img src=""ce13829637a66b69cc5970787b9cf5c9.png""></div>"
"An amino acid's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">isoelectric&nbsp;</b><b>point</b></span> is the pH at which the protein has a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""net&#x20;&lt;i&gt;neutral&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;charge"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"An amino acid's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">isoelectric&nbsp;</b><b>point</b></span> is the pH at which the protein has a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">net <i>neutral</i> charge</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Only ionizable amino acids affect its isoelectric point.<div><br></div><div><img src=""fa7894e83b390be315de6a6adaba8e77.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6bdcb989c4a49d9e83b08c9644c069e6.png""><br></div></div>"
"An amino acid's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;isoelectric&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;b&gt;point&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> is the pH at which the protein has a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">net <i>neutral</i> charge</span>."	"An amino acid's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">isoelectric&nbsp;</b><b>point</b></span> is the pH at which the protein has a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">net <i>neutral</i> charge</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Only ionizable amino acids affect its isoelectric point.<div><br></div><div><img src=""fa7894e83b390be315de6a6adaba8e77.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6bdcb989c4a49d9e83b08c9644c069e6.png""><br></div></div>"
"In <span style=""font-weight: bold;"">isoelectric focusing</span>, the peptide stops moving when <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""pH"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> = <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""pI"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"In <span style=""font-weight: bold;"">isoelectric focusing</span>, the peptide stops moving when <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">pH</span> = <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">pI</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"The S/R designation of OH&nbsp;or H&nbsp;on <b style="""">Fischer projections</b> should <i>always</i> be <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;inverted&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>." Does <i>not</i>&nbsp;disrupt disulfide bonds</div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

You might confuse this with ""reducing SDS-PAGE,"" where the presence of a reducing agent is what reduces the disulfide bonds.</div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- α-amylase cleaves <i>randomly </i>(either amylose <i>or</i> amylopectin). β-amylase cleaves exactly at the reducing end.</div>"
"What's the <i>difference</i> between&nbsp;<u>α-amylase</u> &amp;&nbsp;<u>β-amylase</u>?<div><br></div><div><b>α-amylase</b>: cleaves <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""amylose&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;amylopectin"" data-ordinal=""1"">[amylose/amylopectin/both]</span>.<br></div><div><b>β-amylase</b>: cleaves <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;only&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;amylose"" data-ordinal=""1"">[amylose/amylopectin/both]</span>.</div>"	"What's the <i>difference</i> between&nbsp;<u>α-amylase</u> &amp;&nbsp;<u>β-amylase</u>?<div><br></div><div><b>α-amylase</b>: cleaves <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">amylose &amp; amylopectin</span>.<br></div><div><b>β-amylase</b>: cleaves <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>only</i> amylose</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<i><b>α</b>&nbsp;</i><i><b>α</b></i>mylose&nbsp;<i><b>α</b></i>mylopectin<div><i><b>β</b>ut only amylose</i><br></div></div>"
"What's the <u style="""">difference</u> between&nbsp;<b style="""">α-amylase</b> &amp;&nbsp;<b style="""">β-amylase</b>? (in terms of the thing they cleave)<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""β"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-amylase: cleaves <u><i>only</i> amylose</u>.<br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""α"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-amylase: cleaves <u style="""">amylose &amp; amylopectin</u>.</div>"	"What's the <u style="""">difference</u> between&nbsp;<b style="""">α-amylase</b> &amp;&nbsp;<b style="""">β-amylase</b>? (in terms of the thing they cleave)<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">β</span>-amylase: cleaves <u><i>only</i> amylose</u>.<br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">α</span>-amylase: cleaves <u style="""">amylose &amp; amylopectin</u>.</div>

</div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""ce8df75b8ab72c49e6589cd4040f136d.png""></div>"
"<b style="""">Serum albumin</b> is composed primarily of&nbsp;<u>hydro</u><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;phobic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> amino acids." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""Nn3mzLIFkCV3kZEyuxaPDw.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">RNA</b></span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>has an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""A"" data-ordinal=""1"">[A/B]</span>-form helix, while <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">DNA</b></span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>has a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""B"" data-ordinal=""1"">[A/B]</span>-form&nbsp;helix."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">RNA</b></span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>has an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">A</span>-form helix, while <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">DNA</b></span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>has a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">B</span>-form&nbsp;helix.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- A form helix: packed tightly<div>- B form helix: not coiled as tightly</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;RNA&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[DNA/RNA]</span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>has an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">A</span>-form helix, while <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;DNA&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[DNA/RNA]</span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>has a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">B</span>-form&nbsp;helix."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">RNA</b></span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>has an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">A</span>-form helix, while <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">DNA</b></span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>has a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">B</span>-form&nbsp;helix.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- A form helix: packed tightly<div>- B form helix: not coiled as tightly</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>RNA</b></span> has a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[higher/lower]</span> <u>melting point</u> than <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>DNA</b></span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>RNA</b></span> has a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>higher</i></span> <u>melting point</u> than <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>DNA</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Due to RNA's A-form helix (more compact = more Van der Waals)</div>"
"Why does <b style="""">RNA </b>have a higher melting point than DNA?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""RNA&#x20;forms&#x20;an&#x20;&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;A&#x2D;form&#x20;helix&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;which&#x20;is&#x20;much&#x20;more&#x20;compact&#x20;than&#x20;DNA&#x27;s&#x20;B&#x2D;form&#x20;helix&#x20;&#x28;more&#x20;compact&#x20;Van&#x20;der&#x20;Waal&#x20;forces&#x29;&#x2C;&#x20;and&#x20;it&#x20;also&#x20;has&#x20;an&#x20;additional&#x20;OH&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"Why does <b style="""">RNA </b>have a higher melting point than DNA?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">RNA forms an <b style="""">A-form helix</b>, which is much more compact than DNA's B-form helix (more compact Van der Waal forces), and it also has an additional OH.</span><br></div>

"In a&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;positive&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[+/-]</span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Tollen's</b></span> test, Ag(NH<sub style="""">3</sub>)<sub style="""">2</sub><sup style="""">+</sup>&nbsp;is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>reduced</i> to <u>Ag (silver)</u></span> to detect reducing sugars." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Detects reducing sugars, because Ag<sup>+</sup> will only be <i>reduced </i>to Ag <i>by </i>a reducing sugar.<div><br></div><div><img src=""d70a535d387ae47a52fee71c75d53a7f.png""><br></div></div>"
"In a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>negative</i></span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Benedict&#x27;s&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> test, a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">blue Cu<sup style="""">2+</sup> solution</span> forms."	"In a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>negative</i></span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Benedict's</b></span> test, a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">blue Cu<sup style="""">2+</sup> solution</span> forms.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Shows a <i><b>lack </b></i>of <b><i>reducing sugars</i></b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""d018a130dddec6bc03f82c529912b512.png""></div>"
"In a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>negative</i></span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Benedict's</b></span> test, a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""blue&#x20;Cu&lt;sup&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;2&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x20;solution"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> forms."	"In a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>negative</i></span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Benedict's</b></span> test, a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">blue Cu<sup style="""">2+</sup> solution</span> forms.
<hr>

These don't require insulin for glucose uptake.</div>"
"Glucose transported by the&nbsp;<b style="""">GLUT-</b><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[2/4]</span> transporter is primarily used for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">glycogenesis</u></span>."	"Glucose transported by the&nbsp;<b style="""">GLUT-</b><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">2</span></span> transporter is primarily used for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">glycogenesis</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>GLUT-2 receptors have low affinities for glucose. This means that GLUT-2-mediated glucose uptake happens mostly when glucose concentrations are very high in the blood (which is when it should be stored as glycogen anyway).</div><div><br></div><img src=""8024645_orig.png""></div>"
"Glucose transported by the&nbsp;<b style="""">GLUT-</b><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">2</span></span> transporter is primarily used for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;glycogenesis&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[pathway]</span>."	"Glucose transported by the&nbsp;<b style="""">GLUT-</b><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">2</span></span> transporter is primarily used for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">glycogenesis</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>GLUT-2 receptors have low affinities for glucose. This means that GLUT-2-mediated glucose uptake happens mostly when glucose concentrations are very high in the blood (which is when it should be stored as glycogen anyway).</div><div><br></div><img src=""8024645_orig.png""></div>"
"The liver releases <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>stored glycogen</u></span> when blood glucose levels are&nbsp;<i style="""">below&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;GLUT&#x2D;2&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x27;s&#x20;K&lt;sub&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;m&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The liver releases <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>stored glycogen</u></span> when blood glucose levels are&nbsp;<i style="""">below&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>GLUT-2</b>'s K<sub style="""">m</sub></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Helps make sure glucose is being released when blood-glucose levels are low.<div>- When blood-glucose exceeds GLUT 2's K<sub>m</sub>, it undergoes <u>glycogenesis</u> (there must be too much glucose in the blood).<br><div><i><br></i></div><div><img src=""glucagon-glycogen-glucose-hormone-pancreas-pancreas-releases-blood-levels-fall-too-low-causes-39693742.jpg""><i><br></i></div></div></div>"
"The liver releases <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;stored&#x20;glycogen&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> when blood glucose levels are&nbsp;<i style="""">below&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>GLUT-2</b>'s K<sub style="""">m</sub></span>."	"The liver releases <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>stored glycogen</u></span> when blood glucose levels are&nbsp;<i style="""">below&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>GLUT-2</b>'s K<sub style="""">m</sub></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Helps make sure glucose is being released when blood-glucose levels are low.<div>- When blood-glucose exceeds GLUT 2's K<sub>m</sub>, it undergoes <u>glycogenesis</u> (there must be too much glucose in the blood).<br><div><i><br></i></div><div><img src=""glucagon-glycogen-glucose-hormone-pancreas-pancreas-releases-blood-levels-fall-too-low-causes-39693742.jpg""><i><br></i></div></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;Glucokinase&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;GLUT&#x2D;2&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[transporter]</span> <i>monitor</i> blood glucose levels for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">insulin</b></span> release." "<b>Insulin</b> shuttles glucose into cells by making&nbsp;<i style="""">more&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><b style="""">GLUT-4</b> transporters</u></span>.

"<b style="""">GLUT-</b><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">4</b></span>&nbsp;has a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">normal</i></span> affinity for glucose.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

GLUT-2, by contrast, has a low affinity.</div>"
"What's the <i>significance </i>behind <b>GLUT-2</b> &amp; <b>GLUT-4</b>'s <u><i>affinity</i></u> for glucose?
<br>
[sound:hypertts-9b1c173bb99b97e2863023d34c2a5139c175cdc5d28549689174d211.mp3]"	"<b><i>GLUT-4</i></b> receptors have a <i>normal</i> affinity, which allows them to easily bring glucose into the cell after a meal to <u><i>generate ATP</i></u>.<div><br></div><div><b><i>GLUT-2</i></b> receptors have a <i>low </i>affinity, which means <u><i>excessive</i></u> glucose concentrations are required for these to shuttle glucose in. This is important because GLUT-2 receptors use glucose for <u><i>glycogenesis</i></u>. Glucose should only ever get stored away when it's in excessive concentrations.</div>

<br>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

GLUT-2, by contrast, has a low affinity.</div>"

"<b>Glucose </b>enters the cell through <i>either </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;active&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[active/passive/facilit.]</span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;facilitated&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[active/passive/facilit.]</span> transport."	"<b>Glucose </b>enters the cell through <i>either </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>active</u></span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>facilitated</u></span> transport.

"What role does <b style="""">PFK-2</b> play in <u style="""">glycogenesis</u> in the liver?

"Does fermentation in <i>humans</i> lead to <u>lactic acid</u> or <u>alcohol</u> production?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Lactic&#x20;acid"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Does fermentation in <i>humans</i> lead to <u>lactic acid</u> or <u>alcohol</u> production?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Lactic acid</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Accumulated lactic acid is carried into the liver (via the Cori cycle) for removal.</div><div><br></div><img src=""cb7628f7c5f145df306cbb16b71e78f1.png""></div>"
"<b style="""">DHAP</b>&nbsp;from glycolysis can be used by cells of the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""liver"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""fat"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">triglyceride synthesis</u></span>."	"<b style="""">DHAP</b>&nbsp;from glycolysis can be used by cells of the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">liver</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">fat</span> for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">triglyceride synthesis</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because DHAP can be <i>converted</i> into <u>G3P</u>&nbsp;(has a glycerol...backbone of triglycerides).</div><div><br></div><img src=""a8959324a4001e149a2af241259034d5.jpg""><br></div>"
"<b style="""">DHAP</b>&nbsp;from glycolysis can be used by cells of the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">liver</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">fat</span> for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;triglyceride&#x20;synthesis&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>."	"<b style="""">DHAP</b>&nbsp;from glycolysis can be used by cells of the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">liver</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">fat</span> for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">triglyceride synthesis</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because DHAP can be <i>converted</i> into <u>G3P</u>&nbsp;(has a glycerol...backbone of triglycerides).</div><div><br></div><img src=""a8959324a4001e149a2af241259034d5.jpg""><br></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Phosphoglycerate&#x20;kinase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;pyruvate&#x20;kinase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> are the <i>only</i> two glycolysis enzymes that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">produce</i> <u style="""">ATP</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Phosphoglycerate kinase</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">pyruvate kinase</b></span> are the <i>only</i> two glycolysis enzymes that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">produce</i> <u style="""">ATP</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These each produce ATP <u>twice</u>, since the 6-carbon glucose is cleaved into two 3-carbon molecules.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""cb7628f7c5f145df306cbb16b71e78f1.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Phosphoglycerate kinase</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">pyruvate kinase</b></span> are the <i>only</i> two glycolysis enzymes that <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;produce&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;ATP&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Phosphoglycerate kinase</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">pyruvate kinase</b></span> are the <i>only</i> two glycolysis enzymes that <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">produce</i> <u style="""">ATP</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These each produce ATP <u>twice</u>, since the 6-carbon glucose is cleaved into two 3-carbon molecules.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""cb7628f7c5f145df306cbb16b71e78f1.png""><br></div></div>"

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

  <div id=""io-original""><img src=""tmp_lympvou.png"" /></div>
</div>
<div id=""io-footer""></div>

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

"	"
<div id=""io-header""></div>
<div id=""io-wrapper"">
  <div id=""io-overlay""><img src=""00690fa2c8de4b559fb3f3b08186d574-oa-11-A.svg"" /></div>
  <div id=""io-original""><img src=""tmp_lympvou.png"" /></div>
</div>

<button id=""io-revl-btn"" onclick=""toggle();"">Toggle Masks</button>
<div id=""io-extra-wrapper"">
  <div id=""io-extra"">
    
    
    
    
  </div>
</div>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""cb7628f7c5f145df306cbb16b71e78f1.png""></div>"
"&nbsp;<b style="""">2,3-BPG</b> causes a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""right"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-shift on hemoglobin's oxygen-dissociation curve."	"&nbsp;<b style="""">2,3-BPG</b> causes a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">right</span>-shift on hemoglobin's oxygen-dissociation curve.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Left</b>-shift: O<sub>2</sub> affinity is <u>increased</u> (reaches saturation at lower partial pressures).</div><div><b>Right</b>-shift: O<sub>2</sub> affinity is <u>decreased</u> (takes a higher partial pressure to reach saturation).</div><div><br></div><img src=""497b452574410073b287b92842c22bed.png""></div>"
"An <i style="""">abnormally</i> high amount of <b style="""">2,3-BPG</b> hinders <u>hemoglobin's</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""O&lt;sub&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;saturation"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;in the lungs."	"An <i style="""">abnormally</i> high amount of <b style="""">2,3-BPG</b> hinders <u>hemoglobin's</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">O<sub style="""">2</sub> saturation</span>&nbsp;in the lungs.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><i>Abnormal</i> meaning <i>far beyond</i> what the normal increase in 2,3-BPG is.</div><div><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""497b452574410073b287b92842c22bed.png""></div>"
"<b style="""">2,3-BPG</b>&nbsp;can&nbsp;<i>only</i> bind to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""β"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-chain&nbsp;in hemoglobin."	"<b style="""">2,3-BPG</b>&nbsp;can&nbsp;<i>only</i> bind to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">β</span>-chain&nbsp;in hemoglobin.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Since 2,3-BPG can only bind to the β-chain, it can't bind to fetal hemoglobin (α &amp; γ chains).</div><div>- HbA</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""497b452574410073b287b92842c22bed.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Galactokinase</b></span> <i>catalyzes</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;galactose&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;→ <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;galactose&#x2D;1&#x2D;phosphate&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in galactose metabolism."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Galactokinase</b></span> <i>catalyzes</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">galactose</u></span>&nbsp;→ <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">galactose-1-phosphate</u></span> in galactose metabolism.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""1d01b9bac092dac68f6412a02446eb68.png""></div>"

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Galactokinase</b></span> <i>catalyzes</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">galactose</u></span>&nbsp;→ <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">galactose-1-phosphate</u></span> in galactose metabolism.

    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');

    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Aldolase B</b></span> cleaves <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""fructose&#x2D;1&#x2D;phosphate"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">DHAP</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">glyceraldehyde</u></span> in fructose metabolism." "<i>Why </i>do high-fructose drinks give us energy quicker?
<br>
[sound:hypertts-00feaa3d15d1e27ce15e311152adb298fc446b9071bcd6b6b9337b19.mp3]"	"<div>Fructose is metabolized into <b><i>DHAP </i></b>&amp; <b><i>glyceraldehyde</i></b>, which are both <u><i>further down the line in glycolysis</i></u> than if it had entered as regular glucose. Because of this, it <u><i>enters &amp; completes glycolysis quicker</i></u> than regular glucose.</div><div><br></div><img src=""a2a0f5de6ab5c68baa5587170093342f.png"">

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<div>UDP-glucose is then turned into glycogen</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""b46d8ef279fd93e1c4cc7429b04f2ef6.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d6a29cfad512c3c063474ec2d239ba0276e816e9.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In glycogenesis, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">glycogen synthase</b></span> is <i>stimulated</i> by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;text&#x2D;decoration&#x2D;line&#x3A;&#x20;underline&#x3B;&quot;&gt;glucose&#x2D;6&#x2D;phosphate&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[intermediate]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;text&#x2D;decoration&#x2D;line&#x3A;&#x20;underline&#x3B;&quot;&gt;insulin&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[hormone]</span>."	"In glycogenesis, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">glycogen synthase</b></span> is <i>stimulated</i> by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">glucose-6-phosphate</span></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">insulin</span></span>.

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

<img src=""6948a0dfb15b78af89625708c2518e32.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">PEPCK</b></span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>is <i>stimulated </i>by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;glucagon&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[hormone]</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;epinephrine&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[hormone]</span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;cortisol&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[hormone]</span> in gluconeogenesis."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">PEPCK</b></span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>is <i>stimulated </i>by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>glucagon</u></span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>epinephrine</u></span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>cortisol</u></span> in gluconeogenesis.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because PEPCK is needed to undergo gluconeogenesis (i.e. how these hormones produce responses)</div><div><br></div><div>- Epinephrine has no effect because it'd take too long</div><div><br></div><img src=""6948a0dfb15b78af89625708c2518e32.png""></div>"
"<i>Why </i>is <b><i>pyruvate carboxylase</i></b> important for gluconeogenesis?
<br>
[sound:hypertts-c5f60d69e4463d2cf7b687b4a4e4decc6cb7b61bb4e7aab6c6d828a8.mp3]"	"<div>It converts pyruvate into oxaloacetate. <u><i><b>Oxaloacetate </b>is then able to <b>turn back into PEP</b></i></u> - continuing gluconeogenesis. Pyruvate kinase in glycolysis is an irreversible step, so this step circumvents that problem.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6948a0dfb15b78af89625708c2518e32.png"">

<br>
[sound:hypertts-aac3e36d4eef76d85f07f7de6774eb2ce799c913313ce00684275461.mp3]
"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;PEPCK&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> is powered by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">GTP<i style="""">&nbsp;</i>hydrolysis</span> in gluconeogenesis."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>PEPCK</b></span> is powered by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">GTP<i style="""">&nbsp;</i>hydrolysis</span> in gluconeogenesis.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""6948a0dfb15b78af89625708c2518e32.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>PEPCK</b></span> is powered by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""GTP&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;hydrolysis"" data-ordinal=""2"">[reaction]</span> in gluconeogenesis."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>PEPCK</b></span> is powered by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">GTP<i style="""">&nbsp;</i>hydrolysis</span> in gluconeogenesis.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""6948a0dfb15b78af89625708c2518e32.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Fructose&#x2D;1&#x2C;6&#x2D;bisphosphatase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> is the <u style="""">rate-limiting</u> enzyme of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">gluconeogenesis</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase</b></span> is the <u style="""">rate-limiting</u> enzyme of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">gluconeogenesis</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""6948a0dfb15b78af89625708c2518e32.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>activated</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;text&#x2D;decoration&#x2D;line&#x3A;&#x20;underline&#x3B;&quot;&gt;ATP&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[carrier]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inhibited</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;AMP&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[carrier]</span> in gluconeogenesis."	"<b>Fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>activated</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">ATP</span></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inhibited</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>AMP</u></span> in gluconeogenesis.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><i>Too much</i> ATP signals that there's <i>enough </i>energy - it'd be more useful to <i>reverse </i>the process to replenish glucose supplies. AMP signals the opposite.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6948a0dfb15b78af89625708c2518e32.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;activated&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[activated/inhibited]</span><i>&nbsp;</i>by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">ATP</span></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;inhibited&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[activated/inhibited]</span><i>&nbsp;</i>by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>AMP</u></span> in gluconeogenesis."	"<b>Fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>activated</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">ATP</span></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inhibited</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>AMP</u></span> in gluconeogenesis.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><i>Too much</i> ATP signals that there's <i>enough </i>energy - it'd be more useful to <i>reverse </i>the process to replenish glucose supplies. AMP signals the opposite.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6948a0dfb15b78af89625708c2518e32.png""><br></div></div>"

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Glucose-6-phosphatase</b></span> (gluconeogenesis) is found <i>only</i> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">ER</span></span> in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">liver</span></span> cells.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...<i>not </i>found in skeletal muscle.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6948a0dfb15b78af89625708c2518e32.png""></div>"
"Why is glycogen <i>only</i> able to undergo <b style="""">gluconeogenesis </b>in the <u style="""">liver</u>?
<br>
[sound:hypertts-d96da19c254e2227e62c36bef4f981bd266111fec26074c781a43b3b.mp3]"	"<div>Skeletal muscle <i>lacks</i> <b style="""">glucose-6-phosphatase</b>, which is required for the last step in gluconeogenesis. "Why is <b style="""">pyruvate carboxylase</b> <i>only</i> activated by <u style="""">actetyl-CoA</u> from&nbsp;β-oxidation?
<br>
[sound:hypertts-5c78e7d0d62e594591b09f152610ba268f9e84d7832723aaec2cf37e.mp3]"	"<div>The acetyl-CoA produced from pyruvate (after glycolysis) would be ""burned"" for nothing otherwise, since it would immedietly undergo gluconeogenesis after just having finished glycolysis.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>β-oxidation instead indicates that the body is relying on fat reserves - signalling that it'd be a good time to make more glucose.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6948a0dfb15b78af89625708c2518e32.png""><br></div>

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Gluco</b></span><b>genic&nbsp;</b>amino acids are <i>first </i>converted into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">malate</span></span> to <i>later </i>convert into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>oxaloacetate</u></span> for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">gluconeogenesis</span>.

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';

}

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;PEPCK&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[enzyme]</span><b style="""">&nbsp;</b>is <i>stimulated </i>by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>glucagon</u></span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>epinephrine</u></span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>cortisol</u></span> in gluconeogenesis." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Made of long, saturated hydrocarbon chains.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2f63178f45405ae1fdc27a2d948e6a7ab3a8be32.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""A-Lipid-Raft-Membrane-Microdomain.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Flippase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;catalyzes the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>transverse</i> diffusion of <u>phospholipids</u></span> in the bilayer from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>outer</i></span> leaflet to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inner</i></span> leaflet - ATP <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>is</i></span> required.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Flippase</b></span>&nbsp;catalyzes the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>transverse</i> diffusion of <u>phospholipids</u></span> in the bilayer from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>outer</i></span> leaflet to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inner</i></span> leaflet - ATP <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>is</i></span> required.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Flippase</b>: outer → inner using ATP.</div><div>- <b>Floppase</b>: inner → outer using ATP.</div><div>- <b>Scramblase</b> → either direction flowing down its concentration gradient (no ATP).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-9dc2c8ef0fc5c138bc0882acdab15b78261ff365.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""07_10.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Flippase</b></span>&nbsp;catalyzes the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;transverse&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;diffusion&#x20;of&#x20;&lt;u&gt;phospholipids&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in the bilayer from the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;outer&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[inner/outer]</span> leaflet to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;inner&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[inner/outer]</span> leaflet - ATP <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;is&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[is/is not]</span> required.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Flippase</b></span>&nbsp;catalyzes the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>transverse</i> diffusion of <u>phospholipids</u></span> in the bilayer from the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>outer</i></span> leaflet to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inner</i></span> leaflet - ATP <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>is</i></span> required.<br></div>

"<div>What's the <i>difference </i>between the <b>Nernst equation</b> and the <b>Goldman-Hodgkin-Katz voltage</b> equation?</div><div><br></div><div><b><i>Nernst</i></b>: looking at the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;collective&#x20;conentrations&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of all ions</div><div><b><i>Goldman-Hodgkin-Katz</i></b>: looking at <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;specific&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;ion&#x20;contributions"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>What's the <i>difference </i>between the <b>Nernst equation</b> and the <b>Goldman-Hodgkin-Katz voltage</b> equation?</div><div><br></div><div><b><i>Nernst</i></b>: looking at the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>collective conentrations</u></i></span> of all ions</div><div><b><i>Goldman-Hodgkin-Katz</i></b>: looking at <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>specific</u> </i>ion contributions</span></div>

<div><br></div><div></div></div>"
"What's the <b>Goldman-Hodkin-Katz voltage</b> equation?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;6f38b0701150e759c8ecf1211a29b2e9&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"What's the <b>Goldman-Hodkin-Katz voltage</b> equation?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""6f38b0701150e759c8ecf1211a29b2e9.png""></span><br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>(Permeability x Concentration<sub>out&nbsp;</sub>+ ...) / (Permeability x Concentration<sub>in</sub>&nbsp;+ ...)</div><div></div></div>"
"<div>In the <b style="""">Goldman-Hodkin-Katz voltage</b> equation, only <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;text&#x2D;decoration&#x2D;line&#x3A;&#x20;underline&#x3B;&quot;&gt;chloride&#x20;&#x28;Cl&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;&lt;sup&gt;&#x2D;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;text&#x2D;decoration&#x2D;line&#x3A;&#x20;underline&#x3B;&quot;&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ion]</span> is <i>inverted</i> in its in/out position.</div>"	"<div>In the <b style="""">Goldman-Hodkin-Katz voltage</b> equation, only <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">chloride (Cl</span><sup>-</sup><span style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">)</span></span> is <i>inverted</i> in its in/out position.</div>

"There are <i><u>more</u></i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;K&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[K/Na]</span> leak channels than <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;Na&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[K/Na]</span>&nbsp;leak channels."	"There are <i><u>more</u></i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>K<sup>+</sup></i></b></span> leak channels than <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>Na<sup>+</sup></i></b></span>&nbsp;leak channels.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""350px-Membrane_potential_ions_en.svg.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;b&gt;outer&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[inner/outer]</span> <b>mitochondrial membrane</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> permeable to <u>ions</u>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><b>outer</b></i></span> <b>mitochondrial membrane</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> permeable to <u>ions</u>.

<div>Permeability of the outer membrane allows important ions and proteins to diffuse in.</div><div><br></div><img src=""1200px-Mitochondrion_mini.svg.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><b>outer</b></i></span> <b>mitochondrial membrane</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[more/less]</span> permeable to <u>ions</u>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><b>outer</b></i></span> <b>mitochondrial membrane</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> permeable to <u>ions</u>.

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

<div id=""io-footer""></div>

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

</div>"
"In isoelectric focusing, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">cathode</b></span> is at the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;basic&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[acidic/basic]</span>&nbsp;pH&nbsp;end."	"In isoelectric focusing, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">cathode</b></span> is at the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>basic</i></span>&nbsp;pH&nbsp;end.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Highly basic pHs tend to be more negatively charged (OH<sup>-</sup>), while acidic pHs tend to be more positively charged (H<sup>+</sup>).<br>- Isoelectric focusing is an electrolytic cell, which explains why the anode is positive &amp; the cathode is negative (opposite from batteries/galvanic cells).</div><div><br></div><img src=""OXHla5FU6anbfrid9jXxrY5-3jvDSeR161fFOWjQdHgUX1KrluUc33bwf_Xa3iwcnVVRSdd_mTEzEna7EOmD9x6zGr-DvuSfi0OOYgEoH4yC.jpg""></div>"
"In isoelectric focusing, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;cathode&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[anode/cathode]</span> is at the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>basic</i></span>&nbsp;pH&nbsp;end."	"In isoelectric focusing, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">cathode</b></span> is at the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>basic</i></span>&nbsp;pH&nbsp;end.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Isoelectric focusing is an electrolytic cell, which explains why the anode is positive &amp; the cathode is negative (opposite from batteries/galvanic cells).</div><div><br></div><img src=""OXHla5FU6anbfrid9jXxrY5-3jvDSeR161fFOWjQdHgUX1KrluUc33bwf_Xa3iwcnVVRSdd_mTEzEna7EOmD9x6zGr-DvuSfi0OOYgEoH4yC.jpg""></div>"
"In isoelectric focusing, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b><i>cathode</i></b></span> is at the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;negatively&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[positive/negative]</span>&nbsp;charged end."	"In isoelectric focusing, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b><i>cathode</i></b></span> is at the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>negatively</i></span>&nbsp;charged end.

<div>Dihydrolipoyl transacetylase &amp; Dihydrolipoyl dehydrogenase are <u>not</u>&nbsp;regulators - they're only functional enzymes apart of the complex.</div><div><br></div><img src=""82db19d9ab2975fcb2901c5c07ff2a04.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">PD&nbsp;kinase</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">PD&nbsp;phosphatase</b></span> are the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;regulators&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>pyruvate dehydrogenase complex</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">PD&nbsp;kinase</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">PD&nbsp;phosphatase</b></span> are the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>regulators</i></span> of the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>pyruvate dehydrogenase complex</u></span>.

<div>Dihydrolipoyl transacetylase &amp; Dihydrolipoyl dehydrogenase are <u>not</u>&nbsp;regulators - they're only functional enzymes apart of the complex.</div><div><br></div><img src=""82db19d9ab2975fcb2901c5c07ff2a04.png""></div>"
"In the pyruvate dehydrogenase complex, the <i>conversion</i> of <b style="""">pyruvate →</b>&nbsp;<b style="""">acetyl-CoA</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;exer&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><u style="""">gonic</u>." "<b style="""">Ketogenic </b>amino acids lose their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">NH<sub>3</sub></span> groups via <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">transaminations</u></span> <i>before</i> converting into acetyl-CoA.
