"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Keto</b></span><b>genic&nbsp;</b>amino acids can be <i>converted </i>into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">acetyl-CoA</span> for later conversion into <u>ketone bodies</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Acetyl-CoA can then be used for ketogenesis to make ketone bodies.</div>"
"When <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""β&#x2D;oxidation"" data-ordinal=""1"">[metabolic pathway]</span> is overly active, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">acetyl-CoA</b></span> <i>accumulates</i> &amp; leads to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">ketone body</u></span> formation."	"When <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">β-oxidation</span> is overly active, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">acetyl-CoA</b></span> <i>accumulates</i> &amp; leads to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">ketone body</u></span> formation.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Can't be from pyruvate dehydrogenase, since that would suggest plenty of glucose is coming into the body (which is&nbsp;<u>not</u> when you undergo ketogenesis).</div><div><br></div><img src=""ec586dcdb5253de680d1d2a395a70af5.png""></div>"
"When <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">β-oxidation</span> is overly active, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;acetyl&#x2D;CoA&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <i>accumulates</i> &amp; leads to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">ketone body</u></span> formation."	"When <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">β-oxidation</span> is overly active, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">acetyl-CoA</b></span> <i>accumulates</i> &amp; leads to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">ketone body</u></span> formation.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Can't be from pyruvate dehydrogenase, since that would suggest plenty of glucose is coming into the body (which is&nbsp;<u>not</u> when you undergo ketogenesis).</div><div><br></div><img src=""ec586dcdb5253de680d1d2a395a70af5.png""></div>"
"When <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">β-oxidation</span> is overly active, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">acetyl-CoA</b></span> <i>accumulates</i> &amp; leads to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;ketone&#x20;body&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> formation."	"When <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">β-oxidation</span> is overly active, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">acetyl-CoA</b></span> <i>accumulates</i> &amp; leads to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">ketone body</u></span> formation.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Can't be from pyruvate dehydrogenase, since that would suggest plenty of glucose is coming into the body (which is&nbsp;<u>not</u> when you undergo ketogenesis).</div><div><br></div><img src=""ec586dcdb5253de680d1d2a395a70af5.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Alcohol</b><b style="""">&nbsp;dehydrogenase</b></span>&nbsp;&amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">acetaldehyde&nbsp;</b><b>dehydrogenase</b></span>&nbsp;eventually convert <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ethanol"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> → <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""acetyl&#x2D;CoA"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Alcohol</b><b style="""">&nbsp;dehydrogenase</b></span>&nbsp;&amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">acetaldehyde&nbsp;</b><b>dehydrogenase</b></span>&nbsp;eventually convert <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">ethanol</span> → <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">acetyl-CoA</span>.

"<i>Why </i>does the <b><u>citric acid cycle</u></b> <i>not </i>occur in the <i>absence </i>of <b>oxygen</b>?<div><br><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Without&#x20;O&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;the&#x20;ETC&#x20;shuts&#x20;down&#x2E;&#x20;NADH&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;FADH&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;accumulate&#x2C;&#x20;and&#x20;accumulated&#x20;NADH&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;FADH&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;act&#x20;as&#x20;allosteric&#x20;inhibitors&#x20;in&#x20;the&#x20;TCA&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div></div>"	"<i>Why </i>does the <b><u>citric acid cycle</u></b> <i>not </i>occur in the <i>absence </i>of <b>oxygen</b>?<div><br><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Without O<sub>2</sub>, the ETC shuts down. NADH &amp; FADH<sub>2</sub> accumulate, and accumulated NADH &amp; FADH<sub>2</sub>&nbsp;act as allosteric inhibitors in the TCA.</span></div></div>

</div>"
"1 <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;NADH&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[carrier]</span> leads to the production of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">2.5</span> ATP in the electron transport chain." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>NADH</b>: pumps 10 H<sup>+</sup> protons to produce 2.5 ATP.<div>- <b>FADH<sub>2</sub></b>: pumps 6 H<sup>+</sup> protons to produce 1.5 ATP.</div></div>"
"1 <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">NADH</b></span> leads to the production of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2&#x2E;5"" data-ordinal=""2"">[#]</span> ATP in the electron transport chain."	"1 <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">NADH</b></span> leads to the production of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">2.5</span> ATP in the electron transport chain.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>NADH</b>: pumps 10 H<sup>+</sup> protons to produce 2.5 ATP.<div>- <b>FADH<sub>2</sub></b>: pumps 6 H<sup>+</sup> protons to produce 1.5 ATP.</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Isocitrate dehydrogenase</b></span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>α-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase</b></span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>malate dehydrogenase</b></span>&nbsp;produce <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""NADH"" data-ordinal=""1"">[carrier]</span> in the TCA." "<div>In <b>Benedict's test</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">fructose</u></span> gives a false positive since it can <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;tautomerize&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;to&#x20;give&#x20;an&#x20;aldehyde"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in its <i>linear</i> form - making it <i>appear</i> as a reducing sugar.</div>"	"<div>In <b>Benedict's test</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">fructose</u></span> gives a false positive since it can <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>tautomerize </i>to give an aldehyde</span> in its <i>linear</i> form - making it <i>appear</i> as a reducing sugar.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Reducing sugars are those with aldehydes, which can be oxidized further into carboxylic acids. Once the Benedict's reagent is added, the solution will turn&nbsp;<b>red</b>&nbsp;if there&nbsp;<i>are</i>&nbsp;reducing sugars (aldehyde oxidized to COOH)<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""472318df250f6907db8f48c71c824a24.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Isocitrate&#x20;dehydrogenase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;α&#x2D;ketoglutarate&#x20;dehydrogenase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span>&nbsp;are the <i>only </i>enzymes in the TCA that can be<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">allosterically<i>&nbsp;</i></u><i><u>activated</u></i></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Isocitrate dehydrogenase</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>α-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase</b></span>&nbsp;are the <i>only </i>enzymes in the TCA that can be<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">allosterically<i>&nbsp;</i></u><i><u>activated</u></i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The rest of the enzymes are capable of being allosterically inhibited, but these are the only 2 that can be <u>activated</u>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""bcb55efeffc6393df4bdd82a9ac24210.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Isocitrate dehydrogenase</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>α-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase</b></span>&nbsp;are the <i>only </i>enzymes in the TCA that can be<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;allosterically&lt;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;activated&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Isocitrate dehydrogenase</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>α-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase</b></span>&nbsp;are the <i>only </i>enzymes in the TCA that can be<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">allosterically<i>&nbsp;</i></u><i><u>activated</u></i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The rest of the enzymes are capable of being allosterically inhibited, but these are the only 2 that can be <u>activated</u>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""bcb55efeffc6393df4bdd82a9ac24210.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <b>citric acid cycle</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;requires&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[requires/does not require]</span> <u style="""">oxygen</u> to occur."	"The <b>citric acid cycle</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">requires</i></span> <u style="""">oxygen</u> to occur.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Without oxygen, NADH &amp; FADH<sub>2</sub> would accumulate from the ETC. NADH and FADH<sub>2</sub> would then allosterically inhibit the TCA.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ce8c0fb8a36ff0206cfdd6ddfd3b41ee637de15f.jpg""></div>"
"<b><u>Glycolysis</u></b> net reaction...<div><br></div><div>Glucose + <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NAD<sup>+</sup></b></span>&nbsp;+ <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>ADP</b></span> → <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>pyruvate</b></span> + <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NADH</b></span> + <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>ATP</b></span></div>"	"<b><u>Glycolysis</u></b> net reaction...<div><br></div><div>Glucose + <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NAD<sup>+</sup></b></span>&nbsp;+ <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>ADP</b></span> → <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>pyruvate</b></span> + <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NADH</b></span> + <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>ATP</b></span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<b><u>Glycolysis</u></b> net reaction...<div><br></div><div>Glucose + <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;NAD&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[carrier]</span>&nbsp;+ <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ADP&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[carrier]</span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;pyruvate&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> + <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;NADH&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[carrier]</span> + <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ATP&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[carrier]</span></div>"	"<b><u>Glycolysis</u></b> net reaction...<div><br></div><div>Glucose + <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NAD<sup>+</sup></b></span>&nbsp;+ <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>ADP</b></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>pyruvate</b></span> + <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NADH</b></span> + <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>2</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>ATP</b></span></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Cytochromes</b></span> are proteins with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">heme</u></span> groups that facilitate redox reactions by getting&nbsp;<i>reduced </i>to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Fe&lt;sup&gt;2&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <i>re-oxidized</i> to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Fe&lt;sup&gt;3&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in the ETC."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Cytochromes</b></span> are proteins with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">heme</u></span> groups that facilitate redox reactions by getting&nbsp;<i>reduced </i>to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Fe<sup>2+</sup></span> &amp; <i>re-oxidized</i> to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Fe<sup>3+</sup></span> in the ETC.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Cytochromes</b></span> are proteins with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">heme</u></span> groups that facilitate redox reactions by getting&nbsp;<i>reduced </i>to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Fe<sup>2+</sup></span> &amp; <i>re-oxidized</i> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Fe<sup>3+</sup></span> in the ETC.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Cytochromes</b></span> are proteins with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">heme</u></span> groups that facilitate redox reactions by getting&nbsp;<i>reduced </i>to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Fe<sup>2+</sup></span> &amp; <i>re-oxidized</i> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Fe<sup>3+</sup></span> in the ETC.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This is why cytochrome proteins are only capable of transferring <u>1 electron</u>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""7a6616fa22714a6cd84f71a575015c8f.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <b style="""">Q cycle</b> takes place in complex <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""3"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of the ETC."	"The <b style="""">Q cycle</b> takes place in complex <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">3</span> of the ETC.
<hr>

<div><img src=""7a6616fa22714a6cd84f71a575015c8f.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In <b>complex </b><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;3&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of the ETC, electrons are <i>extracted off</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ubiquinone</u></span> and placed <i>onto&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>cytochrome C</u></span>.</div>"	"<div>In <b>complex </b><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>3</b></span> of the ETC, electrons are <i>extracted off</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ubiquinone</u></span> and placed <i>onto&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>cytochrome C</u></span>.</div>

<div>Cytochrome a &amp; cytochrome a<sub>3</sub> are responsible for transferring electrons from cytochrome C onto O<sub>2</sub>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""97c167d0787e8f04fa0bd95cec50c481.png""><br></div></div>"
"The mitochondria's <b>intermembranous space</b>&nbsp;has an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;acidic&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[acidic/basic]</span> pH." "<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;glycerol&#x2D;3&#x2D;P&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>shuttle</b>&nbsp;uses cytosolic <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">NADH oxidation</span> to <i>power</i> the conversion of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>DHAP</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>glycerol-3-P</u></span> in the cytosol - eventually transferring its electrons into complex <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">2</span> of the ETC.</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>glycerol-3-P</b></span> <b>shuttle</b>&nbsp;uses cytosolic <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">NADH oxidation</span> to <i>power</i> the conversion of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>DHAP</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>glycerol-3-P</u></span> in the cytosol - eventually transferring its electrons into complex <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">2</span> of the ETC.</div>

<div>- Cytosolic DHAP → GA3P oxidation &amp; mitochondrial GA3P → DHAP oxidation is where the name ""GA3P shuttle"" comes from.</div><div>- This is in contrast with the malate aspartate shuttle, which instead moves electrons into complex 1.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-04bc26bdbaf07f277d2b178b49edc8b0abaaabe8.jpg""></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>glycerol-3-P</b></span> <b>shuttle</b>&nbsp;uses cytosolic <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""NADH&#x20;oxidation"" data-ordinal=""2"">[reaction]</span> to <i>power</i> the conversion of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>DHAP</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>glycerol-3-P</u></span> in the cytosol - eventually transferring its electrons into complex <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">2</span> of the ETC.</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>glycerol-3-P</b></span> <b>shuttle</b>&nbsp;uses cytosolic <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">NADH oxidation</span> to <i>power</i> the conversion of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>DHAP</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>glycerol-3-P</u></span> in the cytosol - eventually transferring its electrons into complex <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">2</span> of the ETC.</div>

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Pancreatic&#x20;lipase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> uses <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>colipase</u></span> as a cofactor in lipid digestion in the duodenum." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Bile&#x20;salts&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;short&#x2D;chain&#x20;fatty&#x20;acids&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>actively</i></span> transported from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ilium</u></span> of the small intestine into the bloodstream for reabsorption.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Bile salts</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>short-chain fatty acids</b></span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>actively</i></span> transported from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ilium</u></span> of the small intestine into the bloodstream for reabsorption.<br></div>

"After <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>citrate</u></span> <i>exits&nbsp;</i>the mitochondia, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b style="""">citrate lyase</b></span> splits it apart into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">oxaloacetate</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">acetyl-CoA</span>.

"Since acetyl-CoA is in the mitochondria, how does it enter the <i>cytoplasm</i> for <b>fatty acid synthesis</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;&lt;b&gt;Citrate&#x20;naturally&#x20;diffuses&#x20;out&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;mitochondria&#x20;&#x28;once&#x20;TCA&#x20;slows&#x29;&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;gets&#x20;&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;split&#x20;apart&#x20;into&#x20;acetyl&#x2D;CoA&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;oxaloacetate&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;&#x28;i&#x2E;e&#x2E;&#x20;what&#x20;citrate&#x20;is&#x20;made&#x20;of&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Since acetyl-CoA is in the mitochondria, how does it enter the <i>cytoplasm</i> for <b>fatty acid synthesis</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u><b>Citrate naturally diffuses out</b></u></i> of the mitochondria (once TCA slows) &amp; gets <b><i><u>split apart into acetyl-CoA &amp; oxaloacetate</u></i></b> (i.e. what citrate is made of)</span></div>

Acetyl-CoA carboxylase then can work with acetyl-CoA.</div><div>- Long-chain fatty acids inhibit this enzyme (since that signals the body already has enough).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2689ca144f6c84e4dd9d0292600024d2.png""><br></div></div>"

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Acetyl-CoA carboxylase</b></span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>activated</i></span> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>insulin</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>citrate</u></span> in fatty acid synthesis.

<div>Odd-numbered fatty acids go through the priopionoic acid pathway, which eventually generates succinyl-CoA. Succinyl-CoA can be converted into malate.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""0f6a59a25c3772e81884e4a0d803e707.png"">go throu<br></div></div>"
"Only <b><i>odd</i></b>-numbered fatty acids are able to be <b>glucogenic</b>. Why?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""The&#x20;&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;propionyl&#x2D;CoA&#x20;pathway&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;eventually&#x20;results&#x20;in&#x20;&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;succinyl&#x2D;CoA&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;which&#x20;can&#x20;get&#x20;&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;converted&#x20;into&#x20;malate&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x2E;&#x20;Malate&#x20;can&#x20;further&#x20;undergo&#x20;gluconeogenesis&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Only <b><i>odd</i></b>-numbered fatty acids are able to be <b>glucogenic</b>. Why?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">The <b><i><u>propionyl-CoA pathway</u></i></b> eventually results in <b><i><u>succinyl-CoA</u></i></b>, which can get <b><i><u>converted into malate</u></i></b>. <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Acetone is a spontaneous breakdown product of acetoacetate, but it's still considered a ketone body.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ec586dcdb5253de680d1d2a395a70af5.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;Acetoacetate&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;β&#x2D;hydroxybutyrate&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;acetone&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> are the <i>three&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ketone bodies</b></span> transported through the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">bloodstream</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Acetoacetate</u></span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>β-hydroxybutyrate</u></span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>acetone</u></span> are the <i>three&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ketone bodies</b></span> transported through the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">bloodstream</span>.

"The <i>only </i>way to increase V<sub>max</sub> is to <i>increase</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""enzyme&#x20;concentration&#x20;&#x28;&#x5B;E&#x5D;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>V<sub>max</sub> = [E]k<sub>cat</sub></b>.&nbsp;</div><div>- k<sub>cat</sub> is an <u>intrinsic property</u> that cannot be changed.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6f1f15a54db3a911850a31eb80f5a816.png""></div>"
"What's the <b>Michaelis-Menten</b> equation?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;96427da5d8d6f471eb7f67adb66c6c05&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"What's the <b>Michaelis-Menten</b> equation?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""96427da5d8d6f471eb7f67adb66c6c05.png""></span><br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""k&lt;sub&gt;1&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in enzyme kinetics represents <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">E + S ⇌&nbsp;ES</span>.<br>"	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">k<sub>1</sub></span> in enzyme kinetics represents <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">E + S ⇌&nbsp;ES</span>.<br>

"In enzyme kinetics, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>K<sub>m</sub></b></span> is a measure of an enzyme's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>substrate affinity</i></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""96427da5d8d6f471eb7f67adb66c6c05.png""></div>"
"Could you change an enzyme's <b>K<sub>m</sub></b> just by <i>increasing </i>or <i>decreasing </i>substrate concentrations?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;No&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;&#x2D;&#x20;K&lt;sub&gt;m&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;is&#x20;an&#x20;&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;intrinsic&#x20;property&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;of&#x20;an&#x20;enzyme&#x20;since&#x20;it&#x20;represents&#x20;the&#x20;substrate&#x20;concentration&#x20;needed&#x20;for&#x20;V&lt;sub&gt;max&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x2F;2&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Could you change an enzyme's <b>K<sub>m</sub></b> just by <i>increasing </i>or <i>decreasing </i>substrate concentrations?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i><u>No</u></i></b> - K<sub>m</sub> is an <b><i><u>intrinsic property</u></i></b> of an enzyme since it represents the substrate concentration needed for V<sub>max</sub>/2.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Competitive inhibitors increase Km since they <i>artificially </i>make substrate-binding harder.</div><div><br></div><img src=""96427da5d8d6f471eb7f67adb66c6c05.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""V&lt;sub&gt;max&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;= <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">k<sub>cat</sub>[E]</span></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">V<sub>max</sub></span>&nbsp;= <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">k<sub>cat</sub>[E]</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>k<sub>cat</sub></b> <u>does not</u> change with changes in [E].</div><div><br></div><img src=""96427da5d8d6f471eb7f67adb66c6c05.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;k&lt;sub&gt;cat&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is called the enzyme's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">catalytic turnover</u></span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">k<sub>cat</sub></b></span> is called the enzyme's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">catalytic turnover</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>K<sub>cat</sub> = catalytic <i>turnover</i></div><div>K<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub> = catalytic <i>efficiency</i></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;k&lt;sub&gt;cat&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x2F;K&lt;sub&gt;m&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is called either the enzyme's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>catalytic efficiency</u></span> or its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>specificity constant</u></span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub></b></span> is called either the enzyme's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>catalytic efficiency</u></span> or its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>specificity constant</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Use this to <i>compare</i> enzymes<div><br></div><div>K<sub>cat</sub> = catalytic <i>turnover</i></div><div>K<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub> = catalytic <i>efficiency</i></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub></b></span> is called either the enzyme's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;catalytic&#x20;efficiency&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> or its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;specificity&#x20;constant&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub></b></span> is called either the enzyme's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>catalytic efficiency</u></span> or its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>specificity constant</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Use this to <i>compare</i> enzymes<div><br></div><div>K<sub>cat</sub> = catalytic <i>turnover</i></div><div>K<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub> = catalytic <i>efficiency</i></div></div>"
"To <i>compare</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>efficiency</u></span> between two enzymes, compare <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;k&lt;sub&gt;cat&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x2F;K&lt;sub&gt;m&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"To <i>compare</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>efficiency</u></span> between two enzymes, compare <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub></b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"To <i>compare</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;efficiency&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> between two enzymes, compare <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub></b></span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"When <b>mixed</b><b>&nbsp;inhibitors</b> bind to the enzyme's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;ES&#x20;complex&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, K<sub>m</sub> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span>."	"When <b>mixed</b><b>&nbsp;inhibitors</b> bind to the enzyme's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>ES complex</u></span>, K<sub>m</sub> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

...<i>increases </i>affinity, since the inhibitor essentially traps the substrate in place.<div>- <b>Mixed inhibitors</b> bind to either the ES complex or an allosteric site with different affinities.</div><div>- <b>Non-competitive</b> inhibitors bind to either the ES complex or an allosteric site with equal affinities.</div></div>"
"When <b>mixed</b><b>&nbsp;inhibitors</b> bind to the enzyme's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>ES complex</u></span>, K<sub>m</sub> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decreases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[increases/decreases]</span>."	"When <b>mixed</b><b>&nbsp;inhibitors</b> bind to the enzyme's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>ES complex</u></span>, K<sub>m</sub> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

...<i>increases </i>affinity, since the inhibitor essentially traps the substrate in place.<div>- <b>Mixed inhibitors</b> bind to either the ES complex or an allosteric site with different affinities.</div><div>- <b>Non-competitive</b> inhibitors bind to either the ES complex or an allosteric site with equal affinities.</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Mixed</b></span> inhibition...<div><br></div><div>V<sub>max</sub>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""decreased"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div>K<sub>m</sub>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""increased&#x20;or&#x20;decreased"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Mixed</b></span> inhibition...<div><br></div><div>V<sub>max</sub>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">decreased</span></div><div>K<sub>m</sub>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">increased or decreased</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Mixed&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> inhibition...<div><br></div><div>V<sub>max</sub>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">decreased</span></div><div>K<sub>m</sub>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">increased or decreased</span></div>"	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Mixed</b></span> inhibition...<div><br></div><div>V<sub>max</sub>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">decreased</span></div><div>K<sub>m</sub>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">increased or decreased</span></div>

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Uncompetitive&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> inhibitors bind <i>only </i>to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ES complex</u></span> - <i>locking</i> the substrate in place." "<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;Water&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-<u>soluble</u> <b>vitamins </b>can be cleared from the body <i>quicker</i> than <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;fat&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-<u>soluble</u> <b>vitamins</b>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>Water</u></span>-<u>soluble</u> <b>vitamins </b>can be cleared from the body <i>quicker</i> than <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>fat</u></span>-<u>soluble</u> <b>vitamins</b>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Water is removed through the urine, while fat-soluble vitamins accumulate in adipose tissue.</div>- Fat-soluble vitamins (D, A, K, E) can reach <u>toxic levels</u> easier than water-soluble vitamins.<div><br></div><div><img src=""2aa131c9b20853d1b55eb7e1aab35edb.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Retinoic acid</b> is <i>more </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;oxidized&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[oxidized/reduced]</span> than retinol (vitamin A)."	"<b>Retinoic acid</b> is <i>more </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>oxidized</i></u></span> than retinol (vitamin A).

<div>Substrate becomes reduced by using NADPH → NADP<sup>+</sup> oxidation.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1-s2.0-S1367593117300686-sc1.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Reductase</b></span> enzymes <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;reduce&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[oxidize/reduce]</span> their <u>substrates</u> by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;oxidizing&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[oxidize/reduce]</span>&nbsp;<u>electron carriers</u>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Reductase</b></span> enzymes <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">reduce</i></span> their <u>substrates</u> by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">oxidizing</i></span>&nbsp;<u>electron carriers</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Substrate becomes reduced by using NADPH → NADP<sup>+</sup> oxidation.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1-s2.0-S1367593117300686-sc1.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In the <u>Citric Acid cycle</u>,<i> each</i> of the enzymes that produce <b>electron carriers</b> (NADH or FADH<sub>2</sub>) are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""dehydrogenase"" data-ordinal=""1"">[class]</span> enzymes."	"In the <u>Citric Acid cycle</u>,<i> each</i> of the enzymes that produce <b>electron carriers</b> (NADH or FADH<sub>2</sub>) are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">dehydrogenase</span> enzymes.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Pyruvate <u>oxidized</u> to actetyl-CoA; NAD<sup>+</sup> <u>reduced</u> to NADH.</div><div><br></div><img src=""c2355eac536032e4b4d8d0690085b3e7.png""></div>"

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>HMG-CoA lyase</b></span> converts <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>HMG-CoA</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>acetoacetate</u></span> in ketogenesis.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""ec586dcdb5253de680d1d2a395a70af5.png""></div>"
"Some <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>acetoacetate</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>is <i>converted into</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>β-hydroxybutyrate</u></span>&nbsp;using <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""NADH&#x20;oxidation"" data-ordinal=""1"">[reaction]</span>&nbsp;in ketogenesis."	"Some <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>acetoacetate</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>is <i>converted into</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>β-hydroxybutyrate</u></span>&nbsp;using <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">NADH oxidation</span>&nbsp;in ketogenesis.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Acetoacetate gets <u>reduced</u> to β-hydroxybutyrate.</div><div>- Both <b><u>acetoacetate</u></b> &amp; <b><u>β-hydroxybutyrate</u></b> are used as ketone bodies in the bloodstream, but small amounts of acetoacetate are needed to generate β-hydroxybutyrate in the first place.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ec586dcdb5253de680d1d2a395a70af5.png""></div>"
"Some <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;acetoacetate&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><b>&nbsp;</b>is <i>converted into</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>β-hydroxybutyrate</u></span>&nbsp;using <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">NADH oxidation</span>&nbsp;in ketogenesis."	"Some <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>acetoacetate</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>is <i>converted into</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>β-hydroxybutyrate</u></span>&nbsp;using <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">NADH oxidation</span>&nbsp;in ketogenesis.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Acetoacetate gets <u>reduced</u> to β-hydroxybutyrate.</div><div>- Both <b><u>acetoacetate</u></b> &amp; <b><u>β-hydroxybutyrate</u></b> are used as ketone bodies in the bloodstream, but small amounts of acetoacetate are needed to generate β-hydroxybutyrate in the first place.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ec586dcdb5253de680d1d2a395a70af5.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">Dihydrolipoyl dehydrogenase</span></span> <i>re-oxidizes</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">lipoic acid</span> on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">dihydrolipoyl transacetylase</u></span> using <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""FAD&#x20;reduction"" data-ordinal=""4"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">Dihydrolipoyl dehydrogenase</span></span> <i>re-oxidizes</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">lipoic acid</span> on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">dihydrolipoyl transacetylase</u></span> using <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4"">FAD reduction</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The resulting FADH<sub>2</sub> gets <b>re-oxidized</b>&nbsp;into FAD with NAD<sup style="""">+</sup> reduction.</div><div><br></div><img src=""b6bdc6c1ddd8edef6dc8102c9d282e7a.png""></div>"
"Some <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>acetoacetate</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>is <i>converted into</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;β&#x2D;hydroxybutyrate&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>&nbsp;using <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">NADH oxidation</span>&nbsp;in ketogenesis."	"Some <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>acetoacetate</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>is <i>converted into</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>β-hydroxybutyrate</u></span>&nbsp;using <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">NADH oxidation</span>&nbsp;in ketogenesis.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Acetoacetate gets <u>reduced</u> to β-hydroxybutyrate.</div><div>- Both <b><u>acetoacetate</u></b> &amp; <b><u>β-hydroxybutyrate</u></b> are used as ketone bodies in the bloodstream, but small amounts of acetoacetate are needed to generate β-hydroxybutyrate in the first place.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ec586dcdb5253de680d1d2a395a70af5.png""></div>"
"The chemical structures of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;detergents&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[soaps/detergents]</span><b>&nbsp;</b>are long carbon chains with heads of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">sulfate</span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">phosphate salts</span>."	"The chemical structures of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>detergents</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>are long carbon chains with heads of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">sulfate</span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">phosphate salts</span>.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Non&#x2D;competitive&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>&nbsp;inhibitors work by <i>changing </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>enzyme's conformation</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>Non-competitive</b></span>&nbsp;inhibitors work by <i>changing </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>enzyme's conformation</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""daee36e088493759b2638837d42db351.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Anaerobic&#x20;fermentation&#x20;&#x28;ethanol&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[pathway]</span> <i>from pyruvate</i>...<br></div><div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Pyruvate decarboxylase</span></div><div><div>2. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Alcohol dehydrogenase</span></div></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Anaerobic fermentation (ethanol)</span> <i>from pyruvate</i>...<br></div><div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Pyruvate decarboxylase</span></div><div><div>2. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Alcohol dehydrogenase</span></div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Converts pyruvate → ethanol &amp; CO<sub>2</sub></div><div><br></div><img src=""4e3f5ec2a5524e59802e6b25e412058e.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Aldolase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> <i>catalyzes </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">F-1,6-BP</span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">GA3P</span> + <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">DHAP</span> in glycolysis."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Aldolase</b></span> <i>catalyzes </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">F-1,6-BP</span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">GA3P</span> + <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">DHAP</span> in glycolysis.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- This is a <u>reversible</u> reaction.</div><div><br></div><img src=""c69fe88beb66288759eca87f24855de5.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Aldolase</b></span> <i>catalyzes </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""F&#x2D;1&#x2C;6&#x2D;BP"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""GA3P"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> + <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""DHAP"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in glycolysis."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Aldolase</b></span> <i>catalyzes </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">F-1,6-BP</span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">GA3P</span> + <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">DHAP</span> in glycolysis.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- This is a <u>reversible</u> reaction.</div><div><br></div><img src=""c69fe88beb66288759eca87f24855de5.png""></div>"
"The enzymes that <i>dephosphorylate </i>ATP into <b>ADP </b>in <u>glycolysis</u>...<div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Hexokinase&#x2F;glucokinase"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""PFK&#x2D;1"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"The enzymes that <i>dephosphorylate </i>ATP into <b>ADP </b>in <u>glycolysis</u>...<div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Hexokinase/glucokinase</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">PFK-1</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Glycolysis produces a total of 4 ATP (2 rounds of phosphoglycerate kinase &amp; pyruvate kinase), but only has a <u>net</u> of 2 (2 ATP consumed) due to these two enzymes.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c69fe88beb66288759eca87f24855de5.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Glycolysis </b>produces <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""4"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> total ATP but <i>only </i>has a net yield of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> ATP due to ATP <i>dephosphorylation </i>from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>hexokinase</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>PFK-1</u></span>."	"<b>Glycolysis </b>produces <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">4</span> total ATP but <i>only </i>has a net yield of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">2</span> ATP due to ATP <i>dephosphorylation </i>from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>hexokinase</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>PFK-1</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The 4 ATP produced come from <b>phosphoglycerate kinase</b> &amp; <b>pyruvate kinase</b>. 1 glucose molecule undergoes 2 ""rounds"" after being split via aldolase - hence doubling the ATP production to 4.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c69fe88beb66288759eca87f24855de5.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Glycolysis </b>produces <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">4</span> total ATP but <i>only </i>has a net yield of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2</span> ATP due to ATP <i>dephosphorylation </i>from <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;hexokinase&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[enzyme]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;PFK&#x2D;1&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[enzyme]</span>."	"<b>Glycolysis </b>produces <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">4</span> total ATP but <i>only </i>has a net yield of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2</span> ATP due to ATP <i>dephosphorylation </i>from <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>hexokinase</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>PFK-1</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The 4 ATP produced come from <b>phosphoglycerate kinase</b> &amp; <b>pyruvate kinase</b>. 1 glucose molecule undergoes 2 ""rounds"" after being split via aldolase - hence doubling the ATP production to 4.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c69fe88beb66288759eca87f24855de5.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>1 glucose molecule undergoes <u>2 rounds</u> of glycolysis <i>after </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;aldolase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> <i>splits</i> the 6-carbon molecule into two 3-carbon molecules (GA3P &amp; DHAP).<br></div>"	"<div>1 glucose molecule undergoes <u>2 rounds</u> of glycolysis <i>after </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>aldolase</b></span> <i>splits</i> the 6-carbon molecule into two 3-carbon molecules (GA3P &amp; DHAP).<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Aldolase splitting the 6-carbon glucose apart is what causes it to undergo 2 rounds of glycolysis.</div><div><br></div><div>- <b>Hexokinase/glucokinase</b>: dephosphorylates ATP → ADP (lose 1 ATP).<br></div><div>- <b>PFK-1</b>: dephosphorylates ATP → ADP (lose 1 ATP).</div><div><br></div><div>After <u>aldolase</u> splits it into two 3-carbon molecules...&nbsp;</div><div>- <b>Phosphoglycerate kinase</b>: phosphorylates ADP → ATP (makes 2 ATP)</div><div>- <b>Pyruvate kinase</b>: phosphorylates ADP → ATP (makes 2 ATP)</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c69fe88beb66288759eca87f24855de5.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <i>only </i>glycolytic enzymes <u>allosterically regulated</u> are the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;irreversible&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><i>&nbsp;</i>steps (hexokinase, PFK-1, pyruvate kinase)."	"The <i>only </i>glycolytic enzymes <u>allosterically regulated</u> are the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>irreversible</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>steps (hexokinase, PFK-1, pyruvate kinase).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Hexokinase</b>: allosterically regulated by G-6-P</div><div>- <b>PFK-1</b>: allosterically regulated by citrate, ATP/AMP, and F-2,6-BP</div><div>- <b>Pyruvate kinase</b>: allosterically regulated by F-1,6-BP.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c69fe88beb66288759eca87f24855de5.png""><br></div></div>"
"What's the name of this <b>citric acid cycle</b> <u>intermediate</u>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Citrate"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8eb7ec85f9dc76d9b52fb51513351814.png""><br></div>"	"What's the name of this <b>citric acid cycle</b> <u>intermediate</u>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Citrate</span></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8eb7ec85f9dc76d9b52fb51513351814.png""><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-ce8c0fb8a36ff0206cfdd6ddfd3b41ee637de15f.jpg""></div>"

<img src=""paste-ce8c0fb8a36ff0206cfdd6ddfd3b41ee637de15f.jpg""></div>"
"<div><b>Hormone-sensitive lipase (HSL)</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>stimulated</i></span><i> </i>by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;glucagon&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[hormone]</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;epinephrine&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[hormone]</span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;cortisol&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[hormone]</span> - mobilizing fatty acids for metabolism.</div>"	"<div><b>Hormone-sensitive lipase (HSL)</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>stimulated</i></span><i> </i>by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>glucagon</u></span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>epinephrine</u></span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>cortisol</u></span> - mobilizing fatty acids for metabolism.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...releases triglycerides for fatty acid metabolism.</div><div>- Glucagon doesn't mobilize fatty acids, it only stimulates gluconeogenesis &amp; glocogenolysis.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-f3be9b8553de996e3b5ad3861ad936fdfbc3e43b.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""024c862ce081997ba257267ecf56f7ee.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Hormone-sensitive lipase (HSL)</b>&nbsp;is&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;inhibited&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[stimulated/inhibited]</span><i>&nbsp;</i>by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>insulin</u></span> - <i>slowing down</i> fatty acid metabolism."	"<b>Hormone-sensitive lipase (HSL)</b>&nbsp;is&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>inhibited</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>insulin</u></span> - <i>slowing down</i> fatty acid metabolism.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- When insulin is high, it <u>doesn't</u> want to start mobilizing fatty acids (helps store them as triglycerides instead).</div><div>- Insulin stimulates <b>acetyl-CoA carboxylase</b> in fatty acid synthesis... insulin is more concerned with fatty acid <u>storage</u>.</div><div>- HSL in adipocytes hydrolyzes triglycerides into fatty acids for fatty acid metabolism.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-f3be9b8553de996e3b5ad3861ad936fdfbc3e43b.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""024c862ce081997ba257267ecf56f7ee.png""></div>"
"<b>Hormone-sensitive lipase (HSL)</b>&nbsp;is&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>inhibited</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;insulin&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[hormone]</span> - <i>slowing down</i> fatty acid metabolism."	"<b>Hormone-sensitive lipase (HSL)</b>&nbsp;is&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>inhibited</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>insulin</u></span> - <i>slowing down</i> fatty acid metabolism.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- When insulin is high, it <u>doesn't</u> want to start mobilizing fatty acids (helps store them as triglycerides instead).</div><div>- Insulin stimulates <b>acetyl-CoA carboxylase</b> in fatty acid synthesis... insulin is more concerned with fatty acid <u>storage</u>.</div><div>- HSL in adipocytes hydrolyzes triglycerides into fatty acids for fatty acid metabolism.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-f3be9b8553de996e3b5ad3861ad936fdfbc3e43b.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""024c862ce081997ba257267ecf56f7ee.png""></div>"
"<div>After <b>β-oxidation</b>, the released NADH &amp; FADH<sub>2</sub> enter the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""electron&#x20;transport&#x20;chain"" data-ordinal=""1"">[pathway]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>After <b>β-oxidation</b>, the released NADH &amp; FADH<sub>2</sub> enter the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">electron transport chain</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>β-oxidation releases acetyl-CoA, NADH, &amp; FADH<sub>2</sub>.</div><div>- The released <b>acetyl-CoA</b>&nbsp;enters either the TCA or can stimulate gluconeogenesis (liver, via pyruvate carboxylase).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-27cf85628fecbd444b4d7e1d8cf6ba93d47fe052.jpg""><br><div></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Acetone&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ketone body]</span> is a <i>spontaneous </i><u>breakdown product</u> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>acetoacetate</b></span> in the blood, which sometimes is excreted through sweating or exhaling."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Acetone</b></span> is a <i>spontaneous </i><u>breakdown product</u> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>acetoacetate</b></span> in the blood, which sometimes is excreted through sweating or exhaling.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Acetoacetate gets&nbsp;<u>reduced</u>&nbsp;to β-hydroxybutyrate.</div><div>- Both&nbsp;<b><u>acetoacetate</u></b>&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;<b><u>β-hydroxybutyrate</u></b>&nbsp;are used as ketone bodies in the bloodstream, but small amounts of acetoacetate are needed to generate β-hydroxybutyrate in the first place.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ec586dcdb5253de680d1d2a395a70af5.png""><div></div><div></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Acetone</b></span> is a <i>spontaneous </i><u>breakdown product</u> of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;acetoacetate&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[ketone body]</span> in the blood, which sometimes is excreted through sweating or exhaling."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Acetone</b></span> is a <i>spontaneous </i><u>breakdown product</u> of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>acetoacetate</b></span> in the blood, which sometimes is excreted through sweating or exhaling.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Acetoacetate gets&nbsp;<u>reduced</u>&nbsp;to β-hydroxybutyrate.</div><div>- Both&nbsp;<b><u>acetoacetate</u></b>&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;<b><u>β-hydroxybutyrate</u></b>&nbsp;are used as ketone bodies in the bloodstream, but small amounts of acetoacetate are needed to generate β-hydroxybutyrate in the first place.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ec586dcdb5253de680d1d2a395a70af5.png""><div></div><div></div></div>"
"<b>Glycogen phosphorylase</b>&nbsp;<i>only</i>&nbsp;cleaves at the <u>non-reducing ends</u> of glycogen that are <i>at least </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""4"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""monosaccharides"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <i>away </i>from a branch point."	"<b>Glycogen phosphorylase</b>&nbsp;<i>only</i>&nbsp;cleaves at the <u>non-reducing ends</u> of glycogen that are <i>at least </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">4</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">monosaccharides</span> <i>away </i>from a branch point.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- If the non-reducing end is only 2 monosaccharides away from a branch point, it will not cleave there.</div><div>- If the non-reducing end is more than 4 monosaccharides away from a branch point, it'll cleave the end.</div><div><br></div><img src=""Glycogen.jpg""></div>"

"In amino acid metabolism, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>aminotransferase</b></span> <i>transaminates&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">NH<sub>3</sub> groups</span> <i>off </i>from amino acids <i>onto&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>α-ketoglutarate</u></span> to generate <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>glutamate</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Because it'd be below the pK<sub>a</sub> of the COOH &amp; NH<sub>3</sub> group - both would be protonated.</div>"

- This is the pH value for which the amino acid is exactly neutral.<div>- Only ionizable amino acids affect its isoelectric point.<div><br></div><div><img src=""fa7894e83b390be315de6a6adaba8e77.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6bdcb989c4a49d9e83b08c9644c069e6.png""></div></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Native&#x20;PAGE&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[technique]</span> separates <u>proteins</u> by their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">mass-to-charge</span> ratios, along with differences in their size &amp; shape."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Native PAGE</b></span> separates <u>proteins</u> by their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">mass-to-charge</span> ratios, along with differences in their size &amp; shape.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Proteins are left in their <b>non-denatured</b> states and they carry different charges (unlike SDS-PAGE).</div>- Proteins with a <u>large</u> mass-to-charge ratio will <u>migrate further</u> in the gel, since they'll carry a higher overall negative charge (stronger pull to the other side).</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Native PAGE</b></span> separates <u>proteins</u> by their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""mass&#x2D;to&#x2D;charge"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> ratios, along with differences in their size &amp; shape."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Native PAGE</b></span> separates <u>proteins</u> by their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">mass-to-charge</span> ratios, along with differences in their size &amp; shape.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Proteins are left in their <b>non-denatured</b> states and they carry different charges (unlike SDS-PAGE).</div>- Proteins with a <u>large</u> mass-to-charge ratio will <u>migrate further</u> in the gel, since they'll carry a higher overall negative charge (stronger pull to the other side).</div>"
"<div>In<b> Native PAGE</b>, proteins with <i>greater </i><u>charge densities</u> will migrate <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;faster&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[slower/faster]</span>.</div>"	"<div>In<b> Native PAGE</b>, proteins with <i>greater </i><u>charge densities</u> will migrate <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>faster</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Proteins are left in their <b>non-denatured</b> states and they carry different charges (unlike SDS-PAGE).</div>- Charge density refers to their mass-to-charge ratios. Higher net negative charge = it'll migrate further.</div>"
"<div>In<b> Native PAGE</b>, proteins with <i>smaller&nbsp;</i><u>charge densities</u> will migrate <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;slower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[slower/faster]</span>.</div>"	"<div>In<b> Native PAGE</b>, proteins with <i>smaller&nbsp;</i><u>charge densities</u> will migrate <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>slower</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Proteins are left in their <b>non-denatured</b> states and they carry different charges (unlike SDS-PAGE).</div>- Charge density refers to their mass-to-charge ratios. Higher net negative charge = it'll migrate further.</div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>3' ← 5' tRNA</div><div>5 → 3' mRNA</div><div><br></div><img src=""6892cdf3b65ca5787122c19ccbd8fee6.png""><div></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Methionine&#x20;&#x28;Met&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[amino acid]</span> is the <i>first</i> amino acid <u>translated</u> in every protein."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Methionine (Met)</span> is the <i>first</i> amino acid <u>translated</u> in every protein.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>AUG</b></div>"
"The <b>start codon</b>, which codes for methionine (Met), is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""AUG"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <b>start codon</b>, which codes for methionine (Met), is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">AUG</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""63f078ae7e9c46d93f9bba08acdcdf55.png""></div>"
"The genetic code is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""degenerate"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, meaning <i>more than 1</i> codon can specify for the <i>same</i> amino acid."	"The genetic code is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">degenerate</span>, meaning <i>more than 1</i> codon can specify for the <i>same</i> amino acid.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""63f078ae7e9c46d93f9bba08acdcdf55.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Methionine&#x20;&#x28;AUG&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[amino acid]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;tryptophan&#x20;&#x28;UGG&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[amino acid]</span> are the <i>only</i> two amino acids that <u><i>are not</i> degenerate</u> - they can only be coded for by 1 specific codon."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Methionine (AUG)</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>tryptophan (UGG)</b></span> are the <i>only</i> two amino acids that <u><i>are not</i> degenerate</u> - they can only be coded for by 1 specific codon.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""63f078ae7e9c46d93f9bba08acdcdf55.png""></div>"
"The <b>wobble position</b> on a codon is its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""3&lt;sup&gt;rd&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span>&nbsp;base."	"The <b>wobble position</b> on a codon is its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">3<sup>rd</sup></span>&nbsp;base.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""63f078ae7e9c46d93f9bba08acdcdf55.png""></div>"
"Mutations that take place on the <b>wobble position</b> of a codon are <i>usually </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;silent&#x20;mutations&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[mutations]</span>, since the amino acid it codes for is often&nbsp;<i>unchanged</i>."	"Mutations that take place on the <b>wobble position</b> of a codon are <i>usually </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>silent mutations</u></span>, since the amino acid it codes for is often&nbsp;<i>unchanged</i>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""63f078ae7e9c46d93f9bba08acdcdf55.png""></div>"
"<u>Silent</u> mutations, <u>missense</u> mutations, &amp; <u>nonsense</u> mutations are all types of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""point&#x20;mutations"" data-ordinal=""1"">[mutations]</span> in DNA, since they're caused by <i>only 1</i> base substitution."	"<u>Silent</u> mutations, <u>missense</u> mutations, &amp; <u>nonsense</u> mutations are all types of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">point mutations</span> in DNA, since they're caused by <i>only 1</i> base substitution.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Silent mutation</b>: has no effect on the overall protein's function (wobble position).<div>- <b>Missense mutation</b>: 1 amino acid is substituted for another that does affect the protein's structure.</div><div>- <b>Nonsense mutation</b>: encodes a premature stop codon (also called truncation mutations).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c59257855a49c12b5b0f34d18987fd88.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Missense&#x20;mutations&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[mutations]</span> are mutations in DNA where <i>only 1</i> base is substituted - leading to <i>altered </i>protein function."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Missense mutations</b></span> are mutations in DNA where <i>only 1</i> base is substituted - leading to <i>altered </i>protein function.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Silent mutation</b>: has no effect on the overall protein's function (wobble position).<div>- <b>Missense mutation</b>: 1 amino acid is substituted for another that does affect the protein's structure.</div><div>- <b>Nonsense mutation</b>: encodes a premature stop codon (also called truncation mutations).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c59257855a49c12b5b0f34d18987fd88.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Nonsense&#x20;mutations&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[mutations]</span> are mutations in DNA that code for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>premature stop codons</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Nonsense mutations</b></span> are mutations in DNA that code for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>premature stop codons</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Silent mutation</b>: has no effect on the overall protein's function (wobble position).<div>- <b>Missense mutation</b>: 1 amino acid is substituted for another that does affect the protein's structure.</div><div>- <b>Nonsense mutation</b>: encodes a premature stop codon (also called truncation mutations).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c59257855a49c12b5b0f34d18987fd88.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Nonsense mutations</b></span> are mutations in DNA that code for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;premature&#x20;stop&#x20;codons&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Nonsense mutations</b></span> are mutations in DNA that code for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>premature stop codons</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Silent mutation</b>: has no effect on the overall protein's function (wobble position).<div>- <b>Missense mutation</b>: 1 amino acid is substituted for another that does affect the protein's structure.</div><div>- <b>Nonsense mutation</b>: encodes a premature stop codon (also called truncation mutations).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c59257855a49c12b5b0f34d18987fd88.png""><br></div></div>"
"Insertions or deletions in non-multiples of 3 in DNA bases result in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""frameshift&#x20;mutations"" data-ordinal=""1"">[mutations]</span>."	"Insertions or deletions in non-multiples of 3 in DNA bases result in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">frameshift mutations</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Silent mutation</b>: has no effect on the overall protein's function (wobble position).<div>- <b>Missense mutation</b>: 1 amino acid is substituted for another that does affect the protein's structure.</div><div>- <b>Nonsense mutation</b>: encodes a premature stop codon (also called truncation mutations).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c59257855a49c12b5b0f34d18987fd88.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Frameshift mutations</b> where bases are inserted or deleted in multiples of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""3"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> <i>do not</i> change the <u>reading frame</u>."	"<b>Frameshift mutations</b> where bases are inserted or deleted in multiples of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">3</span> <i>do not</i> change the <u>reading frame</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Silent mutation</b>: has no effect on the overall protein's function (wobble position).<div>- <b>Missense mutation</b>: 1 amino acid is substituted for another that does affect the protein's structure.</div><div>- <b>Nonsense mutation</b>: encodes a premature stop codon (also called truncation mutations).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c59257855a49c12b5b0f34d18987fd88.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Cystic&#x20;fibrosis&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[disease]</span> results from a <u>frameshift mutation</u> in a codon encoding <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">phenylalanine</span> - resulting in a <i>defective&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Cl<sup>-</sup> channel</span> that <i>never </i>exits the endoplasmic reticulum."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cystic fibrosis</b></span> results from a <u>frameshift mutation</u> in a codon encoding <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">phenylalanine</span> - resulting in a <i>defective&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Cl<sup>-</sup> channel</span> that <i>never </i>exits the endoplasmic reticulum.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Chaperonin proteins in the ER prevent the mutated Cl<sup>-</sup> channel from exiting (even though it'd still be functional).<br><div><br></div><div><img src=""c59257855a49c12b5b0f34d18987fd88.png""><br></div></div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Chaperonin proteins in the ER prevent the mutated Cl<sup>-</sup> channel from exiting (even though it'd still be functional).<br><div><br></div><div><img src=""c59257855a49c12b5b0f34d18987fd88.png""><br></div></div>"
"In transcription, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;DNA&#x20;helicase&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;topoisomerase&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> <i>unwind </i>and relieve DNA's supercoiling to allow <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>RNA polymerase</b></span> to attach to its gene of interest."	"In transcription, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>DNA helicase</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>topoisomerase</u></span> <i>unwind </i>and relieve DNA's supercoiling to allow <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>RNA polymerase</b></span> to attach to its gene of interest.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""ec15d4f7c26e10f10c98252a32297417.png""></div>"
"In transcription, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>DNA helicase</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>topoisomerase</u></span> <i>unwind </i>and relieve DNA's supercoiling to allow <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;RNA&#x20;polymerase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[enzyme]</span> to attach to its gene of interest." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""ec15d4f7c26e10f10c98252a32297417.png""></div>"
"<div>The promoter region's<b>&nbsp;</b><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>TATA box</b></span>&nbsp;has a high concentration of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;adenine&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;thymine&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> residues, since having a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>fewer</i></span> number of hydrogen bonds makes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">strand separation <i>easier</i></span>.</div>"	"<div>The promoter region's<b>&nbsp;</b><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>TATA box</b></span>&nbsp;has a high concentration of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>adenine</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>thymine</u></span> residues, since having a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>fewer</i></span> number of hydrogen bonds makes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">strand separation <i>easier</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Strand separation is needed for the <u>transcriptional machinery</u> to reach the gene of interest.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ec15d4f7c26e10f10c98252a32297417.png""></div>"
"<b>RNA polymerase</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;1&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[1/2/3]</span> is located in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>nucleolus</u></span> and synthesizes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">rRNA</span>."	"<b>RNA polymerase</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>1</b></span> is located in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>nucleolus</u></span> and synthesizes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">rRNA</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- RNA polymerase&nbsp;<b>1</b>: synthesizes rRNA in the nucleolus.</div><div>- RNA polymerase&nbsp;<b>2</b>: synthesizes hnRNA (pre-mRNA) &amp; snRNA (forms snRNPs) in the nucleus.</div><div>- RNA polymerase&nbsp;<b>3</b>: synthesizes tRNA &amp; some rRNA.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ec15d4f7c26e10f10c98252a32297417.png""><div></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fe1cf8758d064e27aacea811dec5ddaf.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>RNA polymerase</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>1</b></span> is located in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;nucleolus&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[nucleus/nucleolus]</span> and synthesizes <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""rRNA"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<b>RNA polymerase</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>1</b></span> is located in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>nucleolus</u></span> and synthesizes <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">rRNA</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"In splicing, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""introns"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are <i>removed </i>from the hnRNA transcript &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""exons"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are spliced <i>back together</i>."	"In splicing, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">introns</span> are <i>removed </i>from the hnRNA transcript &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">exons</span> are spliced <i>back together</i>.
