<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""a9388b0d7f3d539f5a76af05394c91f9.png""></div>"
"<b>Spliceosomes</b> cleave at&nbsp;<i>both </i>the 3' &amp; 5' ends of introns, where non-coding sequences are then <i>removed </i>in the form of a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""lariet&#x20;&#x28;loop&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<b>Spliceosomes</b> cleave at&nbsp;<i>both </i>the 3' &amp; 5' ends of introns, where non-coding sequences are then <i>removed </i>in the form of a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">lariet (loop)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""a9388b0d7f3d539f5a76af05394c91f9.png""></div>"
"The <b>5' cap</b> added to the end of the <u>hnRNA</u> transcript is made of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""7&#x2D;methylguanylate&#x20;triphosphates"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <b>5' cap</b> added to the end of the <u>hnRNA</u> transcript is made of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">7-methylguanylate triphosphates</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Strings of these nucleotides...</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-e2c7b8fa846b941b8f9b7bdb03c4d4ee45e2a14f.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""a9388b0d7f3d539f5a76af05394c91f9.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;5&#x27;&#x2D;cap&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[5'-cap/3'-tail]</span> added to the <u>hnRNA transcript</u> allows the <i>future </i>mRNA to both <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">survive longer</span> in the cytoplasm &amp; get <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">recognized by ribosomes</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>5'-cap</b></span> added to the <u>hnRNA transcript</u> allows the <i>future </i>mRNA to both <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">survive longer</span> in the cytoplasm &amp; get <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">recognized by ribosomes</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>3'-tail on the other hand serves to limit the amount of time the mRNA transcript can exist in the cytosol (like a ticking time-bomb).</div><div><br></div><img src=""a9388b0d7f3d539f5a76af05394c91f9.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>5'-cap</b></span> added to the <u>hnRNA transcript</u> allows the <i>future </i>mRNA to both <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""survive&#x20;longer"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in the cytoplasm &amp; get <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""recognized&#x20;by&#x20;ribosomes"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>5'-cap</b></span> added to the <u>hnRNA transcript</u> allows the <i>future </i>mRNA to both <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">survive longer</span> in the cytoplasm &amp; get <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">recognized by ribosomes</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>3'-tail on the other hand serves to limit the amount of time the mRNA transcript can exist in the cytosol (like a ticking time-bomb).</div><div><br></div><img src=""a9388b0d7f3d539f5a76af05394c91f9.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;3&#x27;&#x2D;tail&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[5'-cap/3'-tail]</span> added to the <u>hnRNA transcript</u>&nbsp;serves to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>limit </i>mRNA's time in the cytosol</span>, since its residues slowly degrade over time."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>3'-tail</b></span> added to the <u>hnRNA transcript</u>&nbsp;serves to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>limit </i>mRNA's time in the cytosol</span>, since its residues slowly degrade over time.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>5'-cap on the other hand protects the transcript in the cytosol &amp; allows ribosomes to recognize and bind to the transcript.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""a9388b0d7f3d539f5a76af05394c91f9.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>3'-tail</b></span> added to the <u>hnRNA transcript</u>&nbsp;serves to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;limit&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;mRNA&#x27;s&#x20;time&#x20;in&#x20;the&#x20;cytosol"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>, since its residues slowly degrade over time."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>3'-tail</b></span> added to the <u>hnRNA transcript</u>&nbsp;serves to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>limit </i>mRNA's time in the cytosol</span>, since its residues slowly degrade over time.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>5'-cap on the other hand protects the transcript in the cytosol &amp; allows ribosomes to recognize and bind to the transcript.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""a9388b0d7f3d539f5a76af05394c91f9.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;3&#x27;&#x2D;tail&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[5'-cap/3'-tail]</span> added to the <u>hnRNA transcript</u>&nbsp;is made of<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>numerous&nbsp;</i>adenine residues</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>3'-tail</b></span> added to the <u>hnRNA transcript</u>&nbsp;is made of<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>numerous&nbsp;</i>adenine residues</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Catalyzed by <b>polyadenylate polymerase</b> (powered by ATP hydrolysis).</div><div>- 5'-cap is made of 7-methylgnauylate triphosphate residues.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""a9388b0d7f3d539f5a76af05394c91f9.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>3'-tail</b></span> added to the <u>hnRNA transcript</u>&nbsp;is made of<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;numerous&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;adenine&#x20;residues"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>3'-tail</b></span> added to the <u>hnRNA transcript</u>&nbsp;is made of<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>numerous&nbsp;</i>adenine residues</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Catalyzed by <b>polyadenylate polymerase</b> (powered by ATP hydrolysis).</div><div>- 5'-cap is made of 7-methylgnauylate triphosphate residues.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""a9388b0d7f3d539f5a76af05394c91f9.png""></div>"
"<div>mRNA transcripts with <i>longer</i> <u>3'-poly-A tails</u> exist for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;longer&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[shorter/longer]</span> amounts of time in the cytosol.</div>"	"<div>mRNA transcripts with <i>longer</i> <u>3'-poly-A tails</u> exist for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>longer</i></span> amounts of time in the cytosol.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>3'-tail acts like a fuse on a time bomb, since it slowly gets degraded over time.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""a9388b0d7f3d539f5a76af05394c91f9.png""></div>"
"The mRNA transcript has <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;untranslated&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;regions&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;on <i>both</i> its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">3'</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">5'</span> ends, since the ribosome <i>only </i>translates from AUG → stop codon."	"The mRNA transcript has <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>untranslated </i>regions</b></span>&nbsp;on <i>both</i> its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">3'</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">5'</span> ends, since the ribosome <i>only </i>translates from AUG → stop codon.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These are codons that never ended up getting translated.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a9388b0d7f3d539f5a76af05394c91f9.png""><br></div></div>"
"The mRNA transcript has <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>untranslated </i>regions</b></span>&nbsp;on <i>both</i> its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""3&#x27;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""5&#x27;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> ends, since the ribosome <i>only </i>translates from AUG → stop codon."	"The mRNA transcript has <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>untranslated </i>regions</b></span>&nbsp;on <i>both</i> its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">3'</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">5'</span> ends, since the ribosome <i>only </i>translates from AUG → stop codon.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Before <b>proteins</b> can exit the nucleus, they require <u>nuclear export sequences</u> in order to pass through the nuclear pores.</div>"
"<b>RNA polymerase 1</b> transcribes genes for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""5&#x2E;8S"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""18S"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, and <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""28S"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> rRNA moleucles in the nucleolus."	"<b>RNA polymerase 1</b> transcribes genes for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">5.8S</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">18S</span>, and <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">28S</span> rRNA moleucles in the nucleolus.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Normally it goes from the <b>A</b> site → <b>P</b> → <b>E</b>, but the initiator tRNA goes <u>straight</u> into the <b>P</b> site.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c1f34b4ad2542417613bf62a95256437.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div></div>"

"In the initiation of translation, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>initiation factors (IF)</b></span> help <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>direct </i>the <u>large</u>&nbsp;subunit</span> to the mRNA transcript.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Prenylation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[process]</span> is when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>isoprene</u></span> groups are added to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">membrane-bound enzymes</span>." <div>Added to cysteine residues.</div><div><br></div><img src=""3-s2.0-B9780080453828006626-gr28.jpg""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Inducers </b>are needed to stop the repressor's <u>continual</u> blockage of the operator.</div><div><br></div><img src=""17b39d2dc5800777977d4974b4a1a551.png""></div>"
"In <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;inducible&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[inducible/repressible]</span> <b>operons</b>, transcription is <i>always </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>off</i></span> until the <u>inducer</u> inhibits the repressor's blockage of the operator." "In gene expression, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;negative&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-]</span> <b>control </b>is when the <u>regulatory gene</u> produces a <i>repressor</i> that continually <i>inhibits </i>transcription.&nbsp;"	"In gene expression, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>negative</b></span> <b>control </b>is when the <u>regulatory gene</u> produces a <i>repressor</i> that continually <i>inhibits </i>transcription.&nbsp;
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Inducers are then needed to inhibit the repressor.</div><div><br></div><img src=""17b39d2dc5800777977d4974b4a1a551.png""></div>"
"The <b>lac operon</b> is a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""inducible"" data-ordinal=""1"">[inducible/repressible]</span> operon."	"The <b>lac operon</b> is a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">inducible</span> operon.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Uses inducer molecules to bind to repressors to inhibit repression - allowing transcription.</div><div>- Lactase transcription is normally always <u>off</u>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fadb2d33bbcc50475f0cdf2ca0ce207c.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""17b39d2dc5800777977d4974b4a1a551.png""></div>"
"In the <b>lac operon</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;lac&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[protein]</span> subunits combine together to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inhibit</i></span> the transcription of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">lactase</span>."	"In the <b>lac operon</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>lac</u></span> subunits combine together to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inhibit</i></span> the transcription of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">lactase</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Lactose&nbsp;molecules</b> allosterically inhibit these lac subunits - <u>preventing</u> them from forming ""road blocks"" (thereby enabling lactase transcription).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fadb2d33bbcc50475f0cdf2ca0ce207c.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""17b39d2dc5800777977d4974b4a1a551.png""></div>"
"In the <b>lac operon</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>lac</u></span> subunits combine together to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;inhibit&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[inhibit/stimulate]</span> the transcription of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">lactase</span>."	"In the <b>lac operon</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>lac</u></span> subunits combine together to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inhibit</i></span> the transcription of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">lactase</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Lactose&nbsp;molecules</b> allosterically inhibit these lac subunits - <u>preventing</u> them from forming ""road blocks"" (thereby enabling lactase transcription).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fadb2d33bbcc50475f0cdf2ca0ce207c.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""17b39d2dc5800777977d4974b4a1a551.png""></div>"
"In the <b>lac operon</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>lac</u></span> subunits combine together to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inhibit</i></span> the transcription of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""lactase"" data-ordinal=""3"">[enzyme]</span>."	"In the <b>lac operon</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>lac</u></span> subunits combine together to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inhibit</i></span> the transcription of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">lactase</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Lactose&nbsp;molecules</b> allosterically inhibit these lac subunits - <u>preventing</u> them from forming ""road blocks"" (thereby enabling lactase transcription).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fadb2d33bbcc50475f0cdf2ca0ce207c.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""17b39d2dc5800777977d4974b4a1a551.png""></div>"
"<div>In the lac operon, the presence of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;lactose&#x20;molecules&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <i>enables</i> transcription by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inhibiting</i> <u>lac repressors</u></span> - causing them to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>dissociate</i></span> from the operator site.<br></div>"	"<div>In the lac operon, the presence of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>lactose molecules</b></span> <i>enables</i> transcription by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inhibiting</i> <u>lac repressors</u></span> - causing them to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>dissociate</i></span> from the operator site.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Lac proteins are the proteins that assemble on the operator site and prevent transcription from happening.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fadb2d33bbcc50475f0cdf2ca0ce207c.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""17b39d2dc5800777977d4974b4a1a551.png""></div>"
"In the <b>lac operon</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span> glucose levels <i>stimulate</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>catabolite activator protein (CAP)</u></span> transcription - <i>enabling</i> lactase transcription after binding to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">operator site</span>."	"In the <b>lac operon</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> glucose levels <i>stimulate</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>catabolite activator protein (CAP)</u></span> transcription - <i>enabling</i> lactase transcription after binding to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">operator site</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- CAP binding enabling transcription is what makes the lac operon a <b>positive control</b> mechanism.</div><div>- When glucose levels are low, it's a <u>good idea</u> to use <b>lactose</b> as an alternative source of energy (hence why CAP proteins are transcribed).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fadb2d33bbcc50475f0cdf2ca0ce207c.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""17b39d2dc5800777977d4974b4a1a551.png""></div>"
"In the <b>lac operon</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> glucose levels <i>stimulate</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;catabolite&#x20;activator&#x20;protein&#x20;&#x28;CAP&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[protein]</span> transcription - <i>enabling</i> lactase transcription after binding to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">operator site</span>."	"In the <b>lac operon</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> glucose levels <i>stimulate</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>catabolite activator protein (CAP)</u></span> transcription - <i>enabling</i> lactase transcription after binding to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">operator site</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- CAP binding enabling transcription is what makes the lac operon a <b>positive control</b> mechanism.</div><div>- When glucose levels are low, it's a <u>good idea</u> to use <b>lactose</b> as an alternative source of energy (hence why CAP proteins are transcribed).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fadb2d33bbcc50475f0cdf2ca0ce207c.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""17b39d2dc5800777977d4974b4a1a551.png""></div>"
"In the <b>lac operon</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> glucose levels <i>stimulate</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>catabolite activator protein (CAP)</u></span> transcription - <i>enabling</i> lactase transcription after binding to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""operator&#x20;site"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>."	"In the <b>lac operon</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> glucose levels <i>stimulate</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>catabolite activator protein (CAP)</u></span> transcription - <i>enabling</i> lactase transcription after binding to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">operator site</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- CAP binding enabling transcription is what makes the lac operon a <b>positive control</b> mechanism.</div><div>- When glucose levels are low, it's a <u>good idea</u> to use <b>lactose</b> as an alternative source of energy (hence why CAP proteins are transcribed).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fadb2d33bbcc50475f0cdf2ca0ce207c.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""17b39d2dc5800777977d4974b4a1a551.png""></div>"
"The <u>lac operon</u> is an example of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;positive&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-]</span> <b>control</b>, since CAP binding <i>facilitates </i>the transcription of lactase." "<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Transcription&#x20;factors&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> have 2 primary structural regions: the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>DNA-binding</u></span> domain &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>activation</u></span> domain."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Transcription factors</b></span> have 2 primary structural regions: the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>DNA-binding</u></span> domain &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>activation</u></span> domain.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>DNA-binding domain</b>: binds to DNA itself (on the promoter region).</div><div>- <b>Activation domain</b>: binds to other transcription factors, RNA polymerase, histone acetylases, etc.</div><div><br></div><img src=""300px-LacI_Dimer_Structure_Annotated.png""></div>"

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Transcription factors</b></span> have 2 primary structural regions: the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>DNA-binding</u></span> domain &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>activation</u></span> domain.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;activation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>domain </b>on <u>transcription factors</u> binds to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>other</i> transcription factors &amp; enzymes</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>activation</b></span> <b>domain </b>on <u>transcription factors</u> binds to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>other</i> transcription factors &amp; enzymes</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>DNA-binding domain</b>: binds to DNA itself (on the promoter region).</div><div>- <b>Activation domain</b>: binds to other transcription factors, RNA polymerase, histone acetylases, etc.</div><div><br></div><img src=""300px-LacI_Dimer_Structure_Annotated.png""></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;DNA&#x2D;binding&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>domain </b>on <u>transcription factors</u> binds to specific DNA sequences within the promoter region.<br></div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>DNA-binding</b></span> <b>domain </b>on <u>transcription factors</u> binds to specific DNA sequences within the promoter region.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>DNA-binding domain</b>: binds to DNA itself (on the promoter region).</div><div>- <b>Activation domain</b>: binds to other transcription factors, RNA polymerase, histone acetylases, etc.</div><div><br></div><img src=""300px-LacI_Dimer_Structure_Annotated.png""></div>"
"<div>In <b>DNA</b>, <u>promoter regions</u> and <u>enhancer regions</u> are examples of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;cis&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[cis/trans]</span>-<b>regulators</b>.</div>"	"<div>In <b>DNA</b>, <u>promoter regions</u> and <u>enhancer regions</u> are examples of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>cis</b></span>-<b>regulators</b>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Cis</b>: elements that regulate DNA that are located <u>locally</u>.</div><div>- <b>Trans</b>: elements that regulate DNA that originate from distant places (like transcription factors).</div><div><br></div><img src=""Effect-of-cis-and-trans-acting-DNA-variants-on-expression-levels-of-genes-Polymorphic.ppm""></div>"
"<div>In <b>DNA</b>, <u>transcription factors</u>&nbsp;are examples of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;trans&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[cis/trans]</span>-<b>regulators</b>.</div>"	"<div>In <b>DNA</b>, <u>transcription factors</u>&nbsp;are examples of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>trans</b></span>-<b>regulators</b>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Cis</b>: elements that regulate DNA that are located <u>locally</u>.</div><div>- <b>Trans</b>: elements that regulate DNA that originate from distant places (like transcription factors).</div><div><br></div><img src=""Effect-of-cis-and-trans-acting-DNA-variants-on-expression-levels-of-genes-Polymorphic.ppm""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Enhancer&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b> regions</b>&nbsp;are regions on DNA that <i>stimulate</i> <u>gene amplification</u> after <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>particular </i>transcription factors</span>&nbsp;bind to their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">response elements</span> on the mediator complex."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Enhancer</b></span><b> regions</b>&nbsp;are regions on DNA that <i>stimulate</i> <u>gene amplification</u> after <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>particular </i>transcription factors</span>&nbsp;bind to their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">response elements</span> on the mediator complex.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Response elements</b>: particular sequence of DNA that can only bind to one speicfic type of transcription factor.</div><div>- Transcription factors might be specific for estrogen amplification, cortisol amplification, etc.</div><div><br></div><img src=""1f90fe81ff5ba10fdcbcb2e912ffd6bb.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Enhancer</b></span><b> regions</b>&nbsp;are regions on DNA that <i>stimulate</i> <u>gene amplification</u> after <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;particular&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;transcription&#x20;factors"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>&nbsp;bind to their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""response&#x20;elements"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> on the mediator complex."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Enhancer</b></span><b> regions</b>&nbsp;are regions on DNA that <i>stimulate</i> <u>gene amplification</u> after <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>particular </i>transcription factors</span>&nbsp;bind to their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">response elements</span> on the mediator complex.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Response elements</b>: particular sequence of DNA that can only bind to one speicfic type of transcription factor.</div><div>- Transcription factors might be specific for estrogen amplification, cortisol amplification, etc.</div><div><br></div><img src=""1f90fe81ff5ba10fdcbcb2e912ffd6bb.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Response&#x20;elements&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are <i>specific </i><u>DNA sequences</u>&nbsp;located in its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">promoter region</span> that allow the binding of only <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>very specific</i> transcription factors</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Response elements</b></span> are <i>specific </i><u>DNA sequences</u>&nbsp;located in its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">promoter region</span> that allow the binding of only <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>very specific</i> transcription factors</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Transcription factors might be specific for estrogen amplification, cortisol amplification, etc... key point is that DNA can only bind that one particular transcription factor.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""1f90fe81ff5ba10fdcbcb2e912ffd6bb.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Response elements</b></span> are <i>specific </i><u>DNA sequences</u>&nbsp;located in its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""promoter&#x20;region"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> that allow the binding of only <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;very&#x20;specific&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;transcription&#x20;factors"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Response elements</b></span> are <i>specific </i><u>DNA sequences</u>&nbsp;located in its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">promoter region</span> that allow the binding of only <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>very specific</i> transcription factors</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Transcription factors might be specific for estrogen amplification, cortisol amplification, etc... key point is that DNA can only bind that one particular transcription factor.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""1f90fe81ff5ba10fdcbcb2e912ffd6bb.png""></div>"
"In order for DNA's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;enhancer&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> region &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;promoter&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> region to come into <i>contact </i>with each other, DNA has to bend itself into a <u>hairpin loop</u>."	"In order for DNA's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>enhancer</b></span> region &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>promoter</b></span> region to come into <i>contact </i>with each other, DNA has to bend itself into a <u>hairpin loop</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Enhancer region &amp; promoter region are usually located far apart.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1f90fe81ff5ba10fdcbcb2e912ffd6bb.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Histone&#x20;acetylases&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzymes]</span> <i>add </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>acetyl groups</u></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>lysine</u></span> residues on histone tails - <i>neutralizing </i>the histone's charge &amp; causing it to unwind from DNA.&nbsp;"	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Histone acetylases</b></span> <i>add </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>acetyl groups</u></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>lysine</u></span> residues on histone tails - <i>neutralizing </i>the histone's charge &amp; causing it to unwind from DNA.&nbsp;
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This allows transcription factors to then bind to DNA.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a9fcecfb00e1c67cbb62cdd437294096.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Histone acetylases</b></span> <i>add </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;acetyl&#x20;groups&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;lysine&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[a.a.]</span> residues on histone tails - <i>neutralizing </i>the histone's charge &amp; causing it to unwind from DNA.&nbsp;"	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Histone acetylases</b></span> <i>add </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>acetyl groups</u></span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>lysine</u></span> residues on histone tails - <i>neutralizing </i>the histone's charge &amp; causing it to unwind from DNA.&nbsp;
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This allows transcription factors to then bind to DNA.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a9fcecfb00e1c67cbb62cdd437294096.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><b>Histone acetylation</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[inc./dec.]</span> <u>gene expression</u>, since it forms the gene into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""eu"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>chromatin.</div>"	"<div><b>Histone acetylation</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increases</i></span> <u>gene expression</u>, since it forms the gene into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">eu</span>chromatin.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Adds an <b>acetyl group</b> to the <u>lysine</u> residues of histone tails - causing them to unwind from DNA (enabling transcription).</div><div>- This allows transcription factors to then bind to DNA.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a9fcecfb00e1c67cbb62cdd437294096.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Histone&#x20;deacetylases&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzymes]</span>&nbsp;<i>remove</i>&nbsp;<u>acetyl groups</u>&nbsp;from&nbsp;<u>lysine</u>&nbsp;residues on histone tails -&nbsp;<i>restoring&nbsp;</i>the histone's charge &amp; causing it to tighten around DNA.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Histone deacetylases</b></span>&nbsp;<i>remove</i>&nbsp;<u>acetyl groups</u>&nbsp;from&nbsp;<u>lysine</u>&nbsp;residues on histone tails -&nbsp;<i>restoring&nbsp;</i>the histone's charge &amp; causing it to tighten around DNA.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Histone proteins are positively charged, so restoring this positive charge tightens it around DNA (since DNA is negatively charged).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a9fcecfb00e1c67cbb62cdd437294096.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;DNA&#x20;methylase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzymes]</span> <i>adds&nbsp;</i><u>methyl groups</u> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">adenine</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cytosine</span> nucleotides on DNA - <i>silencing </i>gene transcription."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>DNA methylase</b></span> <i>adds&nbsp;</i><u>methyl groups</u> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">adenine</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cytosine</span> nucleotides on DNA - <i>silencing </i>gene transcription.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Condenses the gene into heterochromatin.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-3395413530374ac027ef8218b2abc1ddaf7fdfbc.jpg""></div>"

<div>- These different tRNA molecules pair to their own unique aminoacyl-tRNA synthetases.</div><div>- Wobble pairing occurs because the 3rd base experiences non-Watson and Crick pairing, meaning some codons can base pair with anticodons if only their first 2 match.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""https://www.mun.ca/biology/scarr/MGA2-03-30.jpg""><br></div></div>"

Disulfide bonds <u>are not</u> the only interactions that hold multiple subunits together!<div><br></div><div><img src=""6a211f8baefc02ca84206384086b8a12.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Hemoglobin</b> is found <i>primarily </i>in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""blood"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, whereas <b>myoglobin </b>is found <i>primarily </i>in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""muscle"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""organs"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Even though this looks mostly hydrophobic, the single N<sup>+</sup> charge makes it hydrophilic.</div><div><br></div><img src=""3e7b0d1bab82f8dfca1791aed4fb93a3.png""></div>"
"<b>β-oxidation</b> undergoes <u>multiple rounds</u> <i>per </i>2 carbons, wtih the number of rounds calculated as <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x28;&#x23;carbons&#x29;&#x2F;2&#x20;&#x2D;&#x20;1"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span>."	"<b>β-oxidation</b> undergoes <u>multiple rounds</u> <i>per </i>2 carbons, wtih the number of rounds calculated as <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">(#carbons)/2 - 1</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>A 14 carbon fatty acid undergoes β-oxidation <u>6 times</u> (14/2 - 1 = 6).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-27cf85628fecbd444b4d7e1d8cf6ba93d47fe052.jpg""><br></div>"
"<div>A <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">14</span> carbon <u>fatty acid</u> undergoing <b>β-oxidation</b> <i>alone </i>produces <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""7"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> acetyl-CoA, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""7"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> NADH, and <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""7"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> FADH<sub>2</sub>.</div>"	"<div>A <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">14</span> carbon <u>fatty acid</u> undergoing <b>β-oxidation</b> <i>alone </i>produces <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">7</span> acetyl-CoA, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">7</span> NADH, and <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">7</span> FADH<sub>2</sub>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>7 acetyl-CoA then enters the TCA to make 21 NADH &amp; 7 FADH<sub>2</sub>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-27cf85628fecbd444b4d7e1d8cf6ba93d47fe052.jpg""><br></div>"
"<div>A <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""14"" data-ordinal=""2"">[#]</span> carbon <u>fatty acid</u> undergoing <b>β-oxidation</b> <i>alone </i>produces <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">7</span> acetyl-CoA, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">7</span> NADH, and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">7</span> FADH<sub>2</sub>.</div>"	"<div>A <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">14</span> carbon <u>fatty acid</u> undergoing <b>β-oxidation</b> <i>alone </i>produces <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">7</span> acetyl-CoA, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">7</span> NADH, and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">7</span> FADH<sub>2</sub>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>7 acetyl-CoA then enters the TCA to make 21 NADH &amp; 7 FADH<sub>2</sub>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-27cf85628fecbd444b4d7e1d8cf6ba93d47fe052.jpg""><br></div>"
"<div>These sugars are held together by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""α"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-glycosidic linkages.</div><div><br></div><img src=""f53588a7d9b960e254312529221f72b0.png"">"	"<div>These sugars are held together by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">α</span>-glycosidic linkages.</div><div><br></div><img src=""f53588a7d9b960e254312529221f72b0.png"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Both bonds point in the <u>same</u> direction.</div>"
"<div>These sugars are held together by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""β"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-glycosidic linkages.</div><div><br></div><img src=""c99e54051a6e33f4ad9133d005da7ccb.png""><br>"	"<div>These sugars are held together by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">β</span>-glycosidic linkages.</div><div><br></div><img src=""c99e54051a6e33f4ad9133d005da7ccb.png""><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Bonds face <u>opposite</u> directions.</div>"
"<b>Coupled transport</b> (symport/antiport) is a form of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""active"" data-ordinal=""1"">[passive/active]</span> transport."	"<b>Coupled transport</b> (symport/antiport) is a form of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">active</span> transport.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Even though it does not require ATP, it's still considered active transport (because it's moving <u>against</u> its concentration gradient).</div><div><br></div><img src=""Porters.PNG""></div>"
"<b>Phosphatidyl<u>inositol</u> </b>has a net <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""negative"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-/neutral]</span> charge."	"<b>Phosphatidyl<u>inositol</u> </b>has a net <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">negative</span> charge.

<div>In contrast, water-soluble vitamins exit through the urine.</div><div><br></div><img src=""2aa131c9b20853d1b55eb7e1aab35edb.jpg""></div>"

"In <b>gel electrophoresis</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;short&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[short/large]</span> DNA strands <u>migrate further</u> in the gel." <div>- Separates by size.</div><div>- Charge doesn't factor in, since DNA molecules have the same negative charge.</div><div><br></div><img src=""279dfd0b7fcd68972420fd19772eb965.png""></div>"
"In <b>gel electrophoresis</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;large&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[short/large]</span> DNA strands <u><i>do not</i></u>&nbsp;migrate very far&nbsp;in the gel." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Separates by size.</div><div>- Charge doesn't factor in, since DNA molecules have the same negative charge.</div><div><br></div><img src=""279dfd0b7fcd68972420fd19772eb965.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Ethidium&#x20;bromide&#x20;&#x28;EtBr&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[compound]</span> <i>intercalates </i>in-between <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>DNA basepairs</u></span> to make them <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>fluoresce</i></span> for visualization.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ethidium bromide (EtBr)</b></span> <i>intercalates </i>in-between <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>DNA basepairs</u></span> to make them <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>fluoresce</i></span> for visualization.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Fluoresces under <b>UV light</b>.</div><div><br></div><div>- Separates by size.</div><div>- Charge doesn't factor in, since DNA molecules have the same negative charge.</div><div><br></div><img src=""279dfd0b7fcd68972420fd19772eb965.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ethidium bromide (EtBr)</b></span> <i>intercalates </i>in-between <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;DNA&#x20;basepairs&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to make them <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;fluoresce&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> for visualization.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ethidium bromide (EtBr)</b></span> <i>intercalates </i>in-between <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>DNA basepairs</u></span> to make them <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>fluoresce</i></span> for visualization.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Fluoresces under <b>UV light</b>.</div><div><br></div><div>- Separates by size.</div><div>- Charge doesn't factor in, since DNA molecules have the same negative charge.</div><div><br></div><img src=""279dfd0b7fcd68972420fd19772eb965.png""></div>"
"<b>Microarray hybridization</b> uses wells of either single-stranded DNA or RNA capable of binding to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;complementary&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;DNA&#x2F;RNA"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> to <i>quantify </i><u>gene expression</u>."	"<b>Microarray hybridization</b> uses wells of either single-stranded DNA or RNA capable of binding to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>complementary </i>DNA/RNA</span> to <i>quantify </i><u>gene expression</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Complementary DNA refers to cDNA (DNA synthesized from single-stranded RNA by reverse transcriptase).<div>- Complementary RNA refers to mRNA.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""059ca7ae71979ca4996d918afcdfdae5.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;cDNA&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b>&nbsp;</b>is DNA <i>made </i>from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>mRNA transcripts</u></span>, meaning it can bind <i>directly </i>to DNA's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">sense</span>-strand."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>cDNA</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>is DNA <i>made </i>from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>mRNA transcripts</u></span>, meaning it can bind <i>directly </i>to DNA's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">sense</span>-strand.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Since cDNA is the complement of mRNA (modeled after sense-strand), that means cDNA is the same as DNA's anti-sense strand (i.e. why it's complementary to the sense-strand).</div><div>- Microarray hybridization can be used to detect specific mRNA transcripts based on whether or not cDNA/mRNA binds its complement.</div><div><br></div><img src=""d182898ac435aac4c743120212eb5613.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>cDNA</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>is DNA <i>made </i>from <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;mRNA&#x20;transcripts&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>, meaning it can bind <i>directly </i>to DNA's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""sense"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>-strand."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>cDNA</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>is DNA <i>made </i>from <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>mRNA transcripts</u></span>, meaning it can bind <i>directly </i>to DNA's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">sense</span>-strand.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Since cDNA is the complement of mRNA (modeled after sense-strand), that means cDNA is the same as DNA's anti-sense strand (i.e. why it's complementary to the sense-strand).</div><div>- Microarray hybridization can be used to detect specific mRNA transcripts based on whether or not cDNA/mRNA binds its complement.</div><div><br></div><img src=""d182898ac435aac4c743120212eb5613.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ELISA&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[technique]</span> detects<i> specific</i> proteins in mixtures by using <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>antibodies</u></span> to bind to the protein, <i>washing out</i> non-bound antibodies, and adding substrate to measure <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>enzymatic activity</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ELISA</b></span> detects<i> specific</i> proteins in mixtures by using <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>antibodies</u></span> to bind to the protein, <i>washing out</i> non-bound antibodies, and adding substrate to measure <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>enzymatic activity</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The amount of substrate should be <u>proportional</u> to the amount of protein - don't saturate it with substrate.</div><div><br></div><img src=""53e761e5df86c2f3bb1d63fee8f67c5e.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ELISA</b></span> detects<i> specific</i> proteins in mixtures by using <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;antibodies&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to bind to the protein, <i>washing out</i> non-bound antibodies, and adding substrate to measure <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;enzymatic&#x20;activity&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>." "<b>Telomerase</b> extends DNA's lagging strand by synthesizing an overhang made of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;complementary&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;RNA&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> and allowing DNA polymerase to synthesize its complement."	"<b>Telomerase</b> extends DNA's lagging strand by synthesizing an overhang made of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>complementary </i>RNA</u></span> and allowing DNA polymerase to synthesize its complement.

"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>siRNAs</b></span> are <i>always</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>perfectly</i></span>&nbsp;complementary RNA molecules that&nbsp;bind<i>&nbsp;</i>to&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;mRNA&#x20;transcripts&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">cleave them (destruction)</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>siRNAs</b></span> are <i>always</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>perfectly</i></span>&nbsp;complementary RNA molecules that&nbsp;bind<i>&nbsp;</i>to&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>mRNA transcripts</u></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">cleave them (destruction)</span>.<br></div>

<div>- <b>siRNAs</b> destroy mRNAs by cleaving them.</div><div>- <b>miRNAs</b> destroy mRNAs by preventing translation.</div><div><br></div><img src=""2966e8fc7947467dce265d073b74b626.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;miRNAs&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[miRNAs/siRNAs]</span> can be either <i>somewhat</i>&nbsp;or <i>perfectly</i> complementary RNA molecules that bind<i> </i>to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>mRNA transcripts</u></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">silence their expression</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>miRNAs</b></span> can be either <i>somewhat</i>&nbsp;or <i>perfectly</i> complementary RNA molecules that bind<i> </i>to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>mRNA transcripts</u></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">silence their expression</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>When miRNAs are somewhat complementary, they prevent translation. When they're perfectly complementary, they cleave the transcript.</div><div><br></div><div>-&nbsp;<b>siRNAs</b>&nbsp;destroy&nbsp;mRNAs by signalling for their destruction.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>miRNAs</b>&nbsp;destroy mRNAs by either cutting them in half or inhibiting ribosome-binding.</div><div><br></div><img src=""2966e8fc7947467dce265d073b74b626.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>miRNAs</b></span> can be either <i>somewhat</i>&nbsp;or <i>perfectly</i> complementary RNA molecules that bind<i> </i>to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;mRNA&#x20;transcripts&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">silence their expression</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>miRNAs</b></span> can be either <i>somewhat</i>&nbsp;or <i>perfectly</i> complementary RNA molecules that bind<i> </i>to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>mRNA transcripts</u></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">silence their expression</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>When miRNAs are somewhat complementary, they prevent translation. When they're perfectly complementary, they cleave the transcript.</div><div><br></div><div>-&nbsp;<b>siRNAs</b>&nbsp;destroy&nbsp;mRNAs by signalling for their destruction.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>miRNAs</b>&nbsp;destroy mRNAs by either cutting them in half or inhibiting ribosome-binding.</div><div><br></div><img src=""2966e8fc7947467dce265d073b74b626.png""></div>"
"<b>Alternative splicing</b> donor &amp; acceptor sites <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;are&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[are/are not]</span> found <u>within exons</u>."	"<b>Alternative splicing</b> donor &amp; acceptor sites <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>are not</i></span> found <u>within exons</u>.

"The <b>chain-terminating method</b> of <u>DNA sequencing</u> is <i>also</i> referred to as <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Sanger&#x20;sequencing"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Incorporates ddNTPs (which lack 3' OH's) into the growing DNA strand - terminating its growth. Strands are then separated through gel electrophoresis.</div><div>- Sanger also developed a way to sequence <u>proteins</u> (using DNFB).</div><div><br></div><img src=""75c89e738b2b1123ee383f5eb1fcf141.png""></div>"

<img src=""05009edc9cdf50a9b1c9a065b652ece1.png""></div>"
"1 <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;amino&#x20;acid&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> weighs <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">110</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Daltons (g/mol)</span>."	"1 <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>amino acid</u></span> weighs <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">110</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Daltons (g/mol)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""05009edc9cdf50a9b1c9a065b652ece1.png""></div>"
"Do <b>tight junctions</b> or <b>gap junctions</b> help <i>resist </i><u>mechanical stress</u> at all?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;No&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&#x2D;&#x20;tight&#x20;junctions&#x20;&lt;i&gt;only&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;help&#x20;prevent&#x20;paracellular&#x20;leakage&#x2C;&#x20;while&#x20;gap&#x20;junctions&#x20;&lt;i&gt;only&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;allow&#x20;for&#x20;the&#x20;flow&#x20;of&#x20;ions&#x20;in&#x20;between&#x20;cells&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Do <b>tight junctions</b> or <b>gap junctions</b> help <i>resist </i><u>mechanical stress</u> at all?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>No</u> - tight junctions <i>only</i> help prevent paracellular leakage, while gap junctions <i>only </i>allow for the flow of ions in between cells.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Desmosomes</b> instead help resist mechanical stress by connecting the <u>intermediate filaments</u> of adjacent cells together.</div><div><br></div><img src=""a122cbed3752385dfcf6ff8540fbd654.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Do <u>neutral</u> amino acids have <i>any </i>effect on the peptide's <b>isoelectric point (pI)</b>?<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;No&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&#x2D;&#x20;they&#x20;have&#x20;&lt;i&gt;no&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;effect&#x20;whatsoever&#x2E;&#x20;&lt;i&gt;Only&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&lt;u&gt;ionizable&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;amino&#x20;acids&#x20;&#x28;acidic&#x2C;&#x20;basic&#x2C;&#x20;Tyr&#x2C;&#x20;or&#x20;Cys&#x29;&#x20;have&#x20;an&#x20;effect&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div>Do <u>neutral</u> amino acids have <i>any </i>effect on the peptide's <b>isoelectric point (pI)</b>?<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>No</u> - they have <i>no</i> effect whatsoever. <i>Only</i> <u>ionizable</u> amino acids (acidic, basic, Tyr, or Cys) have an effect.</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Only ionizable amino acids affect its isoelectric point.<div><br></div><div><img src=""c3a0f41635f8fd2074910edf9e7aacbb.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fa7894e83b390be315de6a6adaba8e77.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In <b>isoelectric focusing</b>, the <u>amino acids</u> that make the peptide's <b>pI</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""glutamic&#x20;acid&#x20;&#x28;E&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[a.a.]</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""aspartic&#x20;acid&#x20;&#x28;D&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[a.a.]</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""tyrosine&#x20;&#x28;Y&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[a.a.]</span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""cysteine&#x20;&#x28;C&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[a.a.]</span>.</div>"	"<div>In <b>isoelectric focusing</b>, the <u>amino acids</u> that make the peptide's <b>pI</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">glutamic acid (E)</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">aspartic acid (D)</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">tyrosine (Y)</span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">cysteine (C)</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Only ionizable amino acids affect its isoelectric point.<div><br></div><div><img src=""c3a0f41635f8fd2074910edf9e7aacbb.png""><br><div><br></div><div><img src=""fa7894e83b390be315de6a6adaba8e77.png""><br></div></div></div>"
"<div>In <b>isoelectric focusing</b>, the <u>amino acids</u> that make the peptide's <b>pI</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;high&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">arginine (R)</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">lysine (K)</span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">histidine (H)</span>.</div>"	"<div>In <b>isoelectric focusing</b>, the <u>amino acids</u> that make the peptide's <b>pI</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">arginine (R)</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">lysine (K)</span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">histidine (H)</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Only ionizable amino acids affect its isoelectric point.<div><br></div><div><img src=""c3a0f41635f8fd2074910edf9e7aacbb.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fa7894e83b390be315de6a6adaba8e77.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In <b>isoelectric focusing</b>, the <u>amino acids</u> that make the peptide's <b>pI</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""arginine&#x20;&#x28;R&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[a.a.]</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""lysine&#x20;&#x28;K&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[a.a.]</span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""histidine&#x20;&#x28;H&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[a.a.]</span>.</div>"	"<div>In <b>isoelectric focusing</b>, the <u>amino acids</u> that make the peptide's <b>pI</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">arginine (R)</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">lysine (K)</span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">histidine (H)</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Only ionizable amino acids affect its isoelectric point.<div><br></div><div><img src=""c3a0f41635f8fd2074910edf9e7aacbb.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fa7894e83b390be315de6a6adaba8e77.png""><br></div></div>"
"Proteins with bonds that <i>require </i><u>inputs of energy</u> (like ATP hydrolysis) to form are <b>thermodynamically </b><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""unstable"" data-ordinal=""1"">[stable/unstable]</span>."	"Proteins with bonds that <i>require </i><u>inputs of energy</u> (like ATP hydrolysis) to form are <b>thermodynamically </b><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">unstable</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The reaction must be non-spontaneous since it requires energy input - meaning the reaction on its own is thermodynamically unstable.</div><div>- Think of stability as the ability of the bond to remain bonded.</div><div><br></div><img src=""a4540072021ab40ad125ed142f523f38.png""><div><br></div><div><img src=""bd8d7db5fe1ebc880fc20db2a7990172.png""><br></div></div>"
"Proteins with bonds that <i>do not</i>&nbsp;require<i> </i><u>inputs of energy</u> (like ATP hydrolysis) to form are <b>thermodynamically </b><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""stable"" data-ordinal=""1"">[stable/unstable]</span>."	"Proteins with bonds that <i>do not</i>&nbsp;require<i> </i><u>inputs of energy</u> (like ATP hydrolysis) to form are <b>thermodynamically </b><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">stable</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The reaction must be spontaneous since it <u>does not</u> require energy input - meaning the reaction on its own is thermodynamically stable.</div><div><br></div><img src=""a4540072021ab40ad125ed142f523f38.png""><div><br></div><div><img src=""bd8d7db5fe1ebc880fc20db2a7990172.png""><br></div></div>"
"Proteins with bonds that <i>require&nbsp;</i><u>enzymes</u>&nbsp;to form are <b>kinetically</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""stable"" data-ordinal=""1"">[stable/unstable]</span>."	"Proteins with bonds that <i>require&nbsp;</i><u>enzymes</u>&nbsp;to form are <b>kinetically</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">stable</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>- Because it requires an enzyme to form, it's easy for the bond to remain bonded on its own.</div><div>- Think of stability as the ability of the bond to remain bonded.</div><div><br></div><img src=""a4540072021ab40ad125ed142f523f38.png""><div><br></div><div><img src=""bd8d7db5fe1ebc880fc20db2a7990172.png""></div></div></div>"
"Proteins with bonds that <i>do not&nbsp;</i>require<i>&nbsp;</i><u>enzymes</u>&nbsp;to form are <b>kinetically</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""unstable"" data-ordinal=""1"">[stable/unstable]</span>."	"Proteins with bonds that <i>do not&nbsp;</i>require<i>&nbsp;</i><u>enzymes</u>&nbsp;to form are <b>kinetically</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">unstable</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>- Because it <u>doesn't</u> need an enzyme to revert back to its non-bonded form, the bond is kinetically unstable.</div><div>- Think of stability as the ability of the bond to remain bonded. If&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><img src=""a4540072021ab40ad125ed142f523f38.png""><div><br></div><div><img src=""bd8d7db5fe1ebc880fc20db2a7990172.png""></div></div></div>"
"Equation to calculate the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;percent&#x20;yield&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of one <i>particular</i>&nbsp;<u>protein</u>&nbsp;from an <i>unpurified </i>mixture:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""821175a2892b03c00551b770f6c744d2.png""></span><br></div>"	"Equation to calculate the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>percent yield</b></span> of one <i>particular</i>&nbsp;<u>protein</u>&nbsp;from an <i>unpurified </i>mixture:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""821175a2892b03c00551b770f6c744d2.png""></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""bfe46cec46df0e70a50f2fef54fb0c84.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""dd728729ff6b3c43d41b1bc273993082.png""></div>"
"Equation to calculate the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>percent yield</b></span> of one <i>particular</i>&nbsp;<u>protein</u>&nbsp;from an <i>unpurified </i>mixture:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;821175a2892b03c00551b770f6c744d2&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"Equation to calculate the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>percent yield</b></span> of one <i>particular</i>&nbsp;<u>protein</u>&nbsp;from an <i>unpurified </i>mixture:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""821175a2892b03c00551b770f6c744d2.png""></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""bfe46cec46df0e70a50f2fef54fb0c84.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""dd728729ff6b3c43d41b1bc273993082.png""></div>"
"<div>In the equation to calculate the percent yield of <u>proteins</u> in an <i>unpurified</i> mixture, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>total activity</b></span>&nbsp;is calculated by <i>multiplying&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;total&#x20;protein&#x20;amount&#x20;&#x28;mg&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> x <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;specific&#x20;activity&#x20;&#x28;u&#x2F;mg&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""821175a2892b03c00551b770f6c744d2.png""><br></div>"	"<div>In the equation to calculate the percent yield of <u>proteins</u> in an <i>unpurified</i> mixture, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>total activity</b></span>&nbsp;is calculated by <i>multiplying&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>total protein amount (mg)</u></span> x <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>specific activity (u/mg)</u></span>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""821175a2892b03c00551b770f6c744d2.png""><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Using information below...</div><div><br></div><div>- Total activity of purified protein = (200 mg) x (1) = 200&nbsp;</div><div>- Total activity of unpurified protein = (800 mg) x (0.5) = 400</div><div>- Percent yield = (purified TA)/(unpurified TA) * 100 = 50%.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""bfe46cec46df0e70a50f2fef54fb0c84.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""dd728729ff6b3c43d41b1bc273993082.png""></div>"
"<u>Non-reducing</u> <b>SDS-PAGE</b> separates proteins by molecular weight by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""neutralizing&#x20;their&#x20;charges"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; disrupting <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;all&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;non&#x2D;covalent&#x20;interactions"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> - leaving them in their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">primary</span> structures."	"<u>Non-reducing</u> <b>SDS-PAGE</b> separates proteins by molecular weight by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">neutralizing their charges</span> &amp; disrupting <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>all </i>non-covalent interactions</span> - leaving them in their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">primary</span> structures.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Reducing SDS-PAGE would also disrupt disulfide bonds, in addition to neutralizing the charge &amp; disrupting non-covalent interactions.</div><div><br></div><img src=""7fc7ae5640e98141ec7a88d8c2b31d45.png""></div>"
"<u>Non-reducing</u> <b>SDS-PAGE</b> separates proteins by molecular weight by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">neutralizing their charges</span> &amp; disrupting <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>all </i>non-covalent interactions</span> - leaving them in their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""primary"" data-ordinal=""2"">[1°/2°/3°/4°]</span> structures."	"<u>Non-reducing</u> <b>SDS-PAGE</b> separates proteins by molecular weight by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">neutralizing their charges</span> &amp; disrupting <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>all </i>non-covalent interactions</span> - leaving them in their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">primary</span> structures.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Reducing SDS-PAGE would also disrupt disulfide bonds, in addition to neutralizing the charge &amp; disrupting non-covalent interactions.</div><div><br></div><img src=""7fc7ae5640e98141ec7a88d8c2b31d45.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>quaternary</i></b></span> <b>structure </b>of a protein is primarily stabilized through <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""non&#x2D;covalent&#x20;interactions"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""disulfide&#x20;bonds"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>quaternary</i></b></span> <b>structure </b>of a protein is primarily stabilized through <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">non-covalent interactions</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">disulfide bonds</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Disulfide bonds <u>are not</u> the only interactions that hold multiple subunits together!<div><br></div><div><img src=""6a211f8baefc02ca84206384086b8a12.png""><br></div></div>"
"<u>Reducing</u> <b>SDS-PAGE</b> separates proteins by molecular weight by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""neutralizing&#x20;their&#x20;charges"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, disrupting <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;all&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;non&#x2D;covalent&#x20;interactions"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, and breaking <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""disulfide&#x20;bonds"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<u>Reducing</u> <b>SDS-PAGE</b> separates proteins by molecular weight by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">neutralizing their charges</span>, disrupting <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>all </i>non-covalent interactions</span>, and breaking <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">disulfide bonds</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Non-reducing SDS-PAGE does not disrupt disulfide bonds, despite still neutralizing the charge &amp; disrupting non-covalent interactions.</div><div><br></div><img src=""f4037287b602e5d0c47fa5081a85244a.png""><br></div>"
"<b>Proteins </b>exposed to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span> amounts of <u>salt</u> become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> stable due to a <i>greater </i>number of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>ionic interactions</u></span>."	"<b>Proteins </b>exposed to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> amounts of <u>salt</u> become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> stable due to a <i>greater </i>number of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>ionic interactions</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""7ec23cd9ca51efa6b07850e682bb2e49.png""></div>"
"<b>Proteins </b>exposed to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> amounts of <u>salt</u> become <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[less/more]</span> stable due to a <i>greater </i>number of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>ionic interactions</u></span>."	"<b>Proteins </b>exposed to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> amounts of <u>salt</u> become <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> stable due to a <i>greater </i>number of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>ionic interactions</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""7ec23cd9ca51efa6b07850e682bb2e49.png""></div>"
"<b>Proteins </b>exposed to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> amounts of <u>salt</u> become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> stable due to a <i>greater </i>number of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;ionic&#x20;interactions&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>."	"<b>Proteins </b>exposed to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> amounts of <u>salt</u> become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> stable due to a <i>greater </i>number of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>ionic interactions</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""7ec23cd9ca51efa6b07850e682bb2e49.png""></div>"
"<b>Proteins </b>exposed to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;high&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span> amounts of <u>salt</u> become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>less</i></span> stable due to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">charge neutralization</span> - denaturing them."	"<b>Proteins </b>exposed to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> amounts of <u>salt</u> become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>less</i></span> stable due to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">charge neutralization</span> - denaturing them.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""7ec23cd9ca51efa6b07850e682bb2e49.png""></div>"
"<b>Proteins </b>exposed to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> amounts of <u>salt</u> become <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;less&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[less/more]</span> stable due to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">charge neutralization</span> - denaturing them."	"<b>Proteins </b>exposed to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> amounts of <u>salt</u> become <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>less</i></span> stable due to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">charge neutralization</span> - denaturing them.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""7ec23cd9ca51efa6b07850e682bb2e49.png""></div>"
"<b>Proteins </b>exposed to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> amounts of <u>salt</u> become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>less</i></span> stable due to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""charge&#x20;neutralization"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> - denaturing them."	"<b>Proteins </b>exposed to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> amounts of <u>salt</u> become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>less</i></span> stable due to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">charge neutralization</span> - denaturing them.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>All contain either OH groups, NH<sub>3</sub> groups, or SH groups.</div><div><br></div><img src=""5991a4ddb8131a5a03d09e9e01655942.png""></div>"
"Proteins <i>transitioning</i> from an <b>α-helix</b> → <b>β-sheet</b> become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>aggregated</u></span> after having their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""hydrophobic&#x20;residues&#x20;&lt;i&gt;exposed&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> to water - forcing the residues to aggregate to hide from water."	"Proteins <i>transitioning</i> from an <b>α-helix</b> → <b>β-sheet</b> become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>aggregated</u></span> after having their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">hydrophobic residues <i>exposed</i></span> to water - forcing the residues to aggregate to hide from water.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>After the hydrophobic residues become more exposed to water, water forces them to hide away in order to increase its own entropy. This ""unplanned"" hydrophobic exposure results in aggregation.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ef47b156ca154656644aa0f2e5302393.png""></div>"
"Proteins <i>transitioning</i> from an <b>α-helix</b> → <b>β-sheet</b> become <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;aggregated&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> after having their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">hydrophobic residues <i>exposed</i></span> to water - forcing the residues to aggregate to hide from water."	"Proteins <i>transitioning</i> from an <b>α-helix</b> → <b>β-sheet</b> become <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>aggregated</u></span> after having their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">hydrophobic residues <i>exposed</i></span> to water - forcing the residues to aggregate to hide from water.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>After the hydrophobic residues become more exposed to water, water forces them to hide away in order to increase its own entropy. This ""unplanned"" hydrophobic exposure results in aggregation.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ef47b156ca154656644aa0f2e5302393.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;Anti&#x2D;parallel&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[parallel/anti-parallel]</span> <b>β-sheets</b> are linked together by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>short</i></span> sequences called <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">β-turns</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>Anti-parallel</u></span> <b>β-sheets</b> are linked together by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>short</i></span> sequences called <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">β-turns</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Parallel </b>β-sheets <u>are not</u> linked by β-turns, since β-turns are made primarily of short sequences (parallel linkages requre long 360° loops).</div><div><br></div><img src=""3f8bdb5edc7f973302bcd33b33bc7f52.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>Anti-parallel</u></span> <b>β-sheets</b> are linked together by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;short&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[long/short]</span> sequences called <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""β&#x2D;turns"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>Anti-parallel</u></span> <b>β-sheets</b> are linked together by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>short</i></span> sequences called <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">β-turns</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Parallel </b>β-sheets <u>are not</u> linked by β-turns, since β-turns are made primarily of short sequences (parallel linkages requre long 360° loops).</div><div><br></div><img src=""3f8bdb5edc7f973302bcd33b33bc7f52.png""></div>"
"<div>Are <i>both </i>parallel &amp; anti-parallel<b> β-sheets</b> capable of <u>hydrogen bonding</u> to their neighbor strands?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Yes&#x20;&#x2D;&#x20;both&#x20;parallel&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;anti&#x2D;parallel&#x20;β&#x2D;sheets&#x20;are&#x20;capable&#x20;of&#x20;hydrogen&#x20;bonding&#x20;&#x28;even&#x20;though&#x20;anti&#x2D;parallel&#x20;is&#x20;more&#x20;stable&#x29;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>Are <i>both </i>parallel &amp; anti-parallel<b> β-sheets</b> capable of <u>hydrogen bonding</u> to their neighbor strands?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Yes - both parallel &amp; anti-parallel β-sheets are capable of hydrogen bonding (even though anti-parallel is more stable).</span></div>
