<div>Neutral amino acids have only 2 equivalence points (shown below).</div><div><br></div><img src=""f4bc63314c71f1bd0940cd426064ca41.png""></div>"
"In enzyme kinetics, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;dissociation&#x20;constant&#x20;&#x28;K&lt;sub&gt;d&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> represents the <i>ease </i>at which an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ES complex</u></span> breaks into the separate <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>enzyme</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>substrate</u></span>."	"In enzyme kinetics, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dissociation constant (K<sub>d</sub>)</b></span> represents the <i>ease </i>at which an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ES complex</u></span> breaks into the separate <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>enzyme</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>substrate</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- High K<sub>d</sub> indicates a low binding affinity (easily breaks apart into substrate).<br></div><div>- Low K<sub>d</sub> indicates a high binding affinity (equilibrium has much more of the product than constituent substrates).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""68653b27070bc17b409aef43f54d8658.png""><br></div></div>"
"In enzyme kinetics, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dissociation constant (K<sub>d</sub>)</b></span> represents the <i>ease </i>at which an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;ES&#x20;complex&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> breaks into the separate <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;enzyme&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;substrate&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"In enzyme kinetics, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dissociation constant (K<sub>d</sub>)</b></span> represents the <i>ease </i>at which an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ES complex</u></span> breaks into the separate <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>enzyme</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>substrate</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- High K<sub>d</sub> indicates a low binding affinity (easily breaks apart into substrate).<br></div><div>- Low K<sub>d</sub> indicates a high binding affinity (equilibrium has much more of the product than constituent substrates).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""68653b27070bc17b409aef43f54d8658.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In enzyme kinetics, a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[low/high]</span><b> K</b><sub><b>d</b></sub> represents a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> <u>binding affinity</u>.<br></div>"	"<div>In enzyme kinetics, a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span><b> K</b><sub><b>d</b></sub> represents a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> <u>binding affinity</u>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- High K<sub>d</sub> indicates a low binding affinity (easily breaks apart into substrate).<br></div><div>- Low K<sub>d</sub> indicates a high binding affinity (equilibrium has much more of the product than constituent substrates).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""68653b27070bc17b409aef43f54d8658.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>α-helices</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""3&#x2E;6"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> <u>amino acid</u> residues <i>per turn</i>."	"<b>α-helices</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">3.6</span> <u>amino acid</u> residues <i>per turn</i>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""798cd8c08e2d81a7dae171dff159d078.png""></div>"
"In<b> α-helices</b>, hydrogen bonding <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;cannot&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[can/cannot]</span> happen between the <u>side chains</u> of its residues." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>R-groups stick outward to prevent steric hinderance. Hydrogen bonding <u>only</u> takes place between the <b>carbonyl groups</b> &amp; <b>amide groups</b> in its backbone.</div><div><br></div><img src=""798cd8c08e2d81a7dae171dff159d078.png""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Kinases can also catalyze the backwards reaction (<u>adding</u> a phosphate to ADP to form ATP).</div><div>- ATP is used instead of an organic phsophate, because the transfer of a phosphate onto a molecule is thermodynamically unfavorable. "In <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""base&#x20;excision&#x20;repair"" data-ordinal=""3"">[DNA repair]</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>DNA&nbsp;</b><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">glycosylase</span></span><i>&nbsp;</i><i>removes&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u style="""">damaged bases</u></span> by cleaving their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">N-glycosidic bonds</span> - <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>preserving</i></span> the phosphodiester backbone." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Makes the <u><i>abasic site</i></u></div><div>- <b>AP endonuclease</b> then finds this abasic site and cleaves the backbone.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""676786_633956_ans_5795966e84a64d6090beed0d8803df36.PNG""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""bba7f15574c11be3c688b8cbdb2b5950.png""></div>"
"<div>In the equation to calculate the percent yield of <u>proteins</u> in an <i>unpurified</i> mixture, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;total&#x20;activity&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>&nbsp;is calculated by <i>multiplying&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>total protein amount (mg)</u></span> x <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>specific activity (u/mg)</u></span>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""821175a2892b03c00551b770f6c744d2.png""><br></div>"	"<div>In the equation to calculate the percent yield of <u>proteins</u> in an <i>unpurified</i> mixture, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>total activity</b></span>&nbsp;is calculated by <i>multiplying&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>total protein amount (mg)</u></span> x <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>specific activity (u/mg)</u></span>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""821175a2892b03c00551b770f6c744d2.png""><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Using information below...</div><div><br></div><div>- Total activity of purified protein = (200 mg) x (1) = 200&nbsp;</div><div>- Total activity of unpurified protein = (800 mg) x (0.5) = 400</div><div>- Percent yield = (purified TA)/(unpurified TA) * 100 = 50%.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""bfe46cec46df0e70a50f2fef54fb0c84.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""dd728729ff6b3c43d41b1bc273993082.png""></div>"

"<div>In the ETC, do these electron carriers <u>transfer their electron(s)</u>&nbsp;<i>simultaneously</i> or one at a time?<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NADH</b></span>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""both&#x20;at&#x20;once"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>FADH<sub>2</sub></b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""one&#x20;at&#x20;a&#x20;time"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Ubiquinone</b></span>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""one&#x20;at&#x20;a&#x20;time"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Cytochrome</b> <b>C</b></span>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""one&#x20;&#x28;only&#x20;has&#x20;1&#x20;electron&#x20;anyway&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div>In the ETC, do these electron carriers <u>transfer their electron(s)</u>&nbsp;<i>simultaneously</i> or one at a time?<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NADH</b></span>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">both at once</span>.</div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>FADH<sub>2</sub></b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">one at a time</span>.</div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Ubiquinone</b></span>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">one at a time</span>.</div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Cytochrome</b> <b>C</b></span>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">one (only has 1 electron anyway)</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>NADH is the only carrier that transfers both simultaneously.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d89236841ca1ca6f1983c8e57f417c5143a82bcc.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In the <b>ETC</b>, <u>redox potential</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increases/decreases]</span> the further along you go towards complex 4."	"In the <b>ETC</b>, <u>redox potential</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increases</i></span> the further along you go towards complex 4.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Reduction potential refers to the ability for a compound to become reduced (i.e. accept electrons). If complex 1 had a higher redox potential than complex 2, electrons would never be passed along.</div><div>- Complex 1 has a lower reduction potential than complex 2, which is lower than complex 3, etc.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-a9761b4f1c37be92f18fe013a97ea88d36fd20da.jpg""></div>"
"The <i>only</i> two <u>chiral</u> intermediates in the <b>citric acid cycle</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""L"" data-ordinal=""1"">[D/L]</span>-<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""isocitrate"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""L"" data-ordinal=""1"">[D/L]</span>-<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""malate"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <i>only</i> two <u>chiral</u> intermediates in the <b>citric acid cycle</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">L</span>-<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">isocitrate</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">L</span>-<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">malate</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Acopnitase</b> &amp; <b>fumarase</b> are therefore the only enzymes with <u>stereospecificity</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ce8c0fb8a36ff0206cfdd6ddfd3b41ee637de15f.jpg""></div>"
"<div>The <i>only</i> enzymes in the <b>citric acid cycle</b> with <u>stereospecificity</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""aconitase"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""fumarase"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span>.</div>"	"<div>The <i>only</i> enzymes in the <b>citric acid cycle</b> with <u>stereospecificity</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">aconitase</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">fumarase</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because <b>malate</b> &amp; <b>isocitrate</b> are the only <u>chiral</u> intermediates in the TCA. The enzymes that form them must be stereospecific (only produce one configuration).</div><div>- Aconitase only produces L-isocitrate.</div><div>- Fumarase only produces L-malate.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ce8c0fb8a36ff0206cfdd6ddfd3b41ee637de15f.jpg""></div>"
"In the pentose phosphate pathway, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>ribose-5-P</b></span> can be formed from the <i>fusion</i> of 2 glycolytic intermediates: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;fructose&#x2D;6&#x2D;P&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;GA3P&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"In the pentose phosphate pathway, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>ribose-5-P</b></span> can be formed from the <i>fusion</i> of 2 glycolytic intermediates: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>fructose-6-P</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>GA3P</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Fuse together &amp; re-arrange their functional groups.</div><div>- This ""fusion"" is mediated by <b>transaldolase</b> &amp; <b>transketolase</b>.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-e00c4dcfe3e602b130c7766d354136ee9c7f95ce.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""bf0c5c2624e66061d6d1769734fc4b3e.png""><br></div></div>"
"In the pentose phosphate pathway, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ribose&#x2D;5&#x2D;P&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[intermediate]</span> can be formed from the <i>fusion</i> of 2 glycolytic intermediates: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>fructose-6-P</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>GA3P</u></span>."	"In the pentose phosphate pathway, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>ribose-5-P</b></span> can be formed from the <i>fusion</i> of 2 glycolytic intermediates: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>fructose-6-P</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>GA3P</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Fuse together &amp; re-arrange their functional groups.</div><div>- This ""fusion"" is mediated by <b>transaldolase</b> &amp; <b>transketolase</b>.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-e00c4dcfe3e602b130c7766d354136ee9c7f95ce.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""bf0c5c2624e66061d6d1769734fc4b3e.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Phosphoglucomutase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> <i>catalyzes</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>glucose-1-P</u></span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>glucose-6-P</u></span> in glycogenolysis."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Phosphoglucomutase</b></span> <i>catalyzes</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>glucose-1-P</u></span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>glucose-6-P</u></span> in glycogenolysis.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Glucose-6-P then turns into glucose via the action of <b>glucose-6-phosphatase</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""b46d8ef279fd93e1c4cc7429b04f2ef6.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d6a29cfad512c3c063474ec2d239ba0276e816e9.jpg""></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Phosphoglucomutase</b></span> <i>catalyzes</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;glucose&#x2D;1&#x2D;P&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;glucose&#x2D;6&#x2D;P&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in glycogenolysis."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Phosphoglucomutase</b></span> <i>catalyzes</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>glucose-1-P</u></span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>glucose-6-P</u></span> in glycogenolysis.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Glucose-6-P then turns into glucose via the action of <b>glucose-6-phosphatase</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""b46d8ef279fd93e1c4cc7429b04f2ef6.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d6a29cfad512c3c063474ec2d239ba0276e816e9.jpg""></div></div>"
"The <i><u>decarboxylation</u></i> of <b>malonyl-CoA </b>takes place in fatty acid <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""synthesis"" data-ordinal=""1"">[oxidation/synthesis]</span>."	"The <i><u>decarboxylation</u></i> of <b>malonyl-CoA </b>takes place in fatty acid <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">synthesis</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Acetyl-CoA carboxylase catalyzes acetyl-CoA → malonyl-CoA, <u>even though</u> malonyl-CoA loses that CO<sub>2</sub> group from fatty acid synthase anyway.</div><div><br></div><img src=""2689ca144f6c84e4dd9d0292600024d2.png""></div>"
"Insulin <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;stimulates&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[inhibits/stimulates]</span> <b>fatty acid synthase</b> in the liver."	"Insulin <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>stimulates</i></span> <b>fatty acid synthase</b> in the liver.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<b>Facilitated diffusion</b> is a form of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""passive"" data-ordinal=""1"">[active/passive]</span> transport, where molecules flow <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;down&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[down/against]</span> their concentration gradients."	"<b>Facilitated diffusion</b> is a form of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">passive</span> transport, where molecules flow <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>down</i></span> their concentration gradients.

"On a Lineweaver-Burke plot, a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>down</u></span><u>ward</u> shift on the y-axis&nbsp;indicates a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increase</i></span> in <b>V<sub>max</sub></b>.

"On a Lineweaver-Burke plot, a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>right</u></span><u>ward</u> shift on the x-axis&nbsp;indicates a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increase</i></span> in <b>K<sub>m</sub></b>.

<div>...because the x-intercept is 1/K<sub>m</sub>.</div><div>- When the x-intercept gets smaller, K<sub>m</sub> gets bigger (it's the reciprocal).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d9cdf60b2aea4b41f51b8e71476f05981c98e590.jpg""></div>"

"On a Lineweaver-Burke plot, a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;left&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><u>ward</u> shift on the x-axis&nbsp;indicates a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decrease</i></span> in <b>K<sub>m</sub></b>."	"On a Lineweaver-Burke plot, a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>left</u></span><u>ward</u> shift on the x-axis&nbsp;indicates a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decrease</i></span> in <b>K<sub>m</sub></b>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because the x-intercept is 1/K<sub>m</sub>.</div><div>- When the <u>absolute value</u> of the x-intercept gets bigger, K<sub>m</sub> gets smaller (it's the reciprocal).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d9cdf60b2aea4b41f51b8e71476f05981c98e590.jpg""></div>"
"On a Lineweaver-Burke plot, a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>left</u></span><u>ward</u> shift on the x-axis&nbsp;indicates a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decrease&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[increase/decrease]</span> in <b>K<sub>m</sub></b>."	"On a Lineweaver-Burke plot, a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>left</u></span><u>ward</u> shift on the x-axis&nbsp;indicates a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decrease</i></span> in <b>K<sub>m</sub></b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because the x-intercept is 1/K<sub>m</sub>.</div><div>- When the <u>absolute value</u> of the x-intercept gets bigger, K<sub>m</sub> gets smaller (it's the reciprocal).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d9cdf60b2aea4b41f51b8e71476f05981c98e590.jpg""></div>"
"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;catalytic&#x20;efficiency&#x20;&#x28;k&lt;sub&gt;cat&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x2F;K&lt;sub&gt;m&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;is represented as the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>inverse</i> of the slope</u></span>."	"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>catalytic efficiency (k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>)</b></span>&nbsp;is represented as the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>inverse</i> of the slope</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This shows the high pressure slope is smaller than the atmospheric slope, meaning k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub> is higher (greater catalytic efficiency).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d9cdf60b2aea4b41f51b8e71476f05981c98e590.jpg""></div>"
"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>catalytic efficiency (k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>)</b></span>&nbsp;is represented as the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;inverse&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;slope&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>catalytic efficiency (k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>)</b></span>&nbsp;is represented as the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>inverse</i> of the slope</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This shows the high pressure slope is smaller than the atmospheric slope, meaning k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub> is higher (greater catalytic efficiency).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d9cdf60b2aea4b41f51b8e71476f05981c98e590.jpg""></div>"
"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, a <u><i>steep</i> slope</u> indicates a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""low"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span> <b>catalytic efficiency (k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>)</b>."	"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, a <u><i>steep</i> slope</u> indicates a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">low</span> <b>catalytic efficiency (k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>)</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Catalytic efficiency (k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>)</b>&nbsp;is the&nbsp;<u>inverse of the slope</u>, since the slope is K<sub>m</sub>/V<sub>max</sub>.</div><div>- This shows the high pressure slope is smaller than the atmospheric slope, meaning k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>&nbsp;is higher (greater catalytic efficiency).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d9cdf60b2aea4b41f51b8e71476f05981c98e590.jpg""></div>"
"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, a <i>flattened</i>&nbsp;slope indicates a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;high&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>catalytic efficiency (k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>)</b></span>."	"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, a <i>flattened</i>&nbsp;slope indicates a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>catalytic efficiency (k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>)</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Catalytic efficiency (k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>)</b> is the <u>inverse of the slope</u>.</div><div>- This shows the high pressure slope is smaller than the atmospheric slope, meaning k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub> is higher (greater catalytic efficiency).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d9cdf60b2aea4b41f51b8e71476f05981c98e590.jpg""></div>"
"Due to the assumptions made in Michaelis-Menten kinetics, the <b>reaction's velocity (V<sub>0</sub>)</b>&nbsp;is <i>only</i> limited by the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""enzyme"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> concentration."	"Due to the assumptions made in Michaelis-Menten kinetics, the <b>reaction's velocity (V<sub>0</sub>)</b>&nbsp;is <i>only</i> limited by the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">enzyme</span> concentration.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Substrate concentration will never be the limiting factor (due to the assumption that substrate concentration will always be at a maximum).</div><div><br></div><img src=""96427da5d8d6f471eb7f67adb66c6c05.png""></div>"
"In enzyme kinetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;first&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b>-order</b> reactions mean the enzyme <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>does</i></span> depend on <u>substrate concentrations</u>."	"In enzyme kinetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>first</b></span><b>-order</b> reactions mean the enzyme <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>does</i></span> depend on <u>substrate concentrations</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>GLUT-4</b> (which depends only on insulin, not the substrate glucose concentrations) is zero order.</div><div>- <b>GLUT-2</b> (which depends on blood-glucose concentrations) is first order.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d3e81acb431d9425288546309dff77468ffbce9c.jpg""></div>"
"In enzyme kinetics, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>first</b></span><b>-order</b> reactions mean the enzyme <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[does/does not]</span> depend on <u>substrate concentrations</u>."	"In enzyme kinetics, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>first</b></span><b>-order</b> reactions mean the enzyme <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>does</i></span> depend on <u>substrate concentrations</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>GLUT-4</b> (which depends only on insulin, not the substrate glucose concentrations) is zero order.</div><div>- <b>GLUT-2</b> (which depends on blood-glucose concentrations) is first order.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d3e81acb431d9425288546309dff77468ffbce9c.jpg""></div>"
"In enzyme kinetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;zero&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b>-order</b> reactions mean the enzyme <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>does not</i></span> depend on <u>substrate concentrations</u>."	"In enzyme kinetics, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>zero</b></span><b>-order</b> reactions mean the enzyme <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>does not</i></span> depend on <u>substrate concentrations</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>GLUT-4</b> (which depends only on insulin, not the substrate glucose concentrations) is zero order.</div><div>- <b>GLUT-2</b> (which depends on blood-glucose concentrations) is first order.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d3e81acb431d9425288546309dff77468ffbce9c.jpg""></div>"
"In enzyme kinetics, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>zero</b></span><b>-order</b> reactions mean the enzyme <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[does/does not]</span> depend on <u>substrate concentrations</u>."	"In enzyme kinetics, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>zero</b></span><b>-order</b> reactions mean the enzyme <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>does not</i></span> depend on <u>substrate concentrations</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>GLUT-4</b> (which depends only on insulin, not the substrate glucose concentrations) is zero order.</div><div>- <b>GLUT-2</b> (which depends on blood-glucose concentrations) is first order.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d3e81acb431d9425288546309dff77468ffbce9c.jpg""></div>"
"Glucose uptake through&nbsp;<b>GLUT-4</b> receptors is a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""zero"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-order reaction since its rate <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> depend on glucose concentrations."	"Glucose uptake through&nbsp;<b>GLUT-4</b> receptors is a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">zero</span>-order reaction since its rate <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>does not</i></span> depend on glucose concentrations.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>GLUT-4</b> (which depends only on insulin, not the substrate glucose concentrations) is zero order.</div><div>- <b>GLUT-2</b> (which depends on blood-glucose concentrations) is first order.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d3e81acb431d9425288546309dff77468ffbce9c.jpg""></div>"
"Glucose uptake through&nbsp;<b>GLUT-2</b> receptors is a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""first"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-order reaction since its rate <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> depend on glucose concentrations."	"Glucose uptake through&nbsp;<b>GLUT-2</b> receptors is a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">first</span>-order reaction since its rate <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>does</i></span> depend on glucose concentrations.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>GLUT-4</b> (which depends only on insulin, not the substrate glucose concentrations) is zero order.</div><div>- <b>GLUT-2</b> (which depends on blood-glucose concentrations) is first order.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d3e81acb431d9425288546309dff77468ffbce9c.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Uncompetitive</b></span> <b>inhibitors</b> must always <i>decrease</i>&nbsp;both V<sub>max</sub> &amp; K<sub>m</sub> by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""the&#x20;&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;same&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;factor&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Uncompetitive</b></span> <b>inhibitors</b> must always <i>decrease</i>&nbsp;both V<sub>max</sub> &amp; K<sub>m</sub> by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">the <u><i>same</i> factor</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because decreasing both by the same factor keeps the K<sub>m</sub>/V<sub>max</sub>&nbsp;ratio the same (reflected as the slopes being the exact same).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""500px-UNCOMPETITIVE.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-08cb692bad3a0748cae4a9eefd5227f5e4eb0aef.jpg""></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Uncompetitive&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <b>inhibitors</b> must always <i>decrease</i>&nbsp;both V<sub>max</sub> &amp; K<sub>m</sub> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">the <u><i>same</i> factor</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Uncompetitive</b></span> <b>inhibitors</b> must always <i>decrease</i>&nbsp;both V<sub>max</sub> &amp; K<sub>m</sub> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">the <u><i>same</i> factor</u></span>.

"Equation to determine an enzyme's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>reaction velocity (V<sub>0</sub>)</b></span>&nbsp;at a point in time using <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">[S]</span>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""V&lt;sub&gt;0&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;n&#x2F;&#x28;n&#x2B;1&#x29;V&lt;sub&gt;max&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Equation to determine an enzyme's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>reaction velocity (V<sub>0</sub>)</b></span>&nbsp;at a point in time using <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">[S]</span>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">V<sub>0</sub> = n/(n+1)V<sub>max</sub></span></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Specific activity refers to the protein's activity in a purified mixture.</div><div>- This can then be used to calculate the % yield of the protien (below).</div><div><br></div><img src=""821175a2892b03c00551b770f6c744d2.png""><div><br></div><div><img src=""dd728729ff6b3c43d41b1bc273993082.png""><br></div></div>"
"3 assumptions made by the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Michaelis&#x2D;Menten&#x20;equation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> that <i>only</i> hold true during the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;reaction&#x27;s&#x20;initial&#x20;rate&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>:<div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Substrate concentration [S]</span> always remains <u>constant</u>.</div><div>2. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Number of ES complexes</span> always remain <u>constant</u>.</div><div>3. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Reaction only proceeds in the <u><i>forward</i>&nbsp;direction</u></span>.</div>"	"3 assumptions made by the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Michaelis-Menten equation</b></span> that <i>only</i> hold true during the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>reaction's initial rate</u></span>:<div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Substrate concentration [S]</span> always remains <u>constant</u>.</div><div>2. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Number of ES complexes</span> always remain <u>constant</u>.</div><div>3. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Reaction only proceeds in the <u><i>forward</i>&nbsp;direction</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These assumptions are only true during the <b>initial reaction</b>, so only the initial reaction should be paid attention to when constructing a Michaelis-Menten plot.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-18b04c767fff644e6b013c9f616b9ccf6e523e7f.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-fe4679ca6056eabe6eeb77cfbd12a043a4073124.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"3 assumptions made by the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Michaelis-Menten equation</b></span> that <i>only</i> hold true during the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>reaction's initial rate</u></span>:<div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Substrate&#x20;concentration&#x20;&#x5B;S&#x5D;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> always remains <u>constant</u>.</div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Number&#x20;of&#x20;ES&#x20;complexes"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> always remain <u>constant</u>.</div><div>3. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Reaction&#x20;only&#x20;proceeds&#x20;in&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;forward&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;direction&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"3 assumptions made by the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Michaelis-Menten equation</b></span> that <i>only</i> hold true during the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>reaction's initial rate</u></span>:<div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Substrate concentration [S]</span> always remains <u>constant</u>.</div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Number of ES complexes</span> always remain <u>constant</u>.</div><div>3. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Reaction only proceeds in the <u><i>forward</i>&nbsp;direction</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These assumptions are only true during the <b>initial reaction</b>, so only the initial reaction should be paid attention to when constructing a Michaelis-Menten plot.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-18b04c767fff644e6b013c9f616b9ccf6e523e7f.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-fe4679ca6056eabe6eeb77cfbd12a043a4073124.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Experimenters <i>only</i>&nbsp;measure the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""initial&#x20;reaction&#x20;rate"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> when constructing a <b>Michaelis-Menten plot</b>, because that's the <i>only</i> point in the reaction where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>assumptions</u> remain <i>true</i></span>."	"Experimenters <i>only</i>&nbsp;measure the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">initial reaction rate</span> when constructing a <b>Michaelis-Menten plot</b>, because that's the <i>only</i> point in the reaction where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>assumptions</u> remain <i>true</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Assumptions made...</div><div>1. [S] always remains constant.</div><div>2. [ES] always remains constant.</div><div>3. Reaction only proceeds in the forward direction.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-18b04c767fff644e6b013c9f616b9ccf6e523e7f.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-fe4679ca6056eabe6eeb77cfbd12a043a4073124.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Lyases</b></span> either <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;break&#x20;bonds&#x20;apart&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;join&#x20;bonds&#x20;together&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <i>without</i> the use of water - either forming or breaking a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">new double bond</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Lyases</b></span> either <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>break bonds apart</u></span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>join bonds together</u></span> <i>without</i> the use of water - either forming or breaking a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">new double bond</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Think of ""lysing"" its product (i.e. breaking it down).<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-91d1b4931cdf36b986bf3393d4fa729b861f7ba5.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Lyases</b></span> either <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>break bonds apart</u></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>join bonds together</u></span> <i>without</i> the use of water - either forming or breaking a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""new&#x20;double&#x20;bond"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Lyases</b></span> either <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>break bonds apart</u></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>join bonds together</u></span> <i>without</i> the use of water - either forming or breaking a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">new double bond</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Think of ""lysing"" its product (i.e. breaking it down).<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-91d1b4931cdf36b986bf3393d4fa729b861f7ba5.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;K&lt;sub&gt;m&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x2F;V&lt;sub&gt;max&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;is represented as the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>slope</u></span>."	"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>K<sub>m</sub>/V<sub>max</sub></b></span>&nbsp;is represented as the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>slope</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- In contrast, the <u>inverse</u> of the slope represents <b>catalytic efficiency</b> <b>(k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>)</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-9ce75f5b74775590c19136f1ee462dc2c1f1d216.jpg""></div>"
"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>K<sub>m</sub>/V<sub>max</sub></b></span>&nbsp;is represented as the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;slope&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>K<sub>m</sub>/V<sub>max</sub></b></span>&nbsp;is represented as the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>slope</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- In contrast, the <u>inverse</u> of the slope represents <b>catalytic efficiency</b> <b>(k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>)</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-9ce75f5b74775590c19136f1ee462dc2c1f1d216.jpg""></div>"
"An enzyme's <b>dissociation constant (K<sub>d</sub>)</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> represent equilibrium concentrations - <i>only</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;binding&#x20;affinity&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"An enzyme's <b>dissociation constant (K<sub>d</sub>)</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>does not</i></span> represent equilibrium concentrations - <i>only</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>binding affinity</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- High K<sub>d</sub>&nbsp;indicates a low binding affinity (easily breaks apart into substrate).<br></div><div>- Low K<sub>d</sub>&nbsp;indicates a high binding affinity (equilibrium has much more of the product than constituent substrates).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""68653b27070bc17b409aef43f54d8658.png""></div></div>"
"<div>Does an enzyme's <b>dissociation constant (K<sub>d</sub>)</b> say anything about enzyme-substrate equilibrium ratios?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""No&#x20;&#x2D;&#x20;K&lt;sub&gt;d&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;only&#x20;describes&#x20;the&#x20;ease&#x20;at&#x20;which&#x20;the&#x20;enzyme&#x20;breaks&#x20;into&#x20;E&#x20;&#x2B;&#x20;S&#x2E;&#x20;This&#x20;does&#x20;not&#x20;alter&#x20;the&#x20;final&#x20;concentrations&#x20;at&#x20;equilibrium&#x20;&#x2D;&#x20;only&#x20;the&#x20;ease&#x20;at&#x20;which&#x20;the&#x20;break&#x20;apart&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>Does an enzyme's <b>dissociation constant (K<sub>d</sub>)</b> say anything about enzyme-substrate equilibrium ratios?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">No - K<sub>d</sub> only describes the ease at which the enzyme breaks into E + S. This does not alter the final concentrations at equilibrium - only the ease at which the break apart.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- High K<sub>d</sub>&nbsp;indicates a low binding affinity (easily breaks apart into substrate).<br></div><div>- Low K<sub>d</sub>&nbsp;indicates a high binding affinity (equilibrium has much more of the product than constituent substrates).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""68653b27070bc17b409aef43f54d8658.png""></div></div>"
"<div><b>Enzymes</b> forming <u>non-covalent interactions</u> <i>after</i> undergoing a conformational change due to substrate-binding is an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""exer"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>gonic process.<br></div>"	"<div><b>Enzymes</b> forming <u>non-covalent interactions</u> <i>after</i> undergoing a conformational change due to substrate-binding is an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">exer</span>gonic process.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>In order to <b>bind</b> the substrate first though, they need to <u>absorb</u> energy (endergonic).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9c1b5a7b749eed947c431b5dfc9228f9.png""></div></div>"
"<div>Even though proteins become more <i>ordered</i> as they fold, folding occurs <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;spontaneously&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[spont./non-spont.]</span> due to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;water&#x27;s&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;increase&#x20;in&#x20;entropy"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> surrounding the protein.</div>"	"<div>Even though proteins become more <i>ordered</i> as they fold, folding occurs <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>spontaneously</i></span> due to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>water's</u> increase in entropy</span> surrounding the protein.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Water experiences a large enough increase in entropy to where the (otherwise unfavorable) folding can occur.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-ee59824d78dd90495ef211217d3ff8dc4764d62a.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Transcription factors</b> are <i>translated</i> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""cytoplasm"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> but wait in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""nucleus"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> until they're needed to regulate gene transcription."	"<b>Transcription factors</b> are <i>translated</i> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">cytoplasm</span> but wait in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">nucleus</span> until they're needed to regulate gene transcription.

"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Short"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>-chain &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""medium"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>-chain&nbsp;fatty acids&nbsp;enter the <u>mitochondria</u> by passing <i>directly</i> through <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>mitochondrial pores</b></span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Short</span>-chain &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">medium</span>-chain&nbsp;fatty acids&nbsp;enter the <u>mitochondria</u> by passing <i>directly</i> through <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>mitochondrial pores</b></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Long-chain fatty acids require the transport system (fatty acyl-CoA synthetase &amp; carnitine acyltransferase-1).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-c943be046a9353bca7895cd684b4ef710bd8e2f7.jpg""></div>"
"Experimenters <i>only</i>&nbsp;measure the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">initial reaction rate</span> when constructing a <b>Michaelis-Menten plot</b>, because that's the <i>only</i> point in the reaction where the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;assumptions&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;remain&#x20;&lt;i&gt;true&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"Experimenters <i>only</i>&nbsp;measure the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">initial reaction rate</span> when constructing a <b>Michaelis-Menten plot</b>, because that's the <i>only</i> point in the reaction where the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>assumptions</u> remain <i>true</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Assumptions made...</div><div>1. [S] always remains constant.</div><div>2. [ES] always remains constant.</div><div>3. Reaction only proceeds in the forward direction.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-18b04c767fff644e6b013c9f616b9ccf6e523e7f.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-fe4679ca6056eabe6eeb77cfbd12a043a4073124.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In the ribosome, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>A</b></span> <b>site </b>is where the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""aminoacyl&#x2D;tRNA"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> complex is held and where incoming <u>tRNA molecules <i>enter</i></u>."	"In the ribosome, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>A</b></span> <b>site </b>is where the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">aminoacyl-tRNA</span> complex is held and where incoming <u>tRNA molecules <i>enter</i></u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""c1f34b4ad2542417613bf62a95256437.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">V<sub>max</sub></span>&nbsp;= <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""k&lt;sub&gt;cat&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x5B;E&#x5D;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">V<sub>max</sub></span>&nbsp;= <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">k<sub>cat</sub>[E]</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>k<sub>cat</sub></b> <u>does not</u> change with changes in [E].</div><div><br></div><img src=""96427da5d8d6f471eb7f67adb66c6c05.png""></div>"
"<b>Ion channels</b> that transport <u>cations</u> (like Ca<sup>2+</sup> or Na<sup>+</sup>) use <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;negatively&#x20;charged&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-/neutral]</span> amino acids in their transmembrane domains."	"<b>Ion channels</b> that transport <u>cations</u> (like Ca<sup>2+</sup> or Na<sup>+</sup>) use <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>negatively charged</i></span> amino acids in their transmembrane domains.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Negatively charged</b> amino acids have <u>strong interactions</u> with the cations - unlike regular polar amino acids.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-42f1b0458369ef1bb15d63f1ae733c75245b1963.jpg""></div>"
"<b>Ion channels</b> that transport <u>anions</u> (like Cl<sup>-</sup>) use <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;positively&#x20;charged&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-/neutral]</span> amino acids in their transmembrane domains."	"<b>Ion channels</b> that transport <u>anions</u> (like Cl<sup>-</sup>) use <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>positively charged</i></span> amino acids in their transmembrane domains.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Positively charged</b> amino acids have <u>strong interactions</u> with the anions - unlike regular polar amino acids.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-42f1b0458369ef1bb15d63f1ae733c75245b1963.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Once <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>cholecalciferol</u></span> is produced in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">skin</span>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">liver</span> converts it into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;calcifediol&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> before the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">kidneys</span> convert it into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;calcitrol&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> - the <i>activated</i> form of <b>vitamin</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;D&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div>Once <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>cholecalciferol</u></span> is produced in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">skin</span>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">liver</span> converts it into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>calcifediol</u></span> before the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">kidneys</span> convert it into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>calcitrol</b></span> - the <i>activated</i> form of <b>vitamin</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>D</b></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Derived from the cholesterol in our skin.<div>- Vitamin D enhances Ca<sup>2+</sup> absorption from the diet (later deposited into the bone)</div></div>"
"<div>Once <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>cholecalciferol</u></span> is produced in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""skin"" data-ordinal=""4"">[organ]</span>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""liver"" data-ordinal=""4"">[organ]</span> converts it into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>calcifediol</u></span> before the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""kidneys"" data-ordinal=""4"">[organ]</span> convert it into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>calcitrol</b></span> - the <i>activated</i> form of <b>vitamin</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>D</b></span>.</div>"	"<div>Once <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>cholecalciferol</u></span> is produced in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4"">skin</span>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4"">liver</span> converts it into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>calcifediol</u></span> before the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4"">kidneys</span> convert it into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>calcitrol</b></span> - the <i>activated</i> form of <b>vitamin</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>D</b></span>.</div>

"<b>DNA</b> <b>methylation</b> results in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decreased&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increased/decreased]</span> <u>gene expression</u>, since it condenses the gene into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""hetero"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>chromatin." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Methyl groups added to adenine &amp; cytosine residues.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-3395413530374ac027ef8218b2abc1ddaf7fdfbc.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Acetylation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[acetyl./methyl.]</span> is a modification done to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>histone proteins</u></span> to alter gene expression, whereas <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;methylation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[acetyl./methyl.]</span> is mainly a modification done to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>DNA</u> <i>directly</i></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Acetylation</b></span> is a modification done to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>histone proteins</u></span> to alter gene expression, whereas <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>methylation</b></span> is mainly a modification done to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>DNA</u> <i>directly</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Acetylation</b>: increases gene expression.</div><div>- <b>Methylation</b>: decreases gene expression.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>- Methylation can sometimes be done directly to histone proteins (like in the case of gene imprinting), but it's mostly done directly to DNA.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a9fcecfb00e1c67cbb62cdd437294096.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-3395413530374ac027ef8218b2abc1ddaf7fdfbc.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Acetylation</b></span> is a modification done to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;histone&#x20;proteins&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to alter gene expression, whereas <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>methylation</b></span> is mainly a modification done to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;DNA&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&lt;i&gt;directly&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This is irreversible.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-498422883f6cb7175d59c7f77751d024e6d2736e.jpg""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""non&#x2D;oxidative"" data-ordinal=""1"">[oxidative/non-oxidative]</span> portion of the <b>pentose phosphate pathway</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>reversibly</i></span> catalyzes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>ribulose-5-P</u></span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>fructose-6-P</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>GA3P</u></span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">non-oxidative</span> portion of the <b>pentose phosphate pathway</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>reversibly</i></span> catalyzes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>ribulose-5-P</u></span> ⇌ <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>fructose-6-P</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>GA3P</u></span>.

<div>- Only the <b>liver</b> has <u>glucose-6-phosphatase</u>, so only the liver can regulate blood-glucose levels with its glycogen stores.</div><div><br></div><img src=""b46d8ef279fd93e1c4cc7429b04f2ef6.png""></div>"
"<b>Glycogenolysis</b> in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>skeletal muscle</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>does not</i></span> result in increased blood glucose levels - it instead <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""feeds&#x20;G&#x2D;6&#x2D;P&#x20;&lt;i&gt;directly&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;into&#x20;glycolysis"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>."	"<b>Glycogenolysis</b> in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>skeletal muscle</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>does not</i></span> result in increased blood glucose levels - it instead <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">feeds G-6-P <i>directly</i> into glycolysis</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Only the <b>liver</b> has <u>glucose-6-phosphatase</u>, so only the liver can regulate blood-glucose levels with its glycogen stores.</div><div><br></div><img src=""b46d8ef279fd93e1c4cc7429b04f2ef6.png""></div>"
"Only <b>glycogenolysis</b> in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""the&#x20;&lt;u&gt;liver&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[liver/muscle]</span> is able to regulate blood-glucose levels, since it's the <i>only</i> organ that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">contains glucose-6-phosphatase</span>."	"Only <b>glycogenolysis</b> in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">the <u>liver</u></span> is able to regulate blood-glucose levels, since it's the <i>only</i> organ that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">contains glucose-6-phosphatase</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Skeletal muscle lacks glucose-6-phosphatase, since it only needs to break it down to the point of G-6-P in order to feed it directly into glycolysis.</div><div>- Only the <b>liver</b> has <u>glucose-6-phosphatase</u>, so only the liver can regulate blood-glucose levels with its glycogen stores.</div><div><br></div><img src=""b46d8ef279fd93e1c4cc7429b04f2ef6.png""></div>"
"Only <b>glycogenolysis</b> in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">the <u>liver</u></span> is able to regulate blood-glucose levels, since it's the <i>only</i> organ that <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""contains&#x20;glucose&#x2D;6&#x2D;phosphatase"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"Only <b>glycogenolysis</b> in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">the <u>liver</u></span> is able to regulate blood-glucose levels, since it's the <i>only</i> organ that <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">contains glucose-6-phosphatase</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Skeletal muscle lacks glucose-6-phosphatase, since it only needs to break it down to the point of G-6-P in order to feed it directly into glycolysis.</div><div>- Only the <b>liver</b> has <u>glucose-6-phosphatase</u>, so only the liver can regulate blood-glucose levels with its glycogen stores.</div><div><br></div><img src=""b46d8ef279fd93e1c4cc7429b04f2ef6.png""></div>"

"Shortly after eating a meal high in carbohydrates, the <i>primary</i> metabolic process that occurs to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">balance <u>blood-glucose levels</u></span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;glycogenesis&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[pathway]</span>." "<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Lineweaver-Burke plot</b></span> equation:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;paste&#x2D;92678e4a0eb9bce196cab70c0729b1ebfbab7e34&#x2E;jpg&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Lineweaver-Burke plot</b></span> equation:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-92678e4a0eb9bce196cab70c0729b1ebfbab7e34.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Since Lineweaver-Burke plots represent reciprocal values of Vmax and Km, the Lineweaver-Burke plot equation is just a <u>reciprocal of the Michaelis-Menten equation</u>.</div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Lineweaver&#x2D;Burke&#x20;plot&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> equation:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-92678e4a0eb9bce196cab70c0729b1ebfbab7e34.jpg""></span><br>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Lineweaver-Burke plot</b></span> equation:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""paste-92678e4a0eb9bce196cab70c0729b1ebfbab7e34.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Since Lineweaver-Burke plots represent reciprocal values of Vmax and Km, the Lineweaver-Burke plot equation is just a <u>reciprocal of the Michaelis-Menten equation</u>.</div></div>"
"<div><b>Ketone bodies</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>cannot</i></span> be utilized for energy in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;liver&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[organ]</span>, since this organ <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>lacks</i> thiophorase</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><b>Ketone bodies</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>cannot</i></span> be utilized for energy in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>liver</u></span>, since this organ <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>lacks</i> thiophorase</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Thiophorase</b> is the enzyme that catalyzes <u>acetoacetate</u> → <u>2 acetyl-CoA</u>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""ec586dcdb5253de680d1d2a395a70af5.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><b>Ketone bodies</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>cannot</i></span> be utilized for energy in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>liver</u></span>, since this organ <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lacks&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;thiophorase"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><b>Ketone bodies</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>cannot</i></span> be utilized for energy in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>liver</u></span>, since this organ <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>lacks</i> thiophorase</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Thiophorase</b> is the enzyme that catalyzes <u>acetoacetate</u> → <u>2 acetyl-CoA</u>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""ec586dcdb5253de680d1d2a395a70af5.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hückel's rule</b></span>:<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""4n&#x20;&#x2B;&#x20;2&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;number&#x20;of&#x20;π&#x2D;electrons"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>Equation for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hückel's rule</b></span>:<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">4n + 2 = number of π-electrons</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- 'n' doesn't represent anything specific. If you plug the number of π electrons in and calculate n, it must come out to some whole number for the compound to be aromatic.</div><div>- Every π bond has 2 π-electrons.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-e3bb91d54833c60f2c1a803b1f47673b2dfdc1e0.jpg""><br></div><div></div></div>"
"<div>Steps to determining if a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ring&#x20;is&#x20;&lt;u&gt;aromatic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> using <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;H&#xFC;ckel&#x27;s&#x20;rule&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>:</div><div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Plug the number of π-electrons (2 per π-bond) into 4n+2 = π-electrons.</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Solve for n.</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">If n is a <u>whole number</u>, the compound is aromatic.</span></div>"	"<div>Steps to determining if a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">ring is <u>aromatic</u></span> using <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hückel's rule</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Plug the number of π-electrons (2 per π-bond) into 4n+2 = π-electrons.</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Solve for n.</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">If n is a <u>whole number</u>, the compound is aromatic.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-e3bb91d54833c60f2c1a803b1f47673b2dfdc1e0.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Steps to determining if a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">ring is <u>aromatic</u></span> using <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hückel's rule</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Plug&#x20;the&#x20;number&#x20;of&#x20;π&#x2D;electrons&#x20;&#x28;2&#x20;per&#x20;π&#x2D;bond&#x29;&#x20;into&#x20;4n&#x2B;2&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;π&#x2D;electrons&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Solve&#x20;for&#x20;n&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""If&#x20;n&#x20;is&#x20;a&#x20;&lt;u&gt;whole&#x20;number&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;the&#x20;compound&#x20;is&#x20;aromatic&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>Steps to determining if a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">ring is <u>aromatic</u></span> using <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hückel's rule</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Plug the number of π-electrons (2 per π-bond) into 4n+2 = π-electrons.</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Solve for n.</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">If n is a <u>whole number</u>, the compound is aromatic.</span></div>

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-c66209f2e393d198c93f59e7f381f97ed62caf17.jpg""></div>"
"1 <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;NADH&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[NADH/FADH<sub>2</sub>]</span> molecule results in the total pumping of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">10</span> <b>H<sup>+</sup> protons</b>&nbsp;across the membrane in the ETC."	"1 <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>NADH</u></span> molecule results in the total pumping of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">10</span> <b>H<sup>+</sup> protons</b>&nbsp;across the membrane in the ETC.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-This is because NADH enters the ETC in complex 1, so it results in the pumping of the 4 protons in complex 1, 4 protons in complex 3, and 2 protons in complex 4 (10 total).</div><div>- FADH<sub>2</sub> results in the pumping of 6 H<sup>+</sup> protons, because it enters in complex 2 (misses the first 4 H<sup>+</sup> in complex 1).</div><div><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""image-9581642473b749b6892da7f1bdb5f0b101bf9916.axd""></div>"
"1 <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>NADH</u></span> molecule results in the total pumping of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""10"" data-ordinal=""2"">[#]</span> <b>H<sup>+</sup> protons</b>&nbsp;across the membrane in the ETC."	"1 <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>NADH</u></span> molecule results in the total pumping of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">10</span> <b>H<sup>+</sup> protons</b>&nbsp;across the membrane in the ETC.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-This is because NADH enters the ETC in complex 1, so it results in the pumping of the 4 protons in complex 1, 4 protons in complex 3, and 2 protons in complex 4 (10 total).</div><div>- FADH<sub>2</sub> results in the pumping of 6 H<sup>+</sup> protons, because it enters in complex 2 (misses the first 4 H<sup>+</sup> in complex 1).</div><div><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""image-9581642473b749b6892da7f1bdb5f0b101bf9916.axd""></div>"
"1 <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;FADH&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;sub&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[NADH/FADH<sub>2</sub>]</span> molecule results in the total pumping of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">6</span> <b>H<sup>+</sup> protons</b>&nbsp;across the membrane in the ETC."	"1 <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>FADH</u><sub style="""">2</sub></span> molecule results in the total pumping of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">6</span> <b>H<sup>+</sup> protons</b>&nbsp;across the membrane in the ETC.

"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, a <i>flattened</i>&nbsp;slope indicates a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;catalytic&#x20;efficiency&#x20;&#x28;k&lt;sub&gt;cat&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x2F;K&lt;sub&gt;m&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"On a Lineweaver-Burk plot, a <i>flattened</i>&nbsp;slope indicates a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>catalytic efficiency (k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>)</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Catalytic efficiency (k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub>)</b> is the <u>inverse of the slope</u>.</div><div>- This shows the high pressure slope is smaller than the atmospheric slope, meaning k<sub>cat</sub>/K<sub>m</sub> is higher (greater catalytic efficiency).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d9cdf60b2aea4b41f51b8e71476f05981c98e590.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Coenzymes</b></span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>organic</i></span> molecules - usually derived from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>vitamins</u></span> - that bind to enzymes via <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""non&#x2D;covalent"" data-ordinal=""3"">[covalent/non-cov.]</span> bonds."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Coenzymes</b></span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>organic</i></span> molecules - usually derived from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>vitamins</u></span> - that bind to enzymes via <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">non-covalent</span> bonds.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Prosthetic</b>&nbsp;<b>groups</b>&nbsp;→ inorganic or organic molecules bind via covalent bonds.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Cofactors</b>&nbsp;→ inorganic molecules that can bind through either covalent or non-covalent bonds.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Coenzymes</b>&nbsp;→ organic molecules bound via non-covalent bonds.</div></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-f29faffd9f692af72580451c32809fc9d2061cda.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Prosthetic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;&lt;b&gt;groups&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>either</i> organic <i>or</i> inorganic</span> molecules required for enzymatic function that bind via <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>covalent</u></span> bonds."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Prosthetic</b> <b>groups</b></span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>either</i> organic <i>or</i> inorganic</span> molecules required for enzymatic function that bind via <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>covalent</u></span> bonds.

"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Cytosolic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[cytosolic/ER-bound]</span> <b>ribosomes</b> translate <u>proteins</u> that are destined for use <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inside</i></span> the cell.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cytosolic</b></span> <b>ribosomes</b> translate <u>proteins</u> that are destined for use <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inside</i></span> the cell.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cytosolic</b></span> <b>ribosomes</b> translate <u>proteins</u> that are destined for use <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;inside&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[inside/outside]</span> the cell.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cytosolic</b></span> <b>ribosomes</b> translate <u>proteins</u> that are destined for use <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inside</i></span> the cell.</div>

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Acetylating&#x20;agents&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are compounds that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>donate</i> <u>acetyl groups</u></span> to other compounds."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Acetylating agents</b></span> are compounds that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>donate</i> <u>acetyl groups</u></span> to other compounds.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Most common <u>organic</u> acetylating agent → <b>acetyl-CoA</b>.</div><div>- Most common <u>inorganic</u> acetylating agent → <b>acetic anhydride</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-6099e8b6351c2c8eef5702dfddefa3ad7d6e267f.jpg""></div>"

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Acetylating agents</b></span> are compounds that <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>donate</i> <u>acetyl groups</u></span> to other compounds.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Most common <u>organic</u> acetylating agent → <b>acetyl-CoA</b>.</div><div>- Most common <u>inorganic</u> acetylating agent → <b>acetic anhydride</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-6099e8b6351c2c8eef5702dfddefa3ad7d6e267f.jpg""></div>"
"In <i>biological</i> reactions, the most common <b>acetylating agent</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""acetyl&#x2D;CoA"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"In <i>biological</i> reactions, the most common <b>acetylating agent</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">acetyl-CoA</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Acetyl-CoA's carbonyl is easily attacked by other compounds - allowing for the transferance of the acetyl group.</div><div><br></div><div>- Most common <u>biological</u> acetylating agent → <b>acetyl-CoA</b>.</div><div>- Most common <u>laboratory</u> acetylating agent → <b>acetic anhydride</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-6099e8b6351c2c8eef5702dfddefa3ad7d6e267f.jpg""></div>"
"On a Michaelis-Menten plot, the <b>initial velocity (V<sub>0</sub>)</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">linear</i></span>&nbsp;to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;enzyme&#x20;&#x5B;E&#x5D;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[[S]/[E]]</span> concentration, while <i>still</i> being <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>first-order</i></span> with respect to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;substrate&#x20;&#x5B;S&#x5D;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[[S]/[E]]</span> concentration."	"On a Michaelis-Menten plot, the <b>initial velocity (V<sub>0</sub>)</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">linear</i></span>&nbsp;to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>enzyme [E]</u></span> concentration, while <i>still</i> being <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>first-order</i></span> with respect to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>substrate [S]</u></span> concentration.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Since [S] is multiplied by V<sub>max</sub> on top, while being added to K<sub>m</sub> on the bottom, [S] <u>cannot</u> be <b>linear</b> to V<sub>0</sub>.</div><div>- The initial rate (V<sub>0</sub>) still depends on substrate concentration, but it's <u>not</u> perfectly linear.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""96427da5d8d6f471eb7f67adb66c6c05.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d3e81acb431d9425288546309dff77468ffbce9c.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"On a Michaelis-Menten plot, the <b>initial velocity (V<sub>0</sub>)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;linear&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[first-order/linear]</span>&nbsp;to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>enzyme [E]</u></span> concentration, while <i>still</i> being <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;first&#x2D;order&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[first-order/linear]</span> with respect to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>substrate [S]</u></span> concentration."	"On a Michaelis-Menten plot, the <b>initial velocity (V<sub>0</sub>)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">linear</i></span>&nbsp;to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>enzyme [E]</u></span> concentration, while <i>still</i> being <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>first-order</i></span> with respect to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>substrate [S]</u></span> concentration.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Since [S] is multiplied by V<sub>max</sub> on top, while being added to K<sub>m</sub> on the bottom, [S] <u>cannot</u> be <b>linear</b> to V<sub>0</sub>.</div><div>- The initial rate (V<sub>0</sub>) still depends on substrate concentration, but it's <u>not</u> perfectly linear.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""96427da5d8d6f471eb7f67adb66c6c05.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d3e81acb431d9425288546309dff77468ffbce9c.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"In gluconeogenesis,&nbsp;<b>pyruvate carboxylase</b> uses <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;vitamin&#x20;B&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;sub&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;7&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x28;&lt;u&gt;biotin&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> as a cofactor."
