"In a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Western&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>blot</b>, the electrophoresis step occurs <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>first</i></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">separate out the proteins of interest</span> from a mixture."	"In a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Western</b></span> <b>blot</b>, the electrophoresis step occurs <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>first</i></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">separate out the proteins of interest</span> from a mixture.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Investigating the&nbsp;interactions between multiple proteins → native conditions.</div><div>- Looking to quantify just 1 protein → denaturing/reducing conditions.</div><div><br></div><img src=""57376bed915c562ee6f2f53ccbbc3762.png""></div>"
"In a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Western</b></span> <b>blot</b>, the electrophoresis step occurs <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;first&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[first/last]</span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""separate&#x20;out&#x20;the&#x20;proteins&#x20;of&#x20;interest"" data-ordinal=""2"">[purpose]</span> from a mixture."	"In a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Western</b></span> <b>blot</b>, the electrophoresis step occurs <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>first</i></span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">separate out the proteins of interest</span> from a mixture.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Investigating the&nbsp;interactions between multiple proteins → native conditions.</div><div>- Looking to quantify just 1 protein → denaturing/reducing conditions.</div><div><br></div><img src=""57376bed915c562ee6f2f53ccbbc3762.png""></div>"
"In a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Western&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>blot</b>, a researcher investigating the <u>interactions between <i>two</i> proteins</u> in a mixture should perform the electrophoresis step under <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">native</span> conditions."	"In a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Western</b></span> <b>blot</b>, a researcher investigating the <u>interactions between <i>two</i> proteins</u> in a mixture should perform the electrophoresis step under <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">native</span> conditions.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Electrophoresis happens <u>first</u>, so if you denatured them during electrophoresis, the interactions would no longer take place. The antibody would then have no interactions to detect.</div><div><br></div><img src=""57376bed915c562ee6f2f53ccbbc3762.png""></div>"
"In a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Western</b></span> <b>blot</b>, a researcher investigating the <u>interactions between <i>two</i> proteins</u> in a mixture should perform the electrophoresis step under <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""native"" data-ordinal=""2"">[denaturing/native]</span> conditions."	"In a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Western</b></span> <b>blot</b>, a researcher investigating the <u>interactions between <i>two</i> proteins</u> in a mixture should perform the electrophoresis step under <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">native</span> conditions.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Electrophoresis happens <u>first</u>, so if you denatured them during electrophoresis, the interactions would no longer take place. The antibody would then have no interactions to detect.</div><div><br></div><img src=""57376bed915c562ee6f2f53ccbbc3762.png""></div>"
"<div>Why should a researcher investigating protein-protein interactions perform the <u>electrophoresis</u> step under <i>native</i> conditions in a Western blot?<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""In&#x20;a&#x20;Western&#x20;blot&#x2C;&#x20;electrophoresis&#x20;happens&#x20;&lt;i&gt;first&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x2E;&#x20;If&#x20;the&#x20;two&#x20;proteins&#x20;were&#x20;separated&#x20;under&#x20;reducing&#x20;and&#x20;denaturing&#x20;conditions&#x2C;&#x20;their&#x20;interactions&#x20;would&#x20;be&#x20;&quot;destroyed&quot;&#x20;before&#x20;the&#x20;antibody&#x20;was&#x20;even&#x20;able&#x20;to&#x20;detect&#x20;anything&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>Why should a researcher investigating protein-protein interactions perform the <u>electrophoresis</u> step under <i>native</i> conditions in a Western blot?<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">In a Western blot, electrophoresis happens <i>first</i>. If the two proteins were separated under reducing and denaturing conditions, their interactions would be ""destroyed"" before the antibody was even able to detect anything.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""57376bed915c562ee6f2f53ccbbc3762.png""><br></div></div>"
"Enzymes that <i>cleave</i> nucleotides from DNA or RNA do it by cleaving the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""P&#x2D;O"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> bonds in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""phosphodiester&#x20;backbone"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>." <div>The phosphodiester backbone is the thing the joins the nucleotides together.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-613e31b8d4baf7cef78c2e6f5cd610950b19d2c6.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Hill coefficients</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>equal</i> to 1</span> indicate a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lack&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;of&#x20;cooperativity"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<b>Hill coefficients</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>equal</i> to 1</span> indicate a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lack</i> of cooperativity</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"In an enzymatic reaction, <b>k<sub>cat</sub></b> describes the <u>turnover rate</u> of the <i>step</i> that is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">rate-limiting</span>.

"In <u>ion exchangers</u>, ions that are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;tightly&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[weakly/tightly]</span> bound to the column require <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>highly</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">concentrated<b> salt gradients</b></span> to be washed out."	"In <u>ion exchangers</u>, ions that are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>tightly</i></span> bound to the column require <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>highly</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">concentrated<b> salt gradients</b></span> to be washed out.

"The <i>reduction</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">1</span> <b>disulfide bond(s)</b> requires the <i>addition</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2</span> <u>electron(s)</u>, since <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>both</i> sulfur atoms need to be reduced (S-S → SH &amp; SH)</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This generates ATP.</div><div><br></div><img src=""cb7628f7c5f145df306cbb16b71e78f1.png""></div>"
"The activity of <i>one</i> enzyme can be detected by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;coupling&#x20;it&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;to&#x20;another&#x20;&#x28;more&#x20;easily&#x20;detectable&#x29;&#x20;enzyme"" data-ordinal=""1"">[process]</span> - where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">products</span> of the <i>first</i> enzyme are the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">reactants</span> for the <i>second</i> enzyme."	"The activity of <i>one</i> enzyme can be detected by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>coupling it</u> to another (more easily detectable) enzyme</span> - where the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">products</span> of the <i>first</i> enzyme are the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">reactants</span> for the <i>second</i> enzyme.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Isopeptide bonds</b></span> are bonds formed between the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""NH&lt;sub&gt;3&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&lt;i&gt;side&#x20;chain&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""carboxylic&#x20;acid&#x20;&lt;i&gt;side&#x20;chain&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of two different amino acids - <i>similar</i> to a peptide bond."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Isopeptide bonds</b></span> are bonds formed between the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">NH<sub>3</sub> <i>side chain</i></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">carboxylic acid <i>side chain</i></span> of two different amino acids - <i>similar</i> to a peptide bond.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Peptide bond</b> → NH<sub>3</sub> and carboxylic acid are from the backbones of two amino acids.</div><div>- <b>Isopeptide bond</b> → NH<sub>3</sub> and carboxylic acid from the side chains of two amino acids.</div><div><br></div><img alt=""Switching Bond: Generation of New Antimicrobial Peptides via the  Incorporation of an Intramolecular Isopeptide Bond | ACS Infectious Diseases"" src=""https://pubs.acs.org/cms/10.1021/acsinfecdis.1c00037/asset/images/large/id1c00037_0001.jpeg""><br></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Isopeptide&#x20;bonds&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are bonds formed between the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">NH<sub>3</sub> <i>side chain</i></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">carboxylic acid <i>side chain</i></span> of two different amino acids - <i>similar</i> to a peptide bond."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Isopeptide bonds</b></span> are bonds formed between the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">NH<sub>3</sub> <i>side chain</i></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">carboxylic acid <i>side chain</i></span> of two different amino acids - <i>similar</i> to a peptide bond.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Peptide bond</b> → NH<sub>3</sub> and carboxylic acid are from the backbones of two amino acids.</div><div>- <b>Isopeptide bond</b> → NH<sub>3</sub> and carboxylic acid from the side chains of two amino acids.</div><div><br></div><img alt=""Switching Bond: Generation of New Antimicrobial Peptides via the  Incorporation of an Intramolecular Isopeptide Bond | ACS Infectious Diseases"" src=""https://pubs.acs.org/cms/10.1021/acsinfecdis.1c00037/asset/images/large/id1c00037_0001.jpeg""><br></div>"
"An <u>amino acid substitution</u> on an enzyme that results in an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increase&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;in&#x20;its&#x20;&lt;b&gt;K&lt;sub&gt;m&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> indicates that the substrate's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ability to <i>bind</i> to the active site</span> is <i>hindered</i>."	"An <u>amino acid substitution</u> on an enzyme that results in an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increase</i> in its <b>K<sub>m</sub></b></span> indicates that the substrate's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ability to <i>bind</i> to the active site</span> is <i>hindered</i>.

"The <i>reduction</i> of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1"" data-ordinal=""3"">[1/2]</span> <b>disulfide bond(s)</b> requires the <i>addition</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2</span> <u>electron(s)</u>, since <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>both</i> sulfur atoms need to be reduced (S-S → SH &amp; SH)</span>." "In <b>isoelectric focusing</b>, the proteins are loaded into the sample under <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""native"" data-ordinal=""1"">[denaturing/native]</span> conditions."	"In <b>isoelectric focusing</b>, the proteins are loaded into the sample under <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">native</span> conditions.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Since isoelectric focusing separates proteins based on their relative charges, adding a reducing &amp; denaturing agent will ruin the sample.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c3a0f41635f8fd2074910edf9e7aacbb.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fa7894e83b390be315de6a6adaba8e77.png""></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Autophosphorylation</b></span> is a process by which <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;kinase&#x20;enzymes&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;phosphorylate&#x20;&lt;i&gt;themselves&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> as opposed to their substrate."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Autophosphorylation</b></span> is a process by which <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>kinase enzymes</u> phosphorylate <i>themselves</i></span> as opposed to their substrate.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>To control for this, researchers can include a well of just the enzyme with ATP (no substrate).&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-96bd8a76869a2f8004234cea4f49cb4f8eaae145.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Autophosphorylation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[process]</span> is a process by which <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>kinase enzymes</u> phosphorylate <i>themselves</i></span> as opposed to their substrate."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Autophosphorylation</b></span> is a process by which <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>kinase enzymes</u> phosphorylate <i>themselves</i></span> as opposed to their substrate.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"To <i>control</i> for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>autophoshorylation</b></span>, researchers should incubate the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>enzyme</u> <i>with</i> <u>ATP</u> (<i>excluding</i> the substrate)</span>&nbsp;in order to measure the level of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""phosphorylation&#x20;associated&#x20;with&#x20;&lt;i&gt;just&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;enzyme&#x20;itself"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>."	"To <i>control</i> for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>autophoshorylation</b></span>, researchers should incubate the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>enzyme</u> <i>with</i> <u>ATP</u> (<i>excluding</i> the substrate)</span>&nbsp;in order to measure the level of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">phosphorylation associated with <i>just</i> the enzyme itself</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Autophosphorylation</b> is when kinase enzymes <u>phosphorylate themselves</u> as opposed to their substrates.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-96bd8a76869a2f8004234cea4f49cb4f8eaae145.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Uncompetitive</b></span> <b>inhibitors</b> <i>more</i> effectively inhibit enzymes when the <u>[inhibitor]</u> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increased&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[increased/decreased]</span> &amp; <u>[S]</u> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increased&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[increased/decreased]</span>.&nbsp;"	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Uncompetitive</b></span> <b>inhibitors</b> <i>more</i> effectively inhibit enzymes when the <u>[inhibitor]</u> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increased</i></span> &amp; <u>[S]</u> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increased</i></span>.&nbsp;
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Uncompetitive inhibitors</b> can only inhibit enzymes by <u>binding to the ES-complex</u>. By increasing substrate concentration, the inhibitor has more of an opportunity to bind &amp; inhibit the enzyme.</div><div><br></div><img src=""500px-UNCOMPETITIVE.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Uncompetitive&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>inhibitors</b> <i>more</i> effectively inhibit enzymes when the <u>[inhibitor]</u> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increased</i></span> &amp; <u>[S]</u> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increased</i></span>.&nbsp;"	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Uncompetitive</b></span> <b>inhibitors</b> <i>more</i> effectively inhibit enzymes when the <u>[inhibitor]</u> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increased</i></span> &amp; <u>[S]</u> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>increased</i></span>.&nbsp;
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Uncompetitive inhibitors</b> can only inhibit enzymes by <u>binding to the ES-complex</u>. By increasing substrate concentration, the inhibitor has more of an opportunity to bind &amp; inhibit the enzyme.</div><div><br></div><img src=""500px-UNCOMPETITIVE.jpg""></div>"
"In enzyme kinetics, <b>affinity constant (K<sub>1</sub>)</b>&nbsp;represents the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;affinity&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;an&#x20;enzyme&#x20;has&#x20;for&#x20;its&#x20;substrate"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"In enzyme kinetics, <b>affinity constant (K<sub>1</sub>)</b>&nbsp;represents the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>affinity</i> an enzyme has for its substrate</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Think of this as the <u>inverse</u> of the dissociation constant (K<sub>d</sub>).<div>- The <b>apparent affinity constant (K<sub>app</sub>)</b> is the apparent affinity of the enzyme for its substrate in the presence of an inhibitor (which tends to be lower than K<sub>1</sub>).</div></div>"

- Think of this as the <u>inverse</u> of the dissociation constant (K<sub>d</sub>).<div>- The <b>apparent affinity constant (K<sub>app</sub>)</b> is the apparent affinity of the enzyme for its substrate in the presence of an inhibitor (which tends to be lower than K<sub>1</sub>).</div></div>"
"<div>In enzyme kinetics, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;apparent&#x20;affinity&#x20;constant&#x20;&#x28;K&lt;sub&gt;app&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> tends to be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> than the <u>affinity constant (K</u><sub style="""">1</sub><u>)</u>, since the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">inhibitor <i>hinders</i> typical enzyme-substrate binding</span>.</div>"	"<div>In enzyme kinetics, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>apparent affinity constant (K<sub>app</sub>)</b></span> tends to be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> than the <u>affinity constant (K</u><sub style="""">1</sub><u>)</u>, since the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">inhibitor <i>hinders</i> typical enzyme-substrate binding</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The <b>apparent affinity constant (K<sub>app</sub>)</b> is the apparent affinity of the enzyme for its substrate in the presence of an inhibitor (which tends to be lower than K<sub>1</sub>).</div><div>- The <b>affinity constant (K<sub>1</sub>)</b> is the affinity an enzyme has for its substrate (<u>directly proportional</u> to E-S affinity).</div></div>"
"<div>In enzyme kinetics, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>apparent affinity constant (K<sub>app</sub>)</b></span> tends to be <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[lower/higher]</span> than the <u>affinity constant (K</u><sub style="""">1</sub><u>)</u>, since the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""inhibitor&#x20;&lt;i&gt;hinders&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;typical&#x20;enzyme&#x2D;substrate&#x20;binding"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div>In enzyme kinetics, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>apparent affinity constant (K<sub>app</sub>)</b></span> tends to be <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> than the <u>affinity constant (K</u><sub style="""">1</sub><u>)</u>, since the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">inhibitor <i>hinders</i> typical enzyme-substrate binding</span>.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The <b>apparent affinity constant (K<sub>app</sub>)</b> is the apparent affinity of the enzyme for its substrate in the presence of an inhibitor (which tends to be lower than K<sub>1</sub>).</div><div>- The <b>affinity constant (K<sub>1</sub>)</b> is the affinity an enzyme has for its substrate (<u>directly proportional</u> to E-S affinity).</div></div>"

"For multimeric proteins that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>are not</i></span> linked together by <u>disulfide bonds</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>non-reducing</b></span> <b>SDS-PAGE</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;will&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[will/will not]</span> separate their subunits into monomers - resulting in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;further&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[shorter/further]</span> migration distances."	"For multimeric proteins that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>are not</i></span> linked together by <u>disulfide bonds</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>non-reducing</b></span> <b>SDS-PAGE</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>will</i></span> separate their subunits into monomers - resulting in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>further</i></span> migration distances.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Multimeric proteins: dimers, trimers, etc.</div><div><br></div><div>- <b>Native PAGE</b> → protein is run in its entirely native, folded state. No separation occurs.</div><div>- <b>Non-reducing SDS-PAGE</b> of <u>non-disulfide</u>-linked proteins → protein is separated into its monomers and travels the furthest distance.</div><div>- <b>Non-reducing SDS-PAGE</b> of <u>disulfide</u> linked proteins → subunits still stay together (disulfides are not reduced), so they don't travel as far.</div><div><br></div><img src=""7fc7ae5640e98141ec7a88d8c2b31d45.png""></div>"
"For multimeric proteins that <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;are&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[are/are not]</span> linked together by <u>disulfide bonds</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>non-reducing</b></span> <b>SDS-PAGE</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>will</i></span> separate their subunits into monomers - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>further</i></span> migration distances."	"For multimeric proteins that <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>are not</i></span> linked together by <u>disulfide bonds</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>non-reducing</b></span> <b>SDS-PAGE</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>will</i></span> separate their subunits into monomers - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>further</i></span> migration distances.

<div>Multimeric proteins: dimers, trimers, etc.</div><div><br></div><div>- <b>Native PAGE</b> → protein is run in its entirely native, folded state. No separation occurs.</div><div>- <b>Non-reducing SDS-PAGE</b> of <u>non-disulfide</u>-linked proteins → protein is separated into its monomers and travels the furthest distance.</div><div>- <b>Non-reducing SDS-PAGE</b> of <u>disulfide</u> linked proteins → subunits still stay together (disulfides are not reduced), so they don't travel as far.</div><div><br></div><img src=""7fc7ae5640e98141ec7a88d8c2b31d45.png""></div>"
"For multimeric proteins that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>are</i></span> linked together by <u>disulfide bonds</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;native&#x2F;non&#x2D;reducing&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[native/non-reducing/either]</span> <b>SDS-PAGE</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>will not</i></span> separate their subunits into monomers - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>shorter</i></span> migration distances."	"For multimeric proteins that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>are</i></span> linked together by <u>disulfide bonds</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>native/non-reducing</b></span> <b>SDS-PAGE</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>will not</i></span> separate their subunits into monomers - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>shorter</i></span> migration distances.

"The <i>denaturation</i> of <b>DNA</b> under various different conditions happens in a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""cooperative"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> manner - it has a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;sigmoidal&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[shape]</span> curve."	"The <i>denaturation</i> of <b>DNA</b> under various different conditions happens in a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">cooperative</span> manner - it has a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>sigmoidal</u></span> curve.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The denaturation of some DNA strands induces further denaturation of other DNA strands - contributing to the sigmoidal curve. Eventually things level off.</div><div><br></div><img src=""Tm.jpg""></div>"
"If a particular <u>gene</u> is expressed <i>only</i> by one type of tissue, it means that <i>only</i> that tissue <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""expresses&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;b&gt;transcription&#x20;factors&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;for&#x20;that&#x20;gene"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"If a particular <u>gene</u> is expressed <i>only</i> by one type of tissue, it means that <i>only</i> that tissue <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">expresses the <b>transcription factors</b> for that gene</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Every chromosome in the body is composed of the same set of genes. Those genes are regulated in different amounts though by whether or not that tissue expresses the transcription factors needed for the promotion of transcription for that one gene.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-df0237c74f0d408e24b99b974da6f90e71e3fca1.jpg""></div>"
"Proteins that are native to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""polar"" data-ordinal=""1"">[polar/non-polar]</span> environments <b>dimerize</b> through interactions between their <u>hydro</u><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>phobic</u></span> residues, since their hydro<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">philic</span> residues will be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>occupied</i> with the polar solvent</span>."	"Proteins that are native to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">polar</span> environments <b>dimerize</b> through interactions between their <u>hydro</u><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>phobic</u></span> residues, since their hydro<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">philic</span> residues will be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>occupied</i> with the polar solvent</span>.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><b>α-ketoglutarate</b></span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>is</i></span> a precursor to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">gluconeogenesis</span>, since it can&nbsp;<i>either</i>&nbsp;be <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">transaminated into <u>glutamate</u> (glucogenic amino acid)</span> <i>or</i> it can form into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>oxaloacetate</u></span>.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Uncommon amino acids</u></span>, like ornihtine, are <i>not</i> incorporated into peptides because they <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lack&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;codons&#x20;in&#x20;the&#x20;genetic&#x20;code"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Uncommon amino acids</u></span>, like ornihtine, are <i>not</i> incorporated into peptides because they <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lack</i>&nbsp;codons in the genetic code</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Codons in the genetic code only code for the 20 common amino acids.</div><div>- Uncommon amino acids can still be synthesized by the cell, but they're synthesized through pathways that don't involve direct transcription → translation.</div><div><br></div><img src=""70-26-8.png""></div>"
"The 64 <b>codons</b> in the genetic code <i>only</i> code for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;common&#x20;amino&#x20;acids&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> - they <i>do not</i> code for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""uncommon&#x20;amino&#x20;acids"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The 64 <b>codons</b> in the genetic code <i>only</i> code for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>common amino acids</u></span> - they <i>do not</i> code for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">uncommon amino acids</span>.

"Patients with <b>diabetes</b> experience sudden weight <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""loss"" data-ordinal=""1"">[loss/gain]</span> due to the <i>rapid</i> mobilization of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""fatty&#x20;acids"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> as <i>alternative</i> sources of fuel."	"Patients with <b>diabetes</b> experience sudden weight <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">loss</span> due to the <i>rapid</i> mobilization of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">fatty acids</span> as <i>alternative</i> sources of fuel.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Since the body has a more difficult time utilizing glucose, fatty acids (in the form of ketone bodies) are quickly mobilized for metabolism. This causes sudden weight loss.</div>"

<div>5' → 3' direction</div><div><br></div><img src=""OSC_Microbio_10_02_NAcidStrand.jpg""></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>glycerol-3-P</b></span> <b>shuttle</b>&nbsp;uses cytosolic <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">NADH oxidation</span> to <i>power</i> the conversion of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>DHAP</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>glycerol-3-P</u></span> in the cytosol - eventually transferring its electrons into complex <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2"" data-ordinal=""4"">[...]</span> of the ETC.</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>glycerol-3-P</b></span> <b>shuttle</b>&nbsp;uses cytosolic <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">NADH oxidation</span> to <i>power</i> the conversion of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>DHAP</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>glycerol-3-P</u></span> in the cytosol - eventually transferring its electrons into complex <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4"">2</span> of the ETC.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Cytosolic DHAP → GA3P oxidation &amp; mitochondrial GA3P → DHAP oxidation is where the name ""GA3P shuttle"" comes from.</div><div>- This is in contrast with the malate aspartate shuttle, which instead moves electrons into complex 1.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-04bc26bdbaf07f277d2b178b49edc8b0abaaabe8.jpg""></div>"
"In the glycerol-3-P shuttle, the <i>conversion</i> of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;DHAP&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> → <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;glycerol&#x2D;3&#x2D;P&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> happens in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cytosol</span> to <i>transfer</i> its electrons to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>glycerol-3-P dehydrogenase</b></span> in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">mitochondria</span> - generating <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">FADH<sub>2</sub></span>."	"In the glycerol-3-P shuttle, the <i>conversion</i> of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>DHAP</u></span> → <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>glycerol-3-P</u></span> happens in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cytosol</span> to <i>transfer</i> its electrons to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>glycerol-3-P dehydrogenase</b></span> in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">mitochondria</span> - generating <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">FADH<sub>2</sub></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Cytosolic DHAP → GA3P oxidation &amp; mitochondrial GA3P → DHAP oxidation is where the name ""GA3P shuttle"" comes from.</div><div>- This is in contrast with the malate aspartate shuttle, which instead moves electrons into complex 1.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-04bc26bdbaf07f277d2b178b49edc8b0abaaabe8.jpg""></div>"
"In the glycerol-3-P shuttle, the <i>conversion</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>DHAP</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>glycerol-3-P</u></span> happens in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""cytosol"" data-ordinal=""2"">[mitochondria/cytosol]</span> to <i>transfer</i> its electrons to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>glycerol-3-P dehydrogenase</b></span> in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">mitochondria</span> - generating <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">FADH<sub>2</sub></span>."	"In the glycerol-3-P shuttle, the <i>conversion</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>DHAP</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>glycerol-3-P</u></span> happens in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">cytosol</span> to <i>transfer</i> its electrons to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>glycerol-3-P dehydrogenase</b></span> in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">mitochondria</span> - generating <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">FADH<sub>2</sub></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Cytosolic DHAP → GA3P oxidation &amp; mitochondrial GA3P → DHAP oxidation is where the name ""GA3P shuttle"" comes from.</div><div>- This is in contrast with the malate aspartate shuttle, which instead moves electrons into complex 1.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-04bc26bdbaf07f277d2b178b49edc8b0abaaabe8.jpg""></div>"
"In the glycerol-3-P shuttle, the <i>conversion</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>DHAP</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>glycerol-3-P</u></span> happens in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cytosol</span> to <i>transfer</i> its electrons to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;glycerol&#x2D;3&#x2D;P&#x20;dehydrogenase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[enzyme]</span> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""mitochondria"" data-ordinal=""3"">[mitochondria/cytosol]</span> - generating <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""FADH&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[cofactor]</span>."	"In the glycerol-3-P shuttle, the <i>conversion</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>DHAP</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>glycerol-3-P</u></span> happens in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cytosol</span> to <i>transfer</i> its electrons to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>glycerol-3-P dehydrogenase</b></span> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">mitochondria</span> - generating <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">FADH<sub>2</sub></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Cytosolic DHAP → GA3P oxidation &amp; mitochondrial GA3P → DHAP oxidation is where the name ""GA3P shuttle"" comes from.</div><div>- This is in contrast with the malate aspartate shuttle, which instead moves electrons into complex 1.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-04bc26bdbaf07f277d2b178b49edc8b0abaaabe8.jpg""></div>"
"In the glycerol-3-P shuttle, the <i>conversion</i> of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""g&lt;u&gt;lycerol&#x2D;3&#x2D;P&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> → <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;DHAP&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> happens in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">mitochondria</span> to <i>generate </i>FADH<sub>2</sub>&nbsp;from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>glycerol-3-P dehydrogenase</b></span> for use in complex <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">2</span> of the ETC."	"In the glycerol-3-P shuttle, the <i>conversion</i> of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">g<u>lycerol-3-P</u></span> → <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>DHAP</u></span> happens in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">mitochondria</span> to <i>generate </i>FADH<sub>2</sub>&nbsp;from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>glycerol-3-P dehydrogenase</b></span> for use in complex <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">2</span> of the ETC.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Cytosolic DHAP → GA3P oxidation &amp; mitochondrial GA3P → DHAP oxidation is where the name ""GA3P shuttle"" comes from.</div><div>- This is in contrast with the malate aspartate shuttle, which instead moves electrons into complex 1.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-04bc26bdbaf07f277d2b178b49edc8b0abaaabe8.jpg""></div>"
"In the glycerol-3-P shuttle, the <i>conversion</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">g<u>lycerol-3-P</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>DHAP</u></span> happens in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""mitochondria"" data-ordinal=""2"">[mitochondria/cytosol]</span> to <i>generate </i>FADH<sub>2</sub>&nbsp;from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>glycerol-3-P dehydrogenase</b></span> for use in complex <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">2</span> of the ETC."	"In the glycerol-3-P shuttle, the <i>conversion</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">g<u>lycerol-3-P</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>DHAP</u></span> happens in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">mitochondria</span> to <i>generate </i>FADH<sub>2</sub>&nbsp;from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>glycerol-3-P dehydrogenase</b></span> for use in complex <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">2</span> of the ETC.

"When researchers design experiments that utilize <b>siRNAs</b> to regulate protein levels, they <i>must</i> establish <u>controls</u> that demonstrate that the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""siRNA&#x20;&lt;i&gt;only&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;affects&#x20;the&#x20;transcript&#x20;of&#x20;interest"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"When researchers design experiments that utilize <b>siRNAs</b> to regulate protein levels, they <i>must</i> establish <u>controls</u> that demonstrate that the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">siRNA <i>only</i> affects the transcript of interest</span>.

<div>This is where the 2.5 ATP for NADH and 1.5 ATP for FADH<sub>2</sub> comes from (i.e. their relative contributions to ATP production based on the number of protons they help pump).</div><div><br></div><img src=""image-9581642473b749b6892da7f1bdb5f0b101bf9916.axd""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This is where the 2.5 ATP for NADH and 1.5 ATP for FADH<sub>2</sub> comes from (i.e. their relative contributions to ATP production based on the number of protons they help pump).</div><div><br></div><img src=""image-9581642473b749b6892da7f1bdb5f0b101bf9916.axd""></div>"
"<div>Amount of <u>energy</u> (kcal) that can be <i>extracted</i> from glycogen, proteins, and triglycerides...<br></div><div><br></div><div><b>Glycogen</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""4&#x20;kcal"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of energy <i>per</i> gram.</div><div><b>Proteins</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""4&#x20;kcal"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of energy <i>per</i> gram.</div><div><b>Triglycerides</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""9&#x20;kcal"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of energy <i>per</i> gram.</div>"	"<div>Amount of <u>energy</u> (kcal) that can be <i>extracted</i> from glycogen, proteins, and triglycerides...<br></div><div><br></div><div><b>Glycogen</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">4 kcal</span> of energy <i>per</i> gram.</div><div><b>Proteins</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">4 kcal</span> of energy <i>per</i> gram.</div><div><b>Triglycerides</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">9 kcal</span> of energy <i>per</i> gram.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<b>Lipoprotein lipase</b> activity is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>stimulated</i></span> by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;insulin&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[hormone]</span>."	"<b>Lipoprotein lipase</b> activity is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>stimulated</i></span> by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>insulin</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Lipoprotein lipase</b>: hydrolyzes triglycerides from VLDLs and cholymicrons in the blood.</div><div>- <b>Insulin</b> is primarily concerned with fat storage. Hydrolyzing these triglycerides allows those fatty acids to be re-assembled into triglycerides in adipose tissue.</div><div><br></div><img src=""8ad2118e2af5f52cb50237710216c242.jpg""></div>"

"<b>Lipoprotein lipase</b> activity is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>stimulated</i></span> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>insulin</u></span>.

Hydrolyzing these triglycerides allows those fatty acids to be re-assembled into triglycerides in adipose tissue.</div><div><br></div><img src=""8ad2118e2af5f52cb50237710216c242.jpg""></div>"

"On fatty acid synthase, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>malonyl-CoA</b></span> attaches to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>acyl-carrier protein (ACP)</u></span> subunit, while the <i>decarboxylated</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>acetyl-CoA</b></span> binds to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>fatty acid synthase</u> <i>directly</i></span>.

"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NAD</b><sup><b>+</b></sup></span>&nbsp;<i>removes</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2&#x20;electrons"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2&#x20;H&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x20;protons"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> from its substrate to reduce itself - accepting <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>both</i></span> electrons but <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>only 1</i></span> H<sup>+</sup> proton to yield <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">NADH &amp; H<sup>+</sup></span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NAD</b><sup><b>+</b></sup></span>&nbsp;<i>removes</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 electrons</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 H<sup>+</sup> protons</span> from its substrate to reduce itself - accepting <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>both</i></span> electrons but <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>only 1</i></span> H<sup>+</sup> proton to yield <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">NADH &amp; H<sup>+</sup></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>NAD<sup>+</sup> + 2e<sup>-</sup> + H<sup>+</sup> → NADH + H<sup>+</sup></div><div><br></div>- NADH oxidation to NAD<sup>+</sup> → donates 2 electrons and 2 protons (the extra and its bound hydrogen).<div>- NAD<sup>+</sup> reduction to NADH → removes 2 electrons and 2 protons &amp; accepts both electrons but only accepts 1 proton (creating an ""extra"" proton).</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;NAD&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;sup&gt;&lt;b&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[NADH/NAD<sup>+</sup>]</span>&nbsp;<i>removes</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 electrons</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 H<sup>+</sup> protons</span> from its substrate to reduce itself - accepting <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>both</i></span> electrons but <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>only 1</i></span> H<sup>+</sup> proton to yield <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">NADH &amp; H<sup>+</sup></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NAD</b><sup><b>+</b></sup></span>&nbsp;<i>removes</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 electrons</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 H<sup>+</sup> protons</span> from its substrate to reduce itself - accepting <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>both</i></span> electrons but <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>only 1</i></span> H<sup>+</sup> proton to yield <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">NADH &amp; H<sup>+</sup></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>NAD<sup>+</sup> + 2e<sup>-</sup> + H<sup>+</sup> → NADH + H<sup>+</sup></div><div><br></div>- NADH oxidation to NAD<sup>+</sup> → donates 2 electrons and 2 protons (the extra and its bound hydrogen).<div>- NAD<sup>+</sup> reduction to NADH → removes 2 electrons and 2 protons &amp; accepts both electrons but only accepts 1 proton (creating an ""extra"" proton).</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NAD</b><sup><b>+</b></sup></span>&nbsp;<i>removes</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 electrons</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 H<sup>+</sup> protons</span> from its substrate to reduce itself - accepting <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;both&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[1/both]</span> electrons but <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;only&#x20;1&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> H<sup>+</sup> proton to yield <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">NADH &amp; H<sup>+</sup></span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NAD</b><sup><b>+</b></sup></span>&nbsp;<i>removes</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 electrons</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 H<sup>+</sup> protons</span> from its substrate to reduce itself - accepting <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>both</i></span> electrons but <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>only 1</i></span> H<sup>+</sup> proton to yield <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4"">NADH &amp; H<sup>+</sup></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>NAD<sup>+</sup> + 2e<sup>-</sup> + H<sup>+</sup> → NADH + H<sup>+</sup></div><div><br></div>- NADH oxidation to NAD<sup>+</sup> → donates 2 electrons and 2 protons (the extra and its bound hydrogen).<div>- NAD<sup>+</sup> reduction to NADH → removes 2 electrons and 2 protons &amp; accepts both electrons but only accepts 1 proton (creating an ""extra"" proton).</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NAD</b><sup><b>+</b></sup></span>&nbsp;<i>removes</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 electrons</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 H<sup>+</sup> protons</span> from its substrate to reduce itself - accepting <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>both</i></span> electrons but <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>only 1</i></span> H<sup>+</sup> proton to yield <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""NADH&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;H&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""4"">[net products]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NAD</b><sup><b>+</b></sup></span>&nbsp;<i>removes</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 electrons</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 H<sup>+</sup> protons</span> from its substrate to reduce itself - accepting <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>both</i></span> electrons but <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>only 1</i></span> H<sup>+</sup> proton to yield <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4"">NADH &amp; H<sup>+</sup></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>NAD<sup>+</sup> + 2e<sup>-</sup> + H<sup>+</sup> → NADH + H<sup>+</sup></div><div><br></div>- NADH oxidation to NAD<sup>+</sup> → donates 2 electrons and 2 protons (the extra and its bound hydrogen).<div>- NAD<sup>+</sup> reduction to NADH → removes 2 electrons and 2 protons &amp; accepts both electrons but only accepts 1 proton (creating an ""extra"" proton).</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NAD</b><b style="""">H</b></span>&nbsp;<i>donates</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2&#x20;electrons"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2&#x20;H&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x20;protons&#x20;&#x28;1&#x20;from&#x20;itself&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;1&#x20;&quot;extra&quot;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> to oxidize itself."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NAD</b><b style="""">H</b></span>&nbsp;<i>donates</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 electrons</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">2 H<sup>+</sup> protons (1 from itself &amp; 1 ""extra"")</span> to oxidize itself.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Flippase</b>: outer → inner using ATP.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Floppase</b>: inner → outer using ATP.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Scramblase</b>&nbsp;→ either direction flowing down its concentration gradient (no ATP).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-9dc2c8ef0fc5c138bc0882acdab15b78261ff365.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""07_10.jpg""></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Floppase</b></span>&nbsp;catalyzes the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;transverse&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;diffusion&#x20;of&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;u&gt;phospholipids&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in the bilayer from the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;inner&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[inner/outer]</span> leaflet to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;outer&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[inner/outer]</span> leaflet - ATP <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;is&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[is/is not]</span> required."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Floppase</b></span>&nbsp;catalyzes the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>transverse</i>&nbsp;diffusion of&nbsp;<u>phospholipids</u></span> in the bilayer from the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inner</i></span> leaflet to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>outer</i></span> leaflet - ATP <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>is</i></span> required.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Flippase</b>: outer → inner using ATP.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Floppase</b>: inner → outer using ATP.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Scramblase</b>&nbsp;→ either direction flowing down its concentration gradient (no ATP).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-9dc2c8ef0fc5c138bc0882acdab15b78261ff365.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""07_10.jpg""></div></div>"
"<u>Histone acetylation</u> can be detected by a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Western&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>blot</b>, since those are capable of detecting <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""proteins&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;protein&#x20;&lt;i&gt;modifications&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<u>Histone acetylation</u> can be detected by a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Western</b></span> <b>blot</b>, since those are capable of detecting <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">proteins &amp; protein <i>modifications</i></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Histone acetylation is a modification made to a protein (histone protein).</div><div><br></div><img src=""57376bed915c562ee6f2f53ccbbc3762.png""></div>"
"Why is ATP considered an allosteric feedback regulator of PFK-1 if PFK-1 only uses ATP as a <i>reactant</i>?<div><br><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Glycolysis&#x20;has&#x20;a&#x20;&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;&lt;u&gt;net&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;&#x20;product&#x20;of&#x20;2&#x20;ATP&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x2E;&#x20;The&#x20;ATP&#x20;that&#x20;acts&#x20;as&#x20;a&#x20;feedback&#x20;inhibitor&#x20;is&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;i&gt;net&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;ATP&#x20;produced&#x20;from&#x20;the&#x20;entirety&#x20;of&#x20;glycolysis&#x20;&#x2D;&#x20;&lt;i&gt;NOT&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;ATP&#x20;it&#x20;uses&#x20;as&#x20;a&#x20;reactant&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div></div>"	"Why is ATP considered an allosteric feedback regulator of PFK-1 if PFK-1 only uses ATP as a <i>reactant</i>?<div><br><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Glycolysis has a <i style=""""><u>net</u></i><u> product of 2 ATP</u>. The ATP that acts as a feedback inhibitor is the <i>net</i> ATP produced from the entirety of glycolysis - <i>NOT</i> the ATP it uses as a reactant.</span></div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""cb7628f7c5f145df306cbb16b71e78f1.png""></div>"
"<div>To <b>PFK-1</b>&nbsp;in glycolysis, ATP <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> act as an <i>allosteric</i> <u>feedback regulator</u>.</div>"	"<div>To <b>PFK-1</b>&nbsp;in glycolysis, ATP <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>does</i></span> act as an <i>allosteric</i> <u>feedback regulator</u>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Even though PFK-1 uses ATP as a reactant, it's the other net ATP produced from the rest of the pathway tha acts as a feedback regulator.</div><div><br></div><img src=""cb7628f7c5f145df306cbb16b71e78f1.png""></div>"
"Proteins that <i>activate</i> <b>transcription factors</b>&nbsp;must do this in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""nucleus"" data-ordinal=""1"">[cellular location]</span>."	"Proteins that <i>activate</i> <b>transcription factors</b>&nbsp;must do this in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">nucleus</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Transcription factors <i>wait</i> in the nucleus until they're needed, so proteins that activate them must travel into the nucleus to do this.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-fdd451ad8aba9fc5572fb07644cf008ed82705b2.jpg""></div>"
"The term ""primary sequence"" refers to the sequence of the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""protein&#x27;s&#x20;&lt;u&gt;amino&#x20;acids&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The term ""primary sequence"" refers to the sequence of the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">protein's <u>amino acids</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Do NOT confuse this with the sequence of nucleotides that comprise the transcript!</div>"

"On carbohydrates, <b>α</b>-anomers &amp; <b>β</b>-anomers <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;do&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[do/do not]</span> differ in <u>optical activity</u>." "<b>k<sub>cat</sub></b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> change with <i>changing</i> <u>enzyme concentrations</u> ([E])." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>k<sub>cat</sub></b> describes the turn-over rate of just the one enzyme.</div>"
"<div>A(n)&nbsp;<i>increase</i> in <u>enzyme concentration</u> ([E]) <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;change"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>k<sub>cat</sub></b>.<br></div>"	"<div>A(n)&nbsp;<i>increase</i> in <u>enzyme concentration</u> ([E]) <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>does not</i> change</span> <b>k<sub>cat</sub></b>.<br></div>

"In the lab, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>RT-PCR</b></span> can be used to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>quantify</i> <u>mRNA</u> levels</span> by measuring the amount of resulting cDNA.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"When an enzyme is already&nbsp;<u>saturated</u> with substrate, an <i>increase</i> in [S] <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;will&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;change"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> the <b>rate (V<sub>0</sub>)</b>." "For two strands of DNA with <i>equal</i> G:C contents, the strand with the <b>G:C base pairs</b> located near <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""the&#x20;&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;ends&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;strand&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>stronger</i></span>."	"For two strands of DNA with <i>equal</i> G:C contents, the strand with the <b>G:C base pairs</b> located near <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">the <u><i>ends</i> of the strand</u></span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>stronger</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- G:C pairs near the ends of the strand → stronger.</div><div>- G:C pairs in the middle of the strand → weaker.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-520e39b6040a0ceca31fef8de06e87bca4e7777b.jpg""></div>"

"For two strands of DNA with <i>equal</i> G:C contents, the strand with the <b>G:C base pairs</b> located near <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">the <u><i>ends</i> of the strand</u></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>stronger</i></span>.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Arginine&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[amino acid]</span> is the <i>most</i> <u>basic</u> amino acid."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Arginine</b></span> is the <i>most</i> <u>basic</u> amino acid.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>At physiological pH, both arginine &amp; lysine are basic. Arginine is more basic though, due to its highly basic <u>guanidinium</u> group.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""355c73cab443ba945d313e3026950432.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""c3a0f41635f8fd2074910edf9e7aacbb.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>dissociation constant (K<sub>d</sub>)</b></span> of a protein for its ligand is defined as the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;ligand&#x20;concentration&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;necessary&#x20;for&#x20;50&#x25;&#x20;binding"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> to the protein."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>dissociation constant (K<sub>d</sub>)</b></span> of a protein for its ligand is defined as the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>ligand concentration</u> necessary for 50% binding</span> to the protein.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

This is commonly used as a glycerophospholipid headgroup.<div><br></div><div><img src=""298c64d6bae34014481a982712a8bcf9.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Mismatch&#x20;repair&#x20;enzymes&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> fix <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>accidentally</i> <u>misplaced bases</u></span>&nbsp;in DNA by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">removing <i>adjacent</i> bases</span> &amp; replacing them all at once."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mismatch repair enzymes</b></span> fix <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>accidentally</i> <u>misplaced bases</u></span>&nbsp;in DNA by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">removing <i>adjacent</i> bases</span> &amp; replacing them all at once.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Fix&nbsp;accidentally misplaced bases&nbsp;in DNA (i.e. fixing an adenine where there&nbsp;should be&nbsp;a cytosine) by&nbsp;removing&nbsp;all&nbsp;nearby&nbsp;bases and replacing everything at once.</div><div><br></div><img src=""a7e2dd14e2179da3c73979b074db2f73.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mismatch repair enzymes</b></span> fix <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;accidentally&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&lt;u&gt;misplaced&#x20;bases&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>&nbsp;in DNA by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">removing <i>adjacent</i> bases</span> &amp; replacing them all at once."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mismatch repair enzymes</b></span> fix <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>accidentally</i> <u>misplaced bases</u></span>&nbsp;in DNA by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">removing <i>adjacent</i> bases</span> &amp; replacing them all at once.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Fix&nbsp;accidentally misplaced bases&nbsp;in DNA (i.e. fixing an adenine where there&nbsp;should be&nbsp;a cytosine) by&nbsp;removing&nbsp;all&nbsp;nearby&nbsp;bases and replacing everything at once.</div><div><br></div><img src=""a7e2dd14e2179da3c73979b074db2f73.png""></div>"

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mismatch repair enzymes</b></span> fix <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>accidentally</i> <u>misplaced bases</u></span>&nbsp;in DNA by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">removing <i>adjacent</i> bases</span> &amp; replacing them all at once.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Fix&nbsp;accidentally misplaced bases&nbsp;in DNA (i.e. fixing an adenine where there&nbsp;should be&nbsp;a cytosine) by&nbsp;removing&nbsp;all&nbsp;nearby&nbsp;bases and replacing everything at once.</div><div><br></div><img src=""a7e2dd14e2179da3c73979b074db2f73.png""></div>"
"In a prokaryotic <b>operon</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>regulator gene</u></span> <i>encodes </i>for&nbsp;the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">repressor/inducer protein</span> that binds to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""operator&#x20;site"" data-ordinal=""3"">[location]</span> &amp; <i>regulates&nbsp;</i>transcription."	"In a prokaryotic <b>operon</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>regulator gene</u></span> <i>encodes </i>for&nbsp;the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">repressor/inducer protein</span> that binds to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">operator site</span> &amp; <i>regulates&nbsp;</i>transcription.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Remember, prokaryotic DNA can be <b>polycistronic </b>and can form different proteins depending on where along the mRNA transcript translation is began.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""OSC_Microbio_11_07_Operon.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Aminoacyl-tRNA synthetase</b></span> hydrolyzes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ATP</span> into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">AMP</span> to power the attachment of <i>correct</i> amino acids to tRNA during <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""tRNA&#x20;charging"" data-ordinal=""3"">[process]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Aminoacyl-tRNA synthetase</b></span> hydrolyzes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ATP</span> into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">AMP</span> to power the attachment of <i>correct</i> amino acids to tRNA during <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">tRNA charging</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- There's an aminoacyl-tRNA synthetase for each different tRNA molecule (so 20).</div><div><u>Two</u> high energy bonds hydrolyzed from ATP!</div><div>- Requires the hydrolysis of 2 high-energy PO<sub>4</sub><sup>3-</sup> bonds from ATP.</div><div><br></div><img src=""9698ca4e595ac73b43c2d3c64b82637b.png""></div>"
"<div>In the ETC, do these electron carriers <u>transfer their electron(s)</u>&nbsp;<i>simultaneously</i> or one at a time?<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;NADH&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[carrier]</span>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">both at once</span>.</div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;FADH&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[carrier]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">one at a time</span>.</div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Ubiquinone&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[carrier]</span>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">one at a time</span>.</div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Cytochrome&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;&lt;b&gt;C&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[carrier]</span>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">one (only has 1 electron anyway)</span>.</div>"	"<div>In the ETC, do these electron carriers <u>transfer their electron(s)</u>&nbsp;<i>simultaneously</i> or one at a time?<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>NADH</b></span>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">both at once</span>.</div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>FADH<sub>2</sub></b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">one at a time</span>.</div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Ubiquinone</b></span>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">one at a time</span>.</div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Cytochrome</b> <b>C</b></span>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">one (only has 1 electron anyway)</span>.</div>

<div><img src=""chemical-structure-cas-109-97-7.jpg-650.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-f7846e80f5194bb0fa78df3dc6ad24d927b50800.jpg""></div>"
"<b>Hydroxylases</b> are <i>classified</i> as <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""oxidoreductases"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme category]</span>."	"<b>Hydroxylases</b> are <i>classified</i> as <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">oxidoreductases</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Hydroxylases</b>: add hydroxyl (OH) groups to compounds - even directly onto carbon atoms.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e8e1b1b0f9bfff7b3ed5e14f53f42fc6157d6bc4.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hydroxylase</b></span>&nbsp;<b>enzymes</b> catalyze the <i>addition</i> of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;hydroxyl&#x20;&#x28;OH&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> groups to compounds - even <i>directly</i> to carbon atoms."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hydroxylase</b></span>&nbsp;<b>enzymes</b> catalyze the <i>addition</i> of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>hydroxyl (OH)</u></span> groups to compounds - even <i>directly</i> to carbon atoms.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These are considered <u>oxidoreductases</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e8e1b1b0f9bfff7b3ed5e14f53f42fc6157d6bc4.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Hydroxylase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;<b>enzymes</b> catalyze the <i>addition</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>hydroxyl (OH)</u></span> groups to compounds - even <i>directly</i> to carbon atoms."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hydroxylase</b></span>&nbsp;<b>enzymes</b> catalyze the <i>addition</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>hydroxyl (OH)</u></span> groups to compounds - even <i>directly</i> to carbon atoms.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These are considered <u>oxidoreductases</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e8e1b1b0f9bfff7b3ed5e14f53f42fc6157d6bc4.jpg""></div>"
"<b>Hydroxylases</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;can&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[can/cannot]</span> add <u>hydroxyl (OH)</u> groups <i>directly</i> to carbon atoms on compounds."	"<b>Hydroxylases</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>can</i></span> add <u>hydroxyl (OH)</u> groups <i>directly</i> to carbon atoms on compounds.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These are considered <u>oxidoreductases</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e8e1b1b0f9bfff7b3ed5e14f53f42fc6157d6bc4.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>RNA replicase</b></span> <i>catalyzes</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>replication of RNA</u></span> from a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">RNA</span> template - turning <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ssRNA"" data-ordinal=""3"">[ssRNA/dsRNA]</span> into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""dsRNA"" data-ordinal=""3"">[ssRNA/dsRNA]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>RNA replicase</b></span> <i>catalyzes</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>replication of RNA</u></span> from a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">RNA</span> template - turning <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">ssRNA</span> into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">dsRNA</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Turns ssRNA → dsRNA.<br></div><div>- Lacks the ability to proofread.</div></div>"
"<div>Identify the structure of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">dihydroxyacetone</span>.<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;paste&#x2D;9ea6b43557f002c53b013de3e22e9ac1f131418b&#x2E;jpg&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div>Identify the structure of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">dihydroxyacetone</span>.<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-9ea6b43557f002c53b013de3e22e9ac1f131418b.jpg""></span><br></div>

"In complex 4 of the ETC, <b>O<sub>2</sub></b> receives <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""4"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> <u>electrons</u> to form <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2"" data-ordinal=""1"">[1/2]</span> mole(s) of H<sub>2</sub>O."	"In complex 4 of the ETC, <b>O<sub>2</sub></b> receives <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">4</span> <u>electrons</u> to form <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">2</span> mole(s) of H<sub>2</sub>O.

"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">liver</span> uses <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;cytochrome&#x20;P450&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[protein]</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u><i>metabolize</i> drugs</u></span> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">oxidizing</span> them through the addition of hydroxyl groups."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">liver</span> uses <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>cytochrome P450</b></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u><i>metabolize</i> drugs</u></span> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">oxidizing</span> them through the addition of hydroxyl groups.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Cytochrome P450 is a monooxygenase, which is essentially a hydroxylase that adds oxygen atoms to drugs.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2363bb3f63c570ec44550ff0ca3420b912d130fb.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""liver"" data-ordinal=""2"">[organ]</span> uses <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>cytochrome P450</b></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u><i>metabolize</i> drugs</u></span> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">oxidizing</span> them through the addition of hydroxyl groups."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">liver</span> uses <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>cytochrome P450</b></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u><i>metabolize</i> drugs</u></span> by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">oxidizing</span> them through the addition of hydroxyl groups.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Cytochrome P450 is a monooxygenase, which is essentially a hydroxylase that adds oxygen atoms to drugs.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2363bb3f63c570ec44550ff0ca3420b912d130fb.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">liver</span> uses <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>cytochrome P450</b></span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u><i>metabolize</i> drugs</u></span> by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">oxidizing</span> them through the addition of hydroxyl groups.

- Cytochrome P450 is a monooxygenase, which is essentially a hydroxylase that adds oxygen atoms to drugs.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2363bb3f63c570ec44550ff0ca3420b912d130fb.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In drug metabolism, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;cytochrome&#x20;P450&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[protein]</span>&nbsp;acts as a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>oxidizing</u></span> agent." - Primary protein that metabolizes drugs in the liver (through the addition of hydroxyl groups).<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2363bb3f63c570ec44550ff0ca3420b912d130fb.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In drug metabolism, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>cytochrome P450</b></span>&nbsp;acts as a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;oxidizing&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[reducing/oxidizing]</span> agent."	"In drug metabolism, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>cytochrome P450</b></span>&nbsp;acts as a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>oxidizing</u></span> agent.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Primary protein that metabolizes drugs in the liver (through the addition of hydroxyl groups).<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-2363bb3f63c570ec44550ff0ca3420b912d130fb.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The iron atom in the center of <b>heme's</b> ring has a <i>preferred</i> oxidation state of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Fe&lt;sup&gt;3&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[Fe<sup>2+</sup>/Fe<sup>3+</sup>]</span> (<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ferric"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ferrous/ferric]</span> state), which means it <i>primarily</i> acts as a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;oxidizing&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[reducing/oxidizing]</span> agent."	"The iron atom in the center of <b>heme's</b> ring has a <i>preferred</i> oxidation state of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Fe<sup>3+</sup></span> (<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">ferric</span> state), which means it <i>primarily</i> acts as a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>oxidizing</u></span> agent.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Fe<sup>3+</sup> easily reduces to Fe2+, which makes it an oxidizing agent.</div><div>- Remember, ""-ic"" indicates a higher oxidation state (H<sub>3</sub>PO<sub>4</sub> is phosphoric acid due to the hydrogen's higher oxidation state).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-f7846e80f5194bb0fa78df3dc6ad24d927b50800.jpg""></div>"
"Even though proteins that use <b>heme</b> as a cofactor can be <i>both</i> oxidized and reduced, they're <i>primarily</i> considered <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;oxidizing&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[reducing/oxidizing]</span> agents due to the iron atom's preference for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Fe&lt;sup&gt;3&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[Fe<sup>2+</sup>/Fe<sup>3+</sup>]</span> (<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ferric"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> state)."	"Even though proteins that use <b>heme</b> as a cofactor can be <i>both</i> oxidized and reduced, they're <i>primarily</i> considered <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>oxidizing</u></span> agents due to the iron atom's preference for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Fe<sup>3+</sup></span> (<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">ferric</span> state).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Fe<sup>3+</sup> easily reduces to Fe2+, which makes it an oxidizing agent.</div><div>- Remember, ""-ic"" indicates a higher oxidation state (H<sub>3</sub>PO<sub>4</sub> is phosphoric acid due to the hydrogen's higher oxidation state).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-f7846e80f5194bb0fa78df3dc6ad24d927b50800.jpg""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;amber&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[nickname]</span> <b>codon</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>TAG</u></span> in DNA &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>UAG</u></span> in RNA - coding for a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>stop codon</u></span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>amber</b></span> <b>codon</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>TAG</u></span> in DNA &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>UAG</u></span> in RNA - coding for a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>stop codon</u></span>.

"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>amber</b></span> <b>codon</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>TAG</u></span> in DNA &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>UAG</u></span> in RNA - coding for a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>stop codon</u></span>.
