"When an amino acid is <i>phosphorylated</i>, the oxygen atom is the oxygen that was attached to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""amino&#x20;acid&#x27;s&#x20;&lt;i&gt;original&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;OH"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"When an amino acid is <i>phosphorylated</i>, the oxygen atom is the oxygen that was attached to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">amino acid's <i>original</i> OH</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because the amino acid's original OH is the thing that <u>nucleophilically attacks</u> the phosphate.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d45bdcae39701ae1b593f6ffe23e3666a8fc6880.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"When an amino acid is <u>phosphorylated</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""amino&#x20;acid&#x27;s&#x20;O&lt;sup&gt;&#x2D;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&#x28;from&#x20;OH&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[group]</span>&nbsp;<i>nucleophilically attacks</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""phosphate"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"When an amino acid is <u>phosphorylated</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">amino acid's O<sup>-</sup>&nbsp;(from OH)</span>&nbsp;<i>nucleophilically attacks</i> the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">phosphate</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img alt=""Phosphorylation Definition and Examples - Biology Online Dictionary"" src=""Serine-phosphorylation-diagram.png""></div>"
"<div>This is the Lineweaver-Burk plot for a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;mixed&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>inhibitor</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7259beefbecb0e61e52fd7868dae383f23dc0cb9.jpg"">"	"<div>This is the Lineweaver-Burk plot for a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>mixed</b></span> <b>inhibitor</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7259beefbecb0e61e52fd7868dae383f23dc0cb9.jpg"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Mixed</b> because it shows that there was a decrease in V<sub>max</sub> but an increase in K<sub>m</sub>. Mixed inhibitors always decrease V<sub>max</sub>, but can either increase or decrease K<sub>m</sub> (depending on where it binds).</div><b><div><b><br></b></div>V<sub>max</sub></b>: decreased<br><b>K<sub>m</sub></b>: increased or decreased</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Gel&#x20;filtration&#x20;chromatography&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[technique]</span> is a type of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">size-exclusion chromatography</span> that separates <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>proteins</u></span> from each other <i>purely</i> on the basis of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">size</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Gel filtration chromatography</b></span> is a type of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">size-exclusion chromatography</span> that separates <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>proteins</u></span> from each other <i>purely</i> on the basis of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">size</span>.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Gel filtration chromatography</b></span> is a type of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""size&#x2D;exclusion&#x20;chromatography"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> that separates <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;proteins&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> from each other <i>purely</i> on the basis of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""size"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Gel filtration chromatography</b></span> is a type of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">size-exclusion chromatography</span> that separates <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>proteins</u></span> from each other <i>purely</i> on the basis of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">size</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Ternary complexes</b>&nbsp;are complexes where 3 proteins complex together - the enzyme (1) and both substrates (2 &amp; 3) - simultaneously.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-a1c8fc77a504fe25cda8f91720b9819a1196a453.jpg""></div></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Acetyl-CoA can then be used for ketogenesis to make ketone bodies.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Alcohol&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;dehydrogenase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[enzyme]</span>&nbsp;&amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;acetaldehyde&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;b&gt;dehydrogenase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[enzyme]</span>&nbsp;eventually convert <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">ethanol</span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">acetyl-CoA</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Alcohol</b><b style="""">&nbsp;dehydrogenase</b></span>&nbsp;&amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">acetaldehyde&nbsp;</b><b>dehydrogenase</b></span>&nbsp;eventually convert <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">ethanol</span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">acetyl-CoA</span>.

"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>α-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase</b></span> catalyzes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>α-ketoglutarate</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>succinyl-CoA</u></span> (<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""5"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> carbons → <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""4"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> carbons).</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>α-ketoglutarate dehydrogenase</b></span> catalyzes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>α-ketoglutarate</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>succinyl-CoA</u></span> (<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">5</span> carbons → <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">4</span> carbons).</div>

"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Dehydrogenase</b></span> enzymes <i>create </i><u>double bonds</u> by <i>removing </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""hydrogens"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Dehydrogenase</b></span> enzymes <i>create </i><u>double bonds</u> by <i>removing </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">hydrogens</span>.<br></div>

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Oleic acid</b></span> is a(n) <b><u><i>mono</i></u></b><u>unsaturated</u>&nbsp;fatty acid with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""18"" data-ordinal=""3"">[#]</span> carbons." "<div>In <b>isoelectric focusing</b>, the <u>amino acids</u> that make the peptide's <b>pI</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">glutamic acid (E)</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">aspartic acid (D)</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">tyrosine (Y)</span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cysteine (C)</span>.</div>"	"<div>In <b>isoelectric focusing</b>, the <u>amino acids</u> that make the peptide's <b>pI</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">glutamic acid (E)</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">aspartic acid (D)</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">tyrosine (Y)</span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cysteine (C)</span>.</div>
